Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: # -*- coding: utf-8 -*-\\n2: # Copyright: (c) 2020-2021, Ansible Project\\n3: # GNU General Public License v3.0+ (see COPYING or https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt)\\n4: \\\"\\\"\\\"Concrete collection candidate management helper module.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n5: \\n6: from __future__ import annotations\\n7: \\n8: import json\\n9: import os\\n10: import tarfile\\n11: import subprocess\\n12: import typing as t\\n13: import yaml\\n14: \\n15: from contextlib import contextmanager\\n16: from hashlib import sha256\\n17: from urllib.error import URLError\\n18: from urllib.parse import urldefrag\\n19: from shutil import rmtree\\n20: from tempfile import mkdtemp\\n21: \\n22: if t.TYPE_CHECKING:\\n23:     from ansible.galaxy.dependency_resolution.dataclasses import (\\n24:         Candidate, Collection, Requirement,\\n25:     )\\n26:     from ansible.galaxy.token import GalaxyToken\\n27: \\n28: from ansible import context\\n29: from ansible.errors import AnsibleError\\n30: from ansible.galaxy import get_collections_galaxy_meta_info\\n31: from ansible.galaxy.api import should_retry_error\\n32: from ansible.galaxy.dependency_resolution.dataclasses import _GALAXY_YAML\\n33: from ansible.galaxy.user_agent import user_agent\\n34: from ansible.module_utils.common.text.converters import to_bytes, to_native, to_text\\n35: from ansible.module_utils.api import retry_with_delays_and_condition\\n36: from ansible.module_utils.api import generate_jittered_backoff\\n37: from ansible.module_utils.common.process import get_bin_path\\n38: from ansible.module_utils.common.sentinel import Sentinel\\n39: from ansible.module_utils.common.yaml import yaml_load\\n40: from ansible.module_utils.urls import open_url\\n41: from ansible.utils.display import Display\\n42: \\n43: import ansible.constants as C\\n44: \\n45: \\n46: display = Display()\\n47: \\n48: MANIFEST_FILENAME = 'MANIFEST.json'\\n49: \\n50: \\n51: class ConcreteArtifactsManager:\\n52:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Manager for on-disk collection artifacts.\\n53: \\n54:     It is responsible for:\\n55:         * downloading remote collections from Galaxy-compatible servers and\\n56:           direct links to tarballs or SCM repositories\\n57:         * keeping track of local ones\\n58:         * keeping track of Galaxy API tokens for downloads from Galaxy'ish\\n59:           as well as the artifact hashes\\n60:         * keeping track of Galaxy API signatures for downloads from Galaxy'ish\\n61:         * caching all of above\\n62:         * retrieving the metadata out of the downloaded artifacts\\n63:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n64:     def __init__(self, b_working_directory, validate_certs=True, keyring=None, timeout=60, required_signature_count=None, ignore_signature_errors=None):\\n65:         # type: (bytes, bool, str, int, str, list[str]) -> None\\n66:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Initialize ConcreteArtifactsManager caches and constraints.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n67:         self._validate_certs = validate_certs  # type: bool\\n68:         self._artifact_cache = {}  # type: dict[bytes, bytes]\\n69:         self._galaxy_artifact_cache = {}  # type: dict[Candidate | Requirement, bytes]\\n70:         self._artifact_meta_cache = {}  # type: dict[bytes, dict[str, str | list[str] | dict[str, str] | None | t.Type[Sentinel]]]\\n71:         self._galaxy_collection_cache = {}  # type: dict[Candidate | Requirement, tuple[str, str, GalaxyToken]]\\n72:         self._galaxy_collection_origin_cache = {}  # type: dict[Candidate, tuple[str, list[dict[str, str]]]]\\n73:         self._b_working_directory = b_working_directory  # type: bytes\\n74:         self._supplemental_signature_cache = {}  # type: dict[str, str]\\n75:         self._keyring = keyring  # type: str\\n76:         self.timeout = timeout  # type: int\\n77:         self._required_signature_count = required_signature_count  # type: str\\n78:         self._ignore_signature_errors = ignore_signature_errors  # type: list[str]\\n79:         self._require_build_metadata = True  # type: bool\\n80: \\n81:     @property\\n82:     def keyring(self):\\n83:         return self._keyring\\n84: \\n85:     @property\\n86:     def required_successful_signature_count(self):\\n87:         return self._required_signature_count\\n88: \\n89:     @property\\n90:     def ignore_signature_errors(self):\\n91:         if self._ignore_signature_errors is None:\\n92:             return []\\n93:         return self._ignore_signature_errors\\n94: \\n95:     @property\\n96:     def require_build_metadata(self):\\n97:         # type: () -> bool\\n98:         return self._require_build_metadata\\n99: \\n100:     @require_build_metadata.setter\\n101:     def require_build_metadata(self, value):\\n102:         # type: (bool) -> None\\n103:         self._require_build_metadata = value\\n104: \\n105:     def get_galaxy_artifact_source_info(self, collection):\\n106:         # type: (Candidate) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[dict[str, str]]]]\\n107:         server = collection.src.api_server\\n108: \\n109:         try:\\n110:             download_url = self._galaxy_collection_cache[collection][0]\\n111:             signatures_url, signatures = self._galaxy_collection_origin_cache[collection]\\n112:         except KeyError as key_err:\\n113:             raise RuntimeError(\\n114:                 'The is no known source for {coll!s}'.\\n115:                 format(coll=collection),\\n116:             ) from key_err\\n117: \\n118:         return {\\n119:             \\\"format_version\\\": \\\"1.0.0\\\",\\n120:             \\\"namespace\\\": collection.namespace,\\n121:             \\\"name\\\": collection.name,\\n122:             \\\"version\\\": collection.ver,\\n123:             \\\"server\\\": server,\\n124:             \\\"version_url\\\": signatures_url,\\n125:             \\\"download_url\\\": download_url,\\n126:             \\\"signatures\\\": signatures,\\n127:         }\\n128: \\n129:     def get_galaxy_artifact_path(self, collection):\\n130:         # type: (t.Union[Candidate, Requirement]) -> bytes\\n131:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Given a Galaxy-stored collection, return a cached path.\\n132: \\n133:         If it's not yet on disk, this method downloads the artifact first.\\n134:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n135:         try:\\n136:             return self._galaxy_artifact_cache[collection]\\n137:         except KeyError:\\n138:             pass\\n139: \\n140:         try:\\n141:             url, sha256_hash, token = self._galaxy_collection_cache[collection]\\n142:         except KeyError as key_err:\\n143:             raise RuntimeError(\\n144:                 'There is no known source for {coll!s}'.\\n145:                 format(coll=collection),\\n146:             ) from key_err\\n147: \\n148:         display.vvvv(\\n149:             \\\"Fetching a collection tarball for '{collection!s}' from \\\"\\n150:             'Ansible Galaxy'.format(collection=collection),\\n151:         )\\n152: \\n153:         try:\\n154:             b_artifact_path = _download_file(\\n155:                 url,\\n156:                 self._b_working_directory,\\n157:                 expected_hash=sha256_hash,\\n158:                 validate_certs=self._validate_certs,\\n159:                 token=token,\\n160:             )  # type: bytes\\n161:         except URLError as err:\\n162:             raise AnsibleError(\\n163:                 'Failed to download collection tar '\\n164:                 \\\"from '{coll_src!s}': {download_err!s}\\\".\\n165:                 format(\\n166:                     coll_src=to_native(collection.src),\\n167:                     download_err=to_native(err),\\n168:                 ),\\n169:             ) from err\\n170:         except Exception as err:\\n171:             raise AnsibleError(\\n172:                 'Failed to download collection tar '\\n173:                 \\\"from '{coll_src!s}' due to the following unforeseen error: \\\"\\n174:                 '{download_err!s}'.\\n175:                 format(\\n176:                     coll_src=to_native(collection.src),\\n177:                     download_err=to_native(err),\\n178:                 ),\\n179:             ) from err\\n180:         else:\\n181:             display.vvv(\\n182:                 \\\"Collection '{coll!s}' obtained from \\\"\\n183:                 'server {server!s} {url!s}'.format(\\n184:                     coll=collection, server=collection.src or 'Galaxy',\\n185:                     url=collection.src.api_server if collection.src is not None\\n186:                     else '',\\n187:                 )\\n188:             )\\n189: \\n190:         self._galaxy_artifact_cache[collection] = b_artifact_path\\n191:         return b_artifact_path\\n192: \\n193:     def get_artifact_path(self, collection):\\n194:         # type: (Collection) -> bytes\\n195:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Given a concrete collection pointer, return a cached path.\\n196: \\n197:         If it's not yet on disk, this method downloads the artifact first.\\n198:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n199:         try:\\n200:             return self._artifact_cache[collection.src]\\n201:         except KeyError:\\n202:             pass\\n203: \\n204:         # NOTE: SCM needs to be special-cased as it may contain either\\n205:         # NOTE: one collection in its root, or a number of top-level\\n206:         # NOTE: collection directories instead.\\n207:         # NOTE: The idea is to store the SCM collection as unpacked\\n208:         # NOTE: directory structure under the temporary location and use\\n209:         # NOTE: a \\\"virtual\\\" collection that has pinned requirements on\\n210:         # NOTE: the directories under that SCM checkout that correspond\\n211:         # NOTE: to collections.\\n212:         # NOTE: This brings us to the idea that we need two separate\\n213:         # NOTE: virtual Requirement/Candidate types --\\n214:         # NOTE: (single) dir + (multidir) subdirs\\n215:         if collection.is_url:\\n216:             display.vvvv(\\n217:                 \\\"Collection requirement '{collection!s}' is a URL \\\"\\n218:                 'to a tar artifact'.format(collection=collection.fqcn),\\n219:             )\\n220:             try:\\n221:                 b_artifact_path = _download_file(\\n222:                     collection.src,\\n223:                     self._b_working_directory,\\n224:                     expected_hash=None,  # NOTE: URLs don't support checksums\\n225:                     validate_certs=self._validate_certs,\\n226:                     timeout=self.timeout\\n227:                 )\\n228:             except Exception as err:\\n229:                 raise AnsibleError(\\n230:                     'Failed to download collection tar '\\n231:                     \\\"from '{coll_src!s}': {download_err!s}\\\".\\n232:                     format(\\n233:                         coll_src=to_native(collection.src),\\n234:                         download_err=to_native(err),\\n235:                     ),\\n236:                 ) from err\\n237:         elif collection.is_scm:\\n238:             b_artifact_path = _extract_collection_from_git(\\n239:                 collection.src,\\n240:                 collection.ver,\\n241:                 self._b_working_directory,\\n242:             )\\n243:         elif collection.is_file or collection.is_dir or collection.is_subdirs:\\n244:             b_artifact_path = to_bytes(collection.src)\\n245:         else:\\n246:             # NOTE: This may happen `if collection.is_online_index_pointer`\\n247:             raise RuntimeError(\\n248:                 'The artifact is of an unexpected type {art_type!s}'.\\n249:                 format(art_type=collection.type)\\n250:             )\\n251: \\n252:         self._artifact_cache[collection.src] = b_artifact_path\\n253:         return b_artifact_path\\n254: \\n255:     def get_artifact_path_from_unknown(self, collection):\\n256:         # type: (Candidate) -> bytes\\n257:         if collection.is_concrete_artifact:\\n258:             return self.get_artifact_path(collection)\\n259:         return self.get_galaxy_artifact_path(collection)\\n260: \\n261:     def _get_direct_collection_namespace(self, collection):\\n262:         # type: (Candidate) -> t.Optional[str]\\n263:         return self.get_direct_collection_meta(collection)['namespace']  # type: ignore[return-value]\\n264: \\n265:     def _get_direct_collection_name(self, collection):\\n266:         # type: (Collection) -> t.Optional[str]\\n267:         return self.get_direct_collection_meta(collection)['name']  # type: ignore[return-value]\\n268: \\n269:     def get_direct_collection_fqcn(self, collection):\\n270:         # type: (Collection) -> t.Optional[str]\\n271:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Extract FQCN from the given on-disk collection artifact.\\n272: \\n273:         If the collection is virtual, ``None`` is returned instead\\n274:         of a string.\\n275:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n276:         if collection.is_virtual:\\n277:             # NOTE: should it be something like \\\"<virtual>\\\"?\\n278:             return None\\n279: \\n280:         return '.'.join((  # type: ignore[type-var]\\n281:             self._get_direct_collection_namespace(collection),  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n282:             self._get_direct_collection_name(collection),\\n283:         ))\\n284: \\n285:     def get_direct_collection_version(self, collection):\\n286:         # type: (Collection) -> str\\n287:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Extract version from the given on-disk collection artifact.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n288:         return self.get_direct_collection_meta(collection)['version']  # type: ignore[return-value]\\n289: \\n290:     def get_direct_collection_dependencies(self, collection):\\n291:         # type: (t.Union[Candidate, Requirement]) -> dict[str, str]\\n292:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Extract deps from the given on-disk collection artifact.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n293:         collection_dependencies = self.get_direct_collection_meta(collection)['dependencies']\\n294:         if collection_dependencies is None:\\n295:             collection_dependencies = {}\\n296:         return collection_dependencies  # type: ignore[return-value]\\n297: \\n298:     def get_direct_collection_meta(self, collection):\\n299:         # type: (Collection) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, dict[str, str], list[str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\\n300:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Extract meta from the given on-disk collection artifact.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n301:         try:  # FIXME: use unique collection identifier as a cache key?\\n302:             return self._artifact_meta_cache[collection.src]\\n303:         except KeyError:\\n304:             b_artifact_path = self.get_artifact_path(collection)\\n305: \\n306:         if collection.is_url or collection.is_file:\\n307:             collection_meta = _get_meta_from_tar(b_artifact_path)\\n308:         elif collection.is_dir:  # should we just build a coll instead?\\n309:             # FIXME: what if there's subdirs?\\n310:             try:\\n311:                 collection_meta = _get_meta_from_dir(b_artifact_path, self.require_build_metadata)\\n312:             except LookupError as lookup_err:\\n313:                 raise AnsibleError(\\n314:                     'Failed to find the collection dir deps: {err!s}'.\\n315:                     format(err=to_native(lookup_err)),\\n316:                 ) from lookup_err\\n317:         elif collection.is_scm:\\n318:             collection_meta = {\\n319:                 'name': None,\\n320:                 'namespace': None,\\n321:                 'dependencies': {to_native(b_artifact_path): '*'},\\n322:                 'version': '*',\\n323:             }\\n324:         elif collection.is_subdirs:\\n325:             collection_meta = {\\n326:                 'name': None,\\n327:                 'namespace': None,\\n328:                 # NOTE: Dropping b_artifact_path since it's based on src anyway\\n329:                 'dependencies': dict.fromkeys(\\n330:                     map(to_native, collection.namespace_collection_paths),\\n331:                     '*',\\n332:                 ),\\n333:                 'version': '*',\\n334:             }\\n335:         else:\\n336:             raise RuntimeError\\n337: \\n338:         self._artifact_meta_cache[collection.src] = collection_meta\\n339:         return collection_meta\\n340: \\n341:     def save_collection_source(self, collection, url, sha256_hash, token, signatures_url, signatures):\\n342:         # type: (Candidate, str, str, GalaxyToken, str, list[dict[str, str]]) -> None\\n343:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Store collection URL, SHA256 hash and Galaxy API token.\\n344: \\n345:         This is a hook that is supposed to be called before attempting to\\n346:         download Galaxy-based collections with ``get_galaxy_artifact_path()``.\\n347:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n348:         self._galaxy_collection_cache[collection] = url, sha256_hash, token\\n349:         self._galaxy_collection_origin_cache[collection] = signatures_url, signatures\\n350: \\n351:     @classmethod\\n352:     @contextmanager\\n353:     def under_tmpdir(\\n354:             cls,\\n355:             temp_dir_base,  # type: str\\n356:             validate_certs=True,  # type: bool\\n357:             keyring=None,  # type: str\\n358:             required_signature_count=None,  # type: str\\n359:             ignore_signature_errors=None,  # type: list[str]\\n360:             require_build_metadata=True,  # type: bool\\n361:     ):  # type: (...) -> t.Iterator[ConcreteArtifactsManager]\\n362:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Custom ConcreteArtifactsManager constructor with temp dir.\\n363: \\n364:         This method returns a context manager that allocates and cleans\\n365:         up a temporary directory for caching the collection artifacts\\n366:         during the dependency resolution process.\\n367:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n368:         # NOTE: Can't use `with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory:`\\n369:         # NOTE: because it's not in Python 2 stdlib.\\n370:         temp_path = mkdtemp(\\n371:             dir=to_bytes(temp_dir_base, errors='surrogate_or_strict'),\\n372:         )\\n373:         b_temp_path = to_bytes(temp_path, errors='surrogate_or_strict')\\n374:         try:\\n375:             yield cls(\\n376:                 b_temp_path,\\n377:                 validate_certs,\\n378:                 keyring=keyring,\\n379:                 required_signature_count=required_signature_count,\\n380:                 ignore_signature_errors=ignore_signature_errors\\n381:             )\\n382:         finally:\\n383:             rmtree(b_temp_path)\\n384: \\n385: \\n386: def parse_scm(collection, version):\\n387:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Extract name, version, path and subdir out of the SCM pointer.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n388:     if ',' in collection:\\n389:         collection, version = collection.split(',', 1)\\n390:     elif version == '*' or not version:\\n391:         version = 'HEAD'\\n392: \\n393:     if collection.startswith('git+'):\\n394:         path = collection[4:]\\n395:     else:\\n396:         path = collection\\n397: \\n398:     path, fragment = urldefrag(path)\\n399:     fragment = fragment.strip(os.path.sep)\\n400: \\n401:     if path.endswith(os.path.sep + '.git'):\\n402:         name = path.split(os.path.sep)[-2]\\n403:     elif '://' not in path and '@' not in path:\\n404:         name = path\\n405:     else:\\n406:         name = path.split('/')[-1]\\n407:         if name.endswith('.git'):\\n408:             name = name[:-4]\\n409: \\n410:     return name, version, path, fragment\\n411: \\n412: \\n413: def _extract_collection_from_git(repo_url, coll_ver, b_path):\\n414:     name, version, git_url, fragment = parse_scm(repo_url, coll_ver)\\n415:     b_checkout_path = mkdtemp(\\n416:         dir=b_path,\\n417:         prefix=to_bytes(name, errors='surrogate_or_strict'),\\n418:     )\\n419: \\n420:     try:\\n421:         git_executable = get_bin_path('git')\\n422:     except ValueError as err:\\n423:         raise AnsibleError(\\n424:             \\\"Could not find git executable to extract the collection from the Git repository `{repo_url!s}`.\\\".\\n425:             format(repo_url=to_native(git_url))\\n426:         ) from err\\n427: \\n428:     # Perform a shallow clone if simply cloning HEAD\\n429:     if version == 'HEAD':\\n430:         git_clone_cmd = [git_executable, 'clone', '--depth=1', git_url, to_text(b_checkout_path)]\\n431:     else:\\n432:         git_clone_cmd = [git_executable, 'clone', git_url, to_text(b_checkout_path)]\\n433:     # FIXME: '--branch', version\\n434: \\n435:     if context.CLIARGS['ignore_certs'] or C.GALAXY_IGNORE_CERTS:\\n436:         git_clone_cmd.extend(['-c', 'http.sslVerify=false'])\\n437: \\n438:     try:\\n439:         subprocess.check_call(git_clone_cmd)\\n440:     except subprocess.CalledProcessError as proc_err:\\n441:         raise AnsibleError(  # should probably be LookupError\\n442:             'Failed to clone a Git repository from `{repo_url!s}`.'.\\n443:             format(repo_url=to_native(git_url)),\\n444:         ) from proc_err\\n445: \\n446:     if version == 'HEAD':\\n447:         git_args = ()\\n448:     else:\\n449:         git_args = '-c', 'advice.detachedHead=false'\\n450: \\n451:     git_switch_cmd = git_executable, *git_args, 'checkout', to_text(version)\\n452:     try:\\n453:         subprocess.check_call(git_switch_cmd, cwd=b_checkout_path)\\n454:     except subprocess.CalledProcessError as proc_err:\\n455:         raise AnsibleError(  # should probably be LookupError\\n456:             'Failed to switch a cloned Git repo `{repo_url!s}` '\\n457:             'to the requested revision `{revision!s}`.'.\\n458:             format(\\n459:                 revision=to_native(version),\\n460:                 repo_url=to_native(git_url),\\n461:             ),\\n462:         ) from proc_err\\n463: \\n464:     return (\\n465:         os.path.join(b_checkout_path, to_bytes(fragment))\\n466:         if fragment else b_checkout_path\\n467:     )\\n468: \\n469: \\n470: # FIXME: use random subdirs while preserving the file names\\n471: @retry_with_delays_and_condition(\\n472:     backoff_iterator=generate_jittered_backoff(retries=6, delay_base=2, delay_threshold=40),\\n473:     should_retry_error=should_retry_error\\n474: )\\n475: def _download_file(url, b_path, expected_hash, validate_certs, token=None, timeout=60):\\n476:     # type: (str, bytes, t.Optional[str], bool, GalaxyToken, int) -> bytes\\n477:     # ^ NOTE: used in download and verify_collections ^\\n478:     b_tarball_name = to_bytes(\\n479:         url.rsplit('/', 1)[1], errors='surrogate_or_strict',\\n480:     )\\n481:     b_file_name = b_tarball_name[:-len('.tar.gz')]\\n482: \\n483:     b_tarball_dir = mkdtemp(\\n484:         dir=b_path,\\n485:         prefix=b'-'.join((b_file_name, b'')),\\n486:     )  # type: bytes\\n487: \\n488:     b_file_path = os.path.join(b_tarball_dir, b_tarball_name)\\n489: \\n490:     display.display(\\\"Downloading %s to %s\\\" % (url, to_text(b_tarball_dir)))\\n491:     # NOTE: Galaxy redirects downloads to S3 which rejects the request\\n492:     # NOTE: if an Authorization header is attached so don't redirect it\\n493:     resp = open_url(\\n494:         to_native(url, errors='surrogate_or_strict'),\\n495:         validate_certs=validate_certs,\\n496:         headers=None if token is None else token.headers(),\\n497:         unredirected_headers=['Authorization'], http_agent=user_agent(),\\n498:         timeout=timeout\\n499:     )\\n500: \\n501:     with open(b_file_path, 'wb') as download_file:  # type: t.BinaryIO\\n502:         actual_hash = _consume_file(resp, write_to=download_file)\\n503: \\n504:     if expected_hash:\\n505:         display.vvvv(\\n506:             'Validating downloaded file hash {actual_hash!s} with '\\n507:             'expected hash {expected_hash!s}'.\\n508:             format(actual_hash=actual_hash, expected_hash=expected_hash)\\n509:         )\\n510:         if expected_hash != actual_hash:\\n511:             raise AnsibleError('Mismatch artifact hash with downloaded file')\\n512: \\n513:     return b_file_path\\n514: \\n515: \\n516: def _consume_file(read_from, write_to=None):\\n517:     # type: (t.BinaryIO, t.BinaryIO) -> str\\n518:     bufsize = 65536\\n519:     sha256_digest = sha256()\\n520:     data = read_from.read(bufsize)\\n521:     while data:\\n522:         if write_to is not None:\\n523:             write_to.write(data)\\n524:             write_to.flush()\\n525:         sha256_digest.update(data)\\n526:         data = read_from.read(bufsize)\\n527: \\n528:     return sha256_digest.hexdigest()\\n529: \\n530: \\n531: def _normalize_galaxy_yml_manifest(\\n532:         galaxy_yml,  # type: dict[str, t.Union[str, list[str], dict[str, str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\\n533:         b_galaxy_yml_path,  # type: bytes\\n534:         require_build_metadata=True,  # type: bool\\n535: ):\\n536:     # type: (...) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[str], dict[str, str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\\n537:     galaxy_yml_schema = (\\n538:         get_collections_galaxy_meta_info()\\n539:     )  # type: list[dict[str, t.Any]]  # FIXME: <--\\n540:     # FIXME: \ud83d\udc46maybe precise type: list[dict[str, t.Union[bool, str, list[str]]]]\\n541: \\n542:     mandatory_keys = set()\\n543:     string_keys = set()  # type: set[str]\\n544:     list_keys = set()  # type: set[str]\\n545:     dict_keys = set()  # type: set[str]\\n546:     sentinel_keys = set()  # type: set[str]\\n547: \\n548:     for info in galaxy_yml_schema:\\n549:         if info.get('required', False):\\n550:             mandatory_keys.add(info['key'])\\n551: \\n552:         key_list_type = {\\n553:             'str': string_keys,\\n554:             'list': list_keys,\\n555:             'dict': dict_keys,\\n556:             'sentinel': sentinel_keys,\\n557:         }[info.get('type', 'str')]\\n558:         key_list_type.add(info['key'])\\n559: \\n560:     all_keys = frozenset(mandatory_keys | string_keys | list_keys | dict_keys | sentinel_keys)\\n561: \\n562:     set_keys = set(galaxy_yml.keys())\\n563:     missing_keys = mandatory_keys.difference(set_keys)\\n564:     if missing_keys:\\n565:         msg = (\\n566:             \\\"The collection galaxy.yml at '%s' is missing the following mandatory keys: %s\\\"\\n567:             % (to_native(b_galaxy_yml_path), \\\", \\\".join(sorted(missing_keys)))\\n568:         )\\n569:         if require_build_metadata:\\n570:             raise AnsibleError(msg)\\n571:         display.warning(msg)\\n572:         raise ValueError(msg)\\n573: \\n574:     extra_keys = set_keys.difference(all_keys)\\n575:     if len(extra_keys) > 0:\\n576:         display.warning(\\\"Found unknown keys in collection galaxy.yml at '%s': %s\\\"\\n577:                         % (to_text(b_galaxy_yml_path), \\\", \\\".join(extra_keys)))\\n578: \\n579:     # Add the defaults if they have not been set\\n580:     for optional_string in string_keys:\\n581:         if optional_string not in galaxy_yml:\\n582:             galaxy_yml[optional_string] = None\\n583: \\n584:     for optional_list in list_keys:\\n585:         list_val = galaxy_yml.get(optional_list, None)\\n586: \\n587:         if list_val is None:\\n588:             galaxy_yml[optional_list] = []\\n589:         elif not isinstance(list_val, list):\\n590:             galaxy_yml[optional_list] = [list_val]  # type: ignore[list-item]\\n591: \\n592:     for optional_dict in dict_keys:\\n593:         if optional_dict not in galaxy_yml:\\n594:             galaxy_yml[optional_dict] = {}\\n595: \\n596:     for optional_sentinel in sentinel_keys:\\n597:         if optional_sentinel not in galaxy_yml:\\n598:             galaxy_yml[optional_sentinel] = Sentinel\\n599: \\n600:     # NOTE: `version: null` is only allowed for `galaxy.yml`\\n601:     # NOTE: and not `MANIFEST.json`. The use-case for it is collections\\n602:     # NOTE: that generate the version from Git before building a\\n603:     # NOTE: distributable tarball artifact.\\n604:     if not galaxy_yml.get('version'):\\n605:         galaxy_yml['version'] = '*'\\n606: \\n607:     return galaxy_yml\\n608: \\n609: \\n610: def _get_meta_from_dir(\\n611:         b_path,  # type: bytes\\n612:         require_build_metadata=True,  # type: bool\\n613: ):  # type: (...) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[str], dict[str, str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\\n614:     try:\\n615:         return _get_meta_from_installed_dir(b_path)\\n616:     except LookupError:\\n617:         return _get_meta_from_src_dir(b_path, require_build_metadata)\\n618: \\n619: \\n620: def _get_meta_from_src_dir(\\n621:         b_path,  # type: bytes\\n622:         require_build_metadata=True,  # type: bool\\n623: ):  # type: (...) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[str], dict[str, str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\\n624:     galaxy_yml = os.path.join(b_path, _GALAXY_YAML)\\n625:     if not os.path.isfile(galaxy_yml):\\n626:         raise LookupError(\\n627:             \\\"The collection galaxy.yml path '{path!s}' does not exist.\\\".\\n628:             format(path=to_native(galaxy_yml))\\n629:         )\\n630: \\n631:     with open(galaxy_yml, 'rb') as manifest_file_obj:\\n632:         try:\\n633:             manifest = yaml_load(manifest_file_obj)\\n634:         except yaml.error.YAMLError as yaml_err:\\n635:             raise AnsibleError(\\n636:                 \\\"Failed to parse the galaxy.yml at '{path!s}' with \\\"\\n637:                 'the following error:\\\\n{err_txt!s}'.\\n638:                 format(\\n639:                     path=to_native(galaxy_yml),\\n640:                     err_txt=to_native(yaml_err),\\n641:                 ),\\n642:             ) from yaml_err\\n643: \\n644:     if not isinstance(manifest, dict):\\n645:         if require_build_metadata:\\n646:             raise AnsibleError(f\\\"The collection galaxy.yml at '{to_native(galaxy_yml)}' is incorrectly formatted.\\\")\\n647:         # Valid build metadata is not required by ansible-galaxy list. Raise ValueError to fall back to implicit metadata.\\n648:         display.warning(f\\\"The collection galaxy.yml at '{to_native(galaxy_yml)}' is incorrectly formatted.\\\")\\n649:         raise ValueError(f\\\"The collection galaxy.yml at '{to_native(galaxy_yml)}' is incorrectly formatted.\\\")\\n650: \\n651:     return _normalize_galaxy_yml_manifest(manifest, galaxy_yml, require_build_metadata)\\n652: \\n653: \\n654: def _get_json_from_installed_dir(\\n655:         b_path,  # type: bytes\\n656:         filename,  # type: str\\n657: ):  # type: (...) -> dict\\n658: \\n659:     b_json_filepath = os.path.join(b_path, to_bytes(filename, errors='surrogate_or_strict'))\\n660: \\n661:     try:\\n662:         with open(b_json_filepath, 'rb') as manifest_fd:\\n663:             b_json_text = manifest_fd.read()\\n664:     except OSError as ex:\\n665:         raise LookupError(f\\\"The collection {filename!r} path {to_text(b_json_filepath)!r} does not exist.\\\") from ex\\n666: \\n667:     manifest_txt = to_text(b_json_text, errors='surrogate_or_strict')\\n668: \\n669:     try:\\n670:         manifest = json.loads(manifest_txt)\\n671:     except ValueError:\\n672:         raise AnsibleError(\\n673:             'Collection tar file member {member!s} does not '\\n674:             'contain a valid json string.'.\\n675:             format(member=filename),\\n676:         )\\n677: \\n678:     return manifest\\n679: \\n680: \\n681: def _get_meta_from_installed_dir(\\n682:         b_path,  # type: bytes\\n683: ):  # type: (...) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[str], dict[str, str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\\n684:     manifest = _get_json_from_installed_dir(b_path, MANIFEST_FILENAME)\\n685:     collection_info = manifest['collection_info']\\n686: \\n687:     version = collection_info.get('version')\\n688:     if not version:\\n689:         raise AnsibleError(\\n690:             u'Collection metadata file `{manifest_filename!s}` at `{meta_file!s}` is expected '\\n691:             u'to have a valid SemVer version value but got {version!s}'.\\n692:             format(\\n693:                 manifest_filename=MANIFEST_FILENAME,\\n694:                 meta_file=to_text(b_path),\\n695:                 version=to_text(repr(version)),\\n696:             ),\\n697:         )\\n698: \\n699:     return collection_info\\n700: \\n701: \\n702: def _get_meta_from_tar(\\n703:         b_path,  # type: bytes\\n704: ):  # type: (...) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[str], dict[str, str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\\n705:     if not os.path.exists(b_path):\\n706:         raise AnsibleError(\\n707:             f\\\"Unable to find collection artifact file at '{to_native(b_path)}'.\\\"\\n708:         )\\n709: \\n710:     if not tarfile.is_tarfile(b_path):\\n711:         raise AnsibleError(\\n712:             \\\"Collection artifact at '{path!s}' is not a valid tar file.\\\".\\n713:             format(path=to_native(b_path)),\\n714:         )\\n715: \\n716:     with tarfile.open(b_path, mode='r') as collection_tar:  # type: tarfile.TarFile\\n717:         try:\\n718:             member = collection_tar.getmember(MANIFEST_FILENAME)\\n719:         except KeyError:\\n720:             raise AnsibleError(\\n721:                 \\\"Collection at '{path!s}' does not contain the \\\"\\n722:                 'required file {manifest_file!s}.'.\\n723:                 format(\\n724:                     path=to_native(b_path),\\n725:                     manifest_file=MANIFEST_FILENAME,\\n726:                 ),\\n727:             )\\n728: \\n729:         with _tarfile_extract(collection_tar, member) as (_member, member_obj):\\n730:             if member_obj is None:\\n731:                 raise AnsibleError(\\n732:                     'Collection tar file does not contain '\\n733:                     'member {member!s}'.format(member=MANIFEST_FILENAME),\\n734:                 )\\n735: \\n736:             text_content = to_text(\\n737:                 member_obj.read(),\\n738:                 errors='surrogate_or_strict',\\n739:             )\\n740: \\n741:             try:\\n742:                 manifest = json.loads(text_content)\\n743:             except ValueError:\\n744:                 raise AnsibleError(\\n745:                     'Collection tar file member {member!s} does not '\\n746:                     'contain a valid json string.'.\\n747:                     format(member=MANIFEST_FILENAME),\\n748:                 )\\n749:             return manifest['collection_info']\\n750: \\n751: \\n752: @contextmanager\\n753: def _tarfile_extract(\\n754:         tar,  # type: tarfile.TarFile\\n755:         member,  # type: tarfile.TarInfo\\n756: ):\\n757:     # type: (...) -> t.Iterator[tuple[tarfile.TarInfo, t.Optional[t.IO[bytes]]]]\\n758:     tar_obj = tar.extractfile(member)\\n759:     try:\\n760:         yield member, tar_obj\\n761:     finally:\\n762:         if tar_obj is not None:\\n763:             tar_obj.close()\\n764: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible@52b7d4d092359e776da0b79db9752bfc6ca91969/-/blob/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/concrete_artifact_manager.py\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: # -*- coding: utf-8 -*-\n2: # Copyright: (c) 2020-2021, Ansible Project\n3: # GNU General Public License v3.0+ (see COPYING or https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt)\n4: \"\"\"Concrete collection candidate management helper module.\"\"\"\n5: \n6: from __future__ import annotations\n7: \n8: import json\n9: import os\n10: import tarfile\n11: import subprocess\n12: import typing as t\n13: import yaml\n14: \n15: from contextlib import contextmanager\n16: from hashlib import sha256\n17: from urllib.error import URLError\n18: from urllib.parse import urldefrag\n19: from shutil import rmtree\n20: from tempfile import mkdtemp\n21: \n22: if t.TYPE_CHECKING:\n23:     from ansible.galaxy.dependency_resolution.dataclasses import (\n24:         Candidate, Collection, Requirement,\n25:     )\n26:     from ansible.galaxy.token import GalaxyToken\n27: \n28: from ansible import context\n29: from ansible.errors import AnsibleError\n30: from ansible.galaxy import get_collections_galaxy_meta_info\n31: from ansible.galaxy.api import should_retry_error\n32: from ansible.galaxy.dependency_resolution.dataclasses import _GALAXY_YAML\n33: from ansible.galaxy.user_agent import user_agent\n34: from ansible.module_utils.common.text.converters import to_bytes, to_native, to_text\n35: from ansible.module_utils.api import retry_with_delays_and_condition\n36: from ansible.module_utils.api import generate_jittered_backoff\n37: from ansible.module_utils.common.process import get_bin_path\n38: from ansible.module_utils.common.sentinel import Sentinel\n39: from ansible.module_utils.common.yaml import yaml_load\n40: from ansible.module_utils.urls import open_url\n41: from ansible.utils.display import Display\n42: \n43: import ansible.constants as C\n44: \n45: \n46: display = Display()\n47: \n48: MANIFEST_FILENAME = 'MANIFEST.json'\n49: \n50: \n51: class ConcreteArtifactsManager:\n52:     \"\"\"Manager for on-disk collection artifacts.\n53: \n54:     It is responsible for:\n55:         * downloading remote collections from Galaxy-compatible servers and\n56:           direct links to tarballs or SCM repositories\n57:         * keeping track of local ones\n58:         * keeping track of Galaxy API tokens for downloads from Galaxy'ish\n59:           as well as the artifact hashes\n60:         * keeping track of Galaxy API signatures for downloads from Galaxy'ish\n61:         * caching all of above\n62:         * retrieving the metadata out of the downloaded artifacts\n63:     \"\"\"\n64:     def __init__(self, b_working_directory, validate_certs=True, keyring=None, timeout=60, required_signature_count=None, ignore_signature_errors=None):\n65:         # type: (bytes, bool, str, int, str, list[str]) -> None\n66:         \"\"\"Initialize ConcreteArtifactsManager caches and constraints.\"\"\"\n67:         self._validate_certs = validate_certs  # type: bool\n68:         self._artifact_cache = {}  # type: dict[bytes, bytes]\n69:         self._galaxy_artifact_cache = {}  # type: dict[Candidate | Requirement, bytes]\n70:         self._artifact_meta_cache = {}  # type: dict[bytes, dict[str, str | list[str] | dict[str, str] | None | t.Type[Sentinel]]]\n71:         self._galaxy_collection_cache = {}  # type: dict[Candidate | Requirement, tuple[str, str, GalaxyToken]]\n72:         self._galaxy_collection_origin_cache = {}  # type: dict[Candidate, tuple[str, list[dict[str, str]]]]\n73:         self._b_working_directory = b_working_directory  # type: bytes\n74:         self._supplemental_signature_cache = {}  # type: dict[str, str]\n75:         self._keyring = keyring  # type: str\n76:         self.timeout = timeout  # type: int\n77:         self._required_signature_count = required_signature_count  # type: str\n78:         self._ignore_signature_errors = ignore_signature_errors  # type: list[str]\n79:         self._require_build_metadata = True  # type: bool\n80: \n81:     @property\n82:     def keyring(self):\n83:         return self._keyring\n84: \n85:     @property\n86:     def required_successful_signature_count(self):\n87:         return self._required_signature_count\n88: \n89:     @property\n90:     def ignore_signature_errors(self):\n91:         if self._ignore_signature_errors is None:\n92:             return []\n93:         return self._ignore_signature_errors\n94: \n95:     @property\n96:     def require_build_metadata(self):\n97:         # type: () -> bool\n98:         return self._require_build_metadata\n99: \n100:     @require_build_metadata.setter\n101:     def require_build_metadata(self, value):\n102:         # type: (bool) -> None\n103:         self._require_build_metadata = value\n104: \n105:     def get_galaxy_artifact_source_info(self, collection):\n106:         # type: (Candidate) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[dict[str, str]]]]\n107:         server = collection.src.api_server\n108: \n109:         try:\n110:             download_url = self._galaxy_collection_cache[collection][0]\n111:             signatures_url, signatures = self._galaxy_collection_origin_cache[collection]\n112:         except KeyError as key_err:\n113:             raise RuntimeError(\n114:                 'The is no known source for {coll!s}'.\n115:                 format(coll=collection),\n116:             ) from key_err\n117: \n118:         return {\n119:             \"format_version\": \"1.0.0\",\n120:             \"namespace\": collection.namespace,\n121:             \"name\": collection.name,\n122:             \"version\": collection.ver,\n123:             \"server\": server,\n124:             \"version_url\": signatures_url,\n125:             \"download_url\": download_url,\n126:             \"signatures\": signatures,\n127:         }\n128: \n129:     def get_galaxy_artifact_path(self, collection):\n130:         # type: (t.Union[Candidate, Requirement]) -> bytes\n131:         \"\"\"Given a Galaxy-stored collection, return a cached path.\n132: \n133:         If it's not yet on disk, this method downloads the artifact first.\n134:         \"\"\"\n135:         try:\n136:             return self._galaxy_artifact_cache[collection]\n137:         except KeyError:\n138:             pass\n139: \n140:         try:\n141:             url, sha256_hash, token = self._galaxy_collection_cache[collection]\n142:         except KeyError as key_err:\n143:             raise RuntimeError(\n144:                 'There is no known source for {coll!s}'.\n145:                 format(coll=collection),\n146:             ) from key_err\n147: \n148:         display.vvvv(\n149:             \"Fetching a collection tarball for '{collection!s}' from \"\n150:             'Ansible Galaxy'.format(collection=collection),\n151:         )\n152: \n153:         try:\n154:             b_artifact_path = _download_file(\n155:                 url,\n156:                 self._b_working_directory,\n157:                 expected_hash=sha256_hash,\n158:                 validate_certs=self._validate_certs,\n159:                 token=token,\n160:             )  # type: bytes\n161:         except URLError as err:\n162:             raise AnsibleError(\n163:                 'Failed to download collection tar '\n164:                 \"from '{coll_src!s}': {download_err!s}\".\n165:                 format(\n166:                     coll_src=to_native(collection.src),\n167:                     download_err=to_native(err),\n168:                 ),\n169:             ) from err\n170:         except Exception as err:\n171:             raise AnsibleError(\n172:                 'Failed to download collection tar '\n173:                 \"from '{coll_src!s}' due to the following unforeseen error: \"\n174:                 '{download_err!s}'.\n175:                 format(\n176:                     coll_src=to_native(collection.src),\n177:                     download_err=to_native(err),\n178:                 ),\n179:             ) from err\n180:         else:\n181:             display.vvv(\n182:                 \"Collection '{coll!s}' obtained from \"\n183:                 'server {server!s} {url!s}'.format(\n184:                     coll=collection, server=collection.src or 'Galaxy',\n185:                     url=collection.src.api_server if collection.src is not None\n186:                     else '',\n187:                 )\n188:             )\n189: \n190:         self._galaxy_artifact_cache[collection] = b_artifact_path\n191:         return b_artifact_path\n192: \n193:     def get_artifact_path(self, collection):\n194:         # type: (Collection) -> bytes\n195:         \"\"\"Given a concrete collection pointer, return a cached path.\n196: \n197:         If it's not yet on disk, this method downloads the artifact first.\n198:         \"\"\"\n199:         try:\n200:             return self._artifact_cache[collection.src]\n201:         except KeyError:\n202:             pass\n203: \n204:         # NOTE: SCM needs to be special-cased as it may contain either\n205:         # NOTE: one collection in its root, or a number of top-level\n206:         # NOTE: collection directories instead.\n207:         # NOTE: The idea is to store the SCM collection as unpacked\n208:         # NOTE: directory structure under the temporary location and use\n209:         # NOTE: a \"virtual\" collection that has pinned requirements on\n210:         # NOTE: the directories under that SCM checkout that correspond\n211:         # NOTE: to collections.\n212:         # NOTE: This brings us to the idea that we need two separate\n213:         # NOTE: virtual Requirement/Candidate types --\n214:         # NOTE: (single) dir + (multidir) subdirs\n215:         if collection.is_url:\n216:             display.vvvv(\n217:                 \"Collection requirement '{collection!s}' is a URL \"\n218:                 'to a tar artifact'.format(collection=collection.fqcn),\n219:             )\n220:             try:\n221:                 b_artifact_path = _download_file(\n222:                     collection.src,\n223:                     self._b_working_directory,\n224:                     expected_hash=None,  # NOTE: URLs don't support checksums\n225:                     validate_certs=self._validate_certs,\n226:                     timeout=self.timeout\n227:                 )\n228:             except Exception as err:\n229:                 raise AnsibleError(\n230:                     'Failed to download collection tar '\n231:                     \"from '{coll_src!s}': {download_err!s}\".\n232:                     format(\n233:                         coll_src=to_native(collection.src),\n234:                         download_err=to_native(err),\n235:                     ),\n236:                 ) from err\n237:         elif collection.is_scm:\n238:             b_artifact_path = _extract_collection_from_git(\n239:                 collection.src,\n240:                 collection.ver,\n241:                 self._b_working_directory,\n242:             )\n243:         elif collection.is_file or collection.is_dir or collection.is_subdirs:\n244:             b_artifact_path = to_bytes(collection.src)\n245:         else:\n246:             # NOTE: This may happen `if collection.is_online_index_pointer`\n247:             raise RuntimeError(\n248:                 'The artifact is of an unexpected type {art_type!s}'.\n249:                 format(art_type=collection.type)\n250:             )\n251: \n252:         self._artifact_cache[collection.src] = b_artifact_path\n253:         return b_artifact_path\n254: \n255:     def get_artifact_path_from_unknown(self, collection):\n256:         # type: (Candidate) -> bytes\n257:         if collection.is_concrete_artifact:\n258:             return self.get_artifact_path(collection)\n259:         return self.get_galaxy_artifact_path(collection)\n260: \n261:     def _get_direct_collection_namespace(self, collection):\n262:         # type: (Candidate) -> t.Optional[str]\n263:         return self.get_direct_collection_meta(collection)['namespace']  # type: ignore[return-value]\n264: \n265:     def _get_direct_collection_name(self, collection):\n266:         # type: (Collection) -> t.Optional[str]\n267:         return self.get_direct_collection_meta(collection)['name']  # type: ignore[return-value]\n268: \n269:     def get_direct_collection_fqcn(self, collection):\n270:         # type: (Collection) -> t.Optional[str]\n271:         \"\"\"Extract FQCN from the given on-disk collection artifact.\n272: \n273:         If the collection is virtual, ``None`` is returned instead\n274:         of a string.\n275:         \"\"\"\n276:         if collection.is_virtual:\n277:             # NOTE: should it be something like \"<virtual>\"?\n278:             return None\n279: \n280:         return '.'.join((  # type: ignore[type-var]\n281:             self._get_direct_collection_namespace(collection),  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n282:             self._get_direct_collection_name(collection),\n283:         ))\n284: \n285:     def get_direct_collection_version(self, collection):\n286:         # type: (Collection) -> str\n287:         \"\"\"Extract version from the given on-disk collection artifact.\"\"\"\n288:         return self.get_direct_collection_meta(collection)['version']  # type: ignore[return-value]\n289: \n290:     def get_direct_collection_dependencies(self, collection):\n291:         # type: (t.Union[Candidate, Requirement]) -> dict[str, str]\n292:         \"\"\"Extract deps from the given on-disk collection artifact.\"\"\"\n293:         collection_dependencies = self.get_direct_collection_meta(collection)['dependencies']\n294:         if collection_dependencies is None:\n295:             collection_dependencies = {}\n296:         return collection_dependencies  # type: ignore[return-value]\n297: \n298:     def get_direct_collection_meta(self, collection):\n299:         # type: (Collection) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, dict[str, str], list[str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\n300:         \"\"\"Extract meta from the given on-disk collection artifact.\"\"\"\n301:         try:  # FIXME: use unique collection identifier as a cache key?\n302:             return self._artifact_meta_cache[collection.src]\n303:         except KeyError:\n304:             b_artifact_path = self.get_artifact_path(collection)\n305: \n306:         if collection.is_url or collection.is_file:\n307:             collection_meta = _get_meta_from_tar(b_artifact_path)\n308:         elif collection.is_dir:  # should we just build a coll instead?\n309:             # FIXME: what if there's subdirs?\n310:             try:\n311:                 collection_meta = _get_meta_from_dir(b_artifact_path, self.require_build_metadata)\n312:             except LookupError as lookup_err:\n313:                 raise AnsibleError(\n314:                     'Failed to find the collection dir deps: {err!s}'.\n315:                     format(err=to_native(lookup_err)),\n316:                 ) from lookup_err\n317:         elif collection.is_scm:\n318:             collection_meta = {\n319:                 'name': None,\n320:                 'namespace': None,\n321:                 'dependencies': {to_native(b_artifact_path): '*'},\n322:                 'version': '*',\n323:             }\n324:         elif collection.is_subdirs:\n325:             collection_meta = {\n326:                 'name': None,\n327:                 'namespace': None,\n328:                 # NOTE: Dropping b_artifact_path since it's based on src anyway\n329:                 'dependencies': dict.fromkeys(\n330:                     map(to_native, collection.namespace_collection_paths),\n331:                     '*',\n332:                 ),\n333:                 'version': '*',\n334:             }\n335:         else:\n336:             raise RuntimeError\n337: \n338:         self._artifact_meta_cache[collection.src] = collection_meta\n339:         return collection_meta\n340: \n341:     def save_collection_source(self, collection, url, sha256_hash, token, signatures_url, signatures):\n342:         # type: (Candidate, str, str, GalaxyToken, str, list[dict[str, str]]) -> None\n343:         \"\"\"Store collection URL, SHA256 hash and Galaxy API token.\n344: \n345:         This is a hook that is supposed to be called before attempting to\n346:         download Galaxy-based collections with ``get_galaxy_artifact_path()``.\n347:         \"\"\"\n348:         self._galaxy_collection_cache[collection] = url, sha256_hash, token\n349:         self._galaxy_collection_origin_cache[collection] = signatures_url, signatures\n350: \n351:     @classmethod\n352:     @contextmanager\n353:     def under_tmpdir(\n354:             cls,\n355:             temp_dir_base,  # type: str\n356:             validate_certs=True,  # type: bool\n357:             keyring=None,  # type: str\n358:             required_signature_count=None,  # type: str\n359:             ignore_signature_errors=None,  # type: list[str]\n360:             require_build_metadata=True,  # type: bool\n361:     ):  # type: (...) -> t.Iterator[ConcreteArtifactsManager]\n362:         \"\"\"Custom ConcreteArtifactsManager constructor with temp dir.\n363: \n364:         This method returns a context manager that allocates and cleans\n365:         up a temporary directory for caching the collection artifacts\n366:         during the dependency resolution process.\n367:         \"\"\"\n368:         # NOTE: Can't use `with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory:`\n369:         # NOTE: because it's not in Python 2 stdlib.\n370:         temp_path = mkdtemp(\n371:             dir=to_bytes(temp_dir_base, errors='surrogate_or_strict'),\n372:         )\n373:         b_temp_path = to_bytes(temp_path, errors='surrogate_or_strict')\n374:         try:\n375:             yield cls(\n376:                 b_temp_path,\n377:                 validate_certs,\n378:                 keyring=keyring,\n379:                 required_signature_count=required_signature_count,\n380:                 ignore_signature_errors=ignore_signature_errors\n381:             )\n382:         finally:\n383:             rmtree(b_temp_path)\n384: \n385: \n386: def parse_scm(collection, version):\n387:     \"\"\"Extract name, version, path and subdir out of the SCM pointer.\"\"\"\n388:     if ',' in collection:\n389:         collection, version = collection.split(',', 1)\n390:     elif version == '*' or not version:\n391:         version = 'HEAD'\n392: \n393:     if collection.startswith('git+'):\n394:         path = collection[4:]\n395:     else:\n396:         path = collection\n397: \n398:     path, fragment = urldefrag(path)\n399:     fragment = fragment.strip(os.path.sep)\n400: \n401:     if path.endswith(os.path.sep + '.git'):\n402:         name = path.split(os.path.sep)[-2]\n403:     elif '://' not in path and '@' not in path:\n404:         name = path\n405:     else:\n406:         name = path.split('/')[-1]\n407:         if name.endswith('.git'):\n408:             name = name[:-4]\n409: \n410:     return name, version, path, fragment\n411: \n412: \n413: def _extract_collection_from_git(repo_url, coll_ver, b_path):\n414:     name, version, git_url, fragment = parse_scm(repo_url, coll_ver)\n415:     b_checkout_path = mkdtemp(\n416:         dir=b_path,\n417:         prefix=to_bytes(name, errors='surrogate_or_strict'),\n418:     )\n419: \n420:     try:\n421:         git_executable = get_bin_path('git')\n422:     except ValueError as err:\n423:         raise AnsibleError(\n424:             \"Could not find git executable to extract the collection from the Git repository `{repo_url!s}`.\".\n425:             format(repo_url=to_native(git_url))\n426:         ) from err\n427: \n428:     # Perform a shallow clone if simply cloning HEAD\n429:     if version == 'HEAD':\n430:         git_clone_cmd = [git_executable, 'clone', '--depth=1', git_url, to_text(b_checkout_path)]\n431:     else:\n432:         git_clone_cmd = [git_executable, 'clone', git_url, to_text(b_checkout_path)]\n433:     # FIXME: '--branch', version\n434: \n435:     if context.CLIARGS['ignore_certs'] or C.GALAXY_IGNORE_CERTS:\n436:         git_clone_cmd.extend(['-c', 'http.sslVerify=false'])\n437: \n438:     try:\n439:         subprocess.check_call(git_clone_cmd)\n440:     except subprocess.CalledProcessError as proc_err:\n441:         raise AnsibleError(  # should probably be LookupError\n442:             'Failed to clone a Git repository from `{repo_url!s}`.'.\n443:             format(repo_url=to_native(git_url)),\n444:         ) from proc_err\n445: \n446:     if version == 'HEAD':\n447:         git_args = ()\n448:     else:\n449:         git_args = '-c', 'advice.detachedHead=false'\n450: \n451:     git_switch_cmd = git_executable, *git_args, 'checkout', to_text(version)\n452:     try:\n453:         subprocess.check_call(git_switch_cmd, cwd=b_checkout_path)\n454:     except subprocess.CalledProcessError as proc_err:\n455:         raise AnsibleError(  # should probably be LookupError\n456:             'Failed to switch a cloned Git repo `{repo_url!s}` '\n457:             'to the requested revision `{revision!s}`.'.\n458:             format(\n459:                 revision=to_native(version),\n460:                 repo_url=to_native(git_url),\n461:             ),\n462:         ) from proc_err\n463: \n464:     return (\n465:         os.path.join(b_checkout_path, to_bytes(fragment))\n466:         if fragment else b_checkout_path\n467:     )\n468: \n469: \n470: # FIXME: use random subdirs while preserving the file names\n471: @retry_with_delays_and_condition(\n472:     backoff_iterator=generate_jittered_backoff(retries=6, delay_base=2, delay_threshold=40),\n473:     should_retry_error=should_retry_error\n474: )\n475: def _download_file(url, b_path, expected_hash, validate_certs, token=None, timeout=60):\n476:     # type: (str, bytes, t.Optional[str], bool, GalaxyToken, int) -> bytes\n477:     # ^ NOTE: used in download and verify_collections ^\n478:     b_tarball_name = to_bytes(\n479:         url.rsplit('/', 1)[1], errors='surrogate_or_strict',\n480:     )\n481:     b_file_name = b_tarball_name[:-len('.tar.gz')]\n482: \n483:     b_tarball_dir = mkdtemp(\n484:         dir=b_path,\n485:         prefix=b'-'.join((b_file_name, b'')),\n486:     )  # type: bytes\n487: \n488:     b_file_path = os.path.join(b_tarball_dir, b_tarball_name)\n489: \n490:     display.display(\"Downloading %s to %s\" % (url, to_text(b_tarball_dir)))\n491:     # NOTE: Galaxy redirects downloads to S3 which rejects the request\n492:     # NOTE: if an Authorization header is attached so don't redirect it\n493:     resp = open_url(\n494:         to_native(url, errors='surrogate_or_strict'),\n495:         validate_certs=validate_certs,\n496:         headers=None if token is None else token.headers(),\n497:         unredirected_headers=['Authorization'], http_agent=user_agent(),\n498:         timeout=timeout\n499:     )\n500: \n501:     with open(b_file_path, 'wb') as download_file:  # type: t.BinaryIO\n502:         actual_hash = _consume_file(resp, write_to=download_file)\n503: \n504:     if expected_hash:\n505:         display.vvvv(\n506:             'Validating downloaded file hash {actual_hash!s} with '\n507:             'expected hash {expected_hash!s}'.\n508:             format(actual_hash=actual_hash, expected_hash=expected_hash)\n509:         )\n510:         if expected_hash != actual_hash:\n511:             raise AnsibleError('Mismatch artifact hash with downloaded file')\n512: \n513:     return b_file_path\n514: \n515: \n516: def _consume_file(read_from, write_to=None):\n517:     # type: (t.BinaryIO, t.BinaryIO) -> str\n518:     bufsize = 65536\n519:     sha256_digest = sha256()\n520:     data = read_from.read(bufsize)\n521:     while data:\n522:         if write_to is not None:\n523:             write_to.write(data)\n524:             write_to.flush()\n525:         sha256_digest.update(data)\n526:         data = read_from.read(bufsize)\n527: \n528:     return sha256_digest.hexdigest()\n529: \n530: \n531: def _normalize_galaxy_yml_manifest(\n532:         galaxy_yml,  # type: dict[str, t.Union[str, list[str], dict[str, str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\n533:         b_galaxy_yml_path,  # type: bytes\n534:         require_build_metadata=True,  # type: bool\n535: ):\n536:     # type: (...) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[str], dict[str, str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\n537:     galaxy_yml_schema = (\n538:         get_collections_galaxy_meta_info()\n539:     )  # type: list[dict[str, t.Any]]  # FIXME: <--\n540:     # FIXME: \ud83d\udc46maybe precise type: list[dict[str, t.Union[bool, str, list[str]]]]\n541: \n542:     mandatory_keys = set()\n543:     string_keys = set()  # type: set[str]\n544:     list_keys = set()  # type: set[str]\n545:     dict_keys = set()  # type: set[str]\n546:     sentinel_keys = set()  # type: set[str]\n547: \n548:     for info in galaxy_yml_schema:\n549:         if info.get('required', False):\n550:             mandatory_keys.add(info['key'])\n551: \n552:         key_list_type = {\n553:             'str': string_keys,\n554:             'list': list_keys,\n555:             'dict': dict_keys,\n556:             'sentinel': sentinel_keys,\n557:         }[info.get('type', 'str')]\n558:         key_list_type.add(info['key'])\n559: \n560:     all_keys = frozenset(mandatory_keys | string_keys | list_keys | dict_keys | sentinel_keys)\n561: \n562:     set_keys = set(galaxy_yml.keys())\n563:     missing_keys = mandatory_keys.difference(set_keys)\n564:     if missing_keys:\n565:         msg = (\n566:             \"The collection galaxy.yml at '%s' is missing the following mandatory keys: %s\"\n567:             % (to_native(b_galaxy_yml_path), \", \".join(sorted(missing_keys)))\n568:         )\n569:         if require_build_metadata:\n570:             raise AnsibleError(msg)\n571:         display.warning(msg)\n572:         raise ValueError(msg)\n573: \n574:     extra_keys = set_keys.difference(all_keys)\n575:     if len(extra_keys) > 0:\n576:         display.warning(\"Found unknown keys in collection galaxy.yml at '%s': %s\"\n577:                         % (to_text(b_galaxy_yml_path), \", \".join(extra_keys)))\n578: \n579:     # Add the defaults if they have not been set\n580:     for optional_string in string_keys:\n581:         if optional_string not in galaxy_yml:\n582:             galaxy_yml[optional_string] = None\n583: \n584:     for optional_list in list_keys:\n585:         list_val = galaxy_yml.get(optional_list, None)\n586: \n587:         if list_val is None:\n588:             galaxy_yml[optional_list] = []\n589:         elif not isinstance(list_val, list):\n590:             galaxy_yml[optional_list] = [list_val]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n591: \n592:     for optional_dict in dict_keys:\n593:         if optional_dict not in galaxy_yml:\n594:             galaxy_yml[optional_dict] = {}\n595: \n596:     for optional_sentinel in sentinel_keys:\n597:         if optional_sentinel not in galaxy_yml:\n598:             galaxy_yml[optional_sentinel] = Sentinel\n599: \n600:     # NOTE: `version: null` is only allowed for `galaxy.yml`\n601:     # NOTE: and not `MANIFEST.json`. The use-case for it is collections\n602:     # NOTE: that generate the version from Git before building a\n603:     # NOTE: distributable tarball artifact.\n604:     if not galaxy_yml.get('version'):\n605:         galaxy_yml['version'] = '*'\n606: \n607:     return galaxy_yml\n608: \n609: \n610: def _get_meta_from_dir(\n611:         b_path,  # type: bytes\n612:         require_build_metadata=True,  # type: bool\n613: ):  # type: (...) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[str], dict[str, str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\n614:     try:\n615:         return _get_meta_from_installed_dir(b_path)\n616:     except LookupError:\n617:         return _get_meta_from_src_dir(b_path, require_build_metadata)\n618: \n619: \n620: def _get_meta_from_src_dir(\n621:         b_path,  # type: bytes\n622:         require_build_metadata=True,  # type: bool\n623: ):  # type: (...) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[str], dict[str, str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\n624:     galaxy_yml = os.path.join(b_path, _GALAXY_YAML)\n625:     if not os.path.isfile(galaxy_yml):\n626:         raise LookupError(\n627:             \"The collection galaxy.yml path '{path!s}' does not exist.\".\n628:             format(path=to_native(galaxy_yml))\n629:         )\n630: \n631:     with open(galaxy_yml, 'rb') as manifest_file_obj:\n632:         try:\n633:             manifest = yaml_load(manifest_file_obj)\n634:         except yaml.error.YAMLError as yaml_err:\n635:             raise AnsibleError(\n636:                 \"Failed to parse the galaxy.yml at '{path!s}' with \"\n637:                 'the following error:\\n{err_txt!s}'.\n638:                 format(\n639:                     path=to_native(galaxy_yml),\n640:                     err_txt=to_native(yaml_err),\n641:                 ),\n642:             ) from yaml_err\n643: \n644:     if not isinstance(manifest, dict):\n645:         if require_build_metadata:\n646:             raise AnsibleError(f\"The collection galaxy.yml at '{to_native(galaxy_yml)}' is incorrectly formatted.\")\n647:         # Valid build metadata is not required by ansible-galaxy list. Raise ValueError to fall back to implicit metadata.\n648:         display.warning(f\"The collection galaxy.yml at '{to_native(galaxy_yml)}' is incorrectly formatted.\")\n649:         raise ValueError(f\"The collection galaxy.yml at '{to_native(galaxy_yml)}' is incorrectly formatted.\")\n650: \n651:     return _normalize_galaxy_yml_manifest(manifest, galaxy_yml, require_build_metadata)\n652: \n653: \n654: def _get_json_from_installed_dir(\n655:         b_path,  # type: bytes\n656:         filename,  # type: str\n657: ):  # type: (...) -> dict\n658: \n659:     b_json_filepath = os.path.join(b_path, to_bytes(filename, errors='surrogate_or_strict'))\n660: \n661:     try:\n662:         with open(b_json_filepath, 'rb') as manifest_fd:\n663:             b_json_text = manifest_fd.read()\n664:     except OSError as ex:\n665:         raise LookupError(f\"The collection {filename!r} path {to_text(b_json_filepath)!r} does not exist.\") from ex\n666: \n667:     manifest_txt = to_text(b_json_text, errors='surrogate_or_strict')\n668: \n669:     try:\n670:         manifest = json.loads(manifest_txt)\n671:     except ValueError:\n672:         raise AnsibleError(\n673:             'Collection tar file member {member!s} does not '\n674:             'contain a valid json string.'.\n675:             format(member=filename),\n676:         )\n677: \n678:     return manifest\n679: \n680: \n681: def _get_meta_from_installed_dir(\n682:         b_path,  # type: bytes\n683: ):  # type: (...) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[str], dict[str, str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\n684:     manifest = _get_json_from_installed_dir(b_path, MANIFEST_FILENAME)\n685:     collection_info = manifest['collection_info']\n686: \n687:     version = collection_info.get('version')\n688:     if not version:\n689:         raise AnsibleError(\n690:             u'Collection metadata file `{manifest_filename!s}` at `{meta_file!s}` is expected '\n691:             u'to have a valid SemVer version value but got {version!s}'.\n692:             format(\n693:                 manifest_filename=MANIFEST_FILENAME,\n694:                 meta_file=to_text(b_path),\n695:                 version=to_text(repr(version)),\n696:             ),\n697:         )\n698: \n699:     return collection_info\n700: \n701: \n702: def _get_meta_from_tar(\n703:         b_path,  # type: bytes\n704: ):  # type: (...) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[str], dict[str, str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\n705:     if not os.path.exists(b_path):\n706:         raise AnsibleError(\n707:             f\"Unable to find collection artifact file at '{to_native(b_path)}'.\"\n708:         )\n709: \n710:     if not tarfile.is_tarfile(b_path):\n711:         raise AnsibleError(\n712:             \"Collection artifact at '{path!s}' is not a valid tar file.\".\n713:             format(path=to_native(b_path)),\n714:         )\n715: \n716:     with tarfile.open(b_path, mode='r') as collection_tar:  # type: tarfile.TarFile\n717:         try:\n718:             member = collection_tar.getmember(MANIFEST_FILENAME)\n719:         except KeyError:\n720:             raise AnsibleError(\n721:                 \"Collection at '{path!s}' does not contain the \"\n722:                 'required file {manifest_file!s}.'.\n723:                 format(\n724:                     path=to_native(b_path),\n725:                     manifest_file=MANIFEST_FILENAME,\n726:                 ),\n727:             )\n728: \n729:         with _tarfile_extract(collection_tar, member) as (_member, member_obj):\n730:             if member_obj is None:\n731:                 raise AnsibleError(\n732:                     'Collection tar file does not contain '\n733:                     'member {member!s}'.format(member=MANIFEST_FILENAME),\n734:                 )\n735: \n736:             text_content = to_text(\n737:                 member_obj.read(),\n738:                 errors='surrogate_or_strict',\n739:             )\n740: \n741:             try:\n742:                 manifest = json.loads(text_content)\n743:             except ValueError:\n744:                 raise AnsibleError(\n745:                     'Collection tar file member {member!s} does not '\n746:                     'contain a valid json string.'.\n747:                     format(member=MANIFEST_FILENAME),\n748:                 )\n749:             return manifest['collection_info']\n750: \n751: \n752: @contextmanager\n753: def _tarfile_extract(\n754:         tar,  # type: tarfile.TarFile\n755:         member,  # type: tarfile.TarInfo\n756: ):\n757:     # type: (...) -> t.Iterator[tuple[tarfile.TarInfo, t.Optional[t.IO[bytes]]]]\n758:     tar_obj = tar.extractfile(member)\n759:     try:\n760:         yield member, tar_obj\n761:     finally:\n762:         if tar_obj is not None:\n763:             tar_obj.close()\n764: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible@52b7d4d092359e776da0b79db9752bfc6ca91969/-/blob/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/concrete_artifact_manager.py"

that there are no diff installing from a repo vs artifact\\n41:   command: \\\"diff -ur {{ collection_from_artifact }} {{ collection_from_repo }} -x *.json\\\"\\n42:   vars:\\n43: LookupBase\\n18: \\n19: \",\"endLine\":19,\"startLine\":16},{\"content\":\"3: \\n4: If pkg_resources is not installed this test will succeed.\\n5: If pkg_resources is installed but is unable to function, this test will fail.\\n6: \\n7: One known failure case this test can detect is when ansible declares a __requires__ and then tests are run without an egg-info directory.\\n8: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n9:           {
            "content": "728: \n729:         with _tarfile_extract(collection_tar, member) as (_member, member_obj):\n730:             if member_obj is None:\n731:                 raise AnsibleError(\n732:                     'Collection tar file does not contain '\n733:                     'member {member!s}'.format(member=MANIFEST_FILENAME),\n734:                 )\n735: ",

      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "745: \n746: - name: install cache.cache at the current latest version\n747:   command: ansible-galaxy collection install cache.cache -s '{{ test_name }}' -vvv\n748:   environment:\n749:     ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH: '{{ galaxy_dir }}/ansible_collections'\n750: \n751: ",
            "endLine": 751,
            "startLine": 745
          },
          {
            "content": "189:     url: '{{ artifact_url_response.json.download_url }}'\n190:     dest: '{{ galaxy_dir }}/namespace3.tar.gz'\n191: \n192: - name: install a collection from a tarball - {{ test_id }}\n193:   command: ansible-galaxy collection install '{{ galaxy_dir }}/namespace3.tar.gz' {{ galaxy_verbosity }}\n194:   register: install_tarball\n195:   environment:\n196:     ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH: '{{ galaxy_dir }}/ansible_collections'\n197: \n198: - name: get result of install collection from a tarball - {{ test_id }}\n199:   slurp:\n200:     path: '{{ galaxy_dir }}/ansible_collections/namespace3/name/MANIFEST.json'\n201:   register: install_tarball_actual\n202: \n203: - name: assert install a collection from a tarball - {{ test_id }}\n204:   assert:\n205: ",
            "endLine": 205,
            "startLine": 189
          },
          {
            "content": "890: \n891:     # Collections are usable in ansible without a galaxy.yml or MANIFEST.json\n892:     - name: create a collection directory\n893:       file:\n894:         state: directory\n895:         path: '{{ galaxy_dir }}/ansible_collections/unrelated_namespace/collection_without_metadata/plugins'\n896: \n897:     - name: install a collection to the same installation directory - {{ test_id }}\n898:       command: ansible-galaxy collection install namespace1.name1\n899:       environment:\n900:         ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH: '{{ galaxy_dir }}/ansible_collections'\n901:       register: install_req\n902: \n903:     - name: assert installed collections with ansible-galaxy install - {{ test_id }}\n904:       assert:\n905: ",
            "endLine": 905,
            "startLine": 890
          }

      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "2: # This test ensures that the bin entry points created by ansible-test work\n3: # when ansible-test is running from an install instead of from source.\n4: \n5: ",
            "endLine": 5,
            "startLine": 2
          },
          {
            "content": "19:     if [ \"${name}\" == \"ansible-test\" ]; then\n20:         echo \"skipped - ansible-test does not support self-testing from an install\"\n21:     else\n22: ",
            "endLine": 22,
            "startLine": 19
          },
          {
            "content": "6: \n7: # The PATH entry ending in \"-bin\" is the injected bin directory created by ansible-test.\n8: bin_dir=\"$(python -c 'import os; print([path for path in os.environ[\"PATH\"].split(\":\") if path.endswith(\"-bin\")][0])')\"\n9: ",
            "endLine": 9,
            "startLine": 6
          }
        ],
        "file": "test/integration/targets/ansible-test-installed/ansible_collections/ns/col/tests/integration/targets/installed/runme.sh",
        "repo": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/blob/test/integration/targets/ansible-test-installed/ansible_collections/ns/col/tests/integration/targets/installed/runme.sh"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "13: \n14:     - name: create test collection install directory - {{ test_name }}\n15:       file:\n16:         path: '{{ galaxy_dir }}/ansible_collections'\n17:         state: directory\n18: \n19:     - name: install simple collection from first accessible server (expected failure)\n20:       command: \"ansible-galaxy collection install namespace1.name1 {{ galaxy_verbosity }}\"\n21:       environment:\n22:         ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH: '{{ galaxy_dir }}/ansible_collections'\n23:         PATH: \"{{ venv_dest }}/bin:{{ ansible_env.PATH }}\"\n24: ",

        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/blob/test/integration/targets/ansible-galaxy-collection-cli/tasks/main.yml"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "1: - name: Install all collections by default\n2:   command: 'ansible-galaxy collection install git+file://{{ test_repo_path }}/.git'\n3: \n4: - name: list installed collections\n5:   command: 'ansible-galaxy collection list'\n6:   register: installed_collections\n7: \n8: ",
            "endLine": 8,
            "startLine": 1
          },
          {
            "content": "9:     that:\n10:       - \"'ansible_test.collection_1' in installed_collections.stdout\"\n11:       - \"'ansible_test.collection_2' in installed_collections.stdout\"\n12: \n13: - name: install from artifact to another path to compare contents\n14:   command: 'ansible-galaxy collection install {{ artifact_path }} -p {{ alt_install_path }} --no-deps'\n15:   vars:\n16:     artifact_path: \"{{ galaxy_dir }}/ansible_test-collection_1-1.0.0.tar.gz\"\n17:   environment:\n18:     ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH: \"\"\n19: \n20: ",
            "endLine": 20,
            "startLine": 9
          },
          {
            "content": "39: \n40: - name: test that there are no diff installing from a repo vs artifact\n41:   command: \"diff -ur {{ collection_from_artifact }} {{ collection_from_repo }} -x *.json\"\n42:   vars:\n43:     collection_from_repo: \"{{ install_path }}/ansible_test/collection_1\"\n44:     collection_from_artifact: \"{{ alt_install_path }}/ansible_test/collection_1\"\n45: \n46: - include_tasks: ./empty_installed_collections.yml\n47:   when: cleanup\n48: ",
            "endLine": 48,
            "startLine": 39
          }
        ],
        "file": "test/integration/targets/ansible-galaxy-collection-scm/tasks/multi_collection_repo_all.yml",
        "repo": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/blob/test/integration/targets/ansible-galaxy-collection-scm/tasks/multi_collection_repo_all.yml"

            "content": "19: \n20: class LookupModule(LookupBase):\n21:     def run(self, terms, variables=None, **kwargs):\n22: ",
            "endLine": 22,

          {
            "content": "16: \n17: from ansible.plugins.lookup import LookupBase\n18: \n19: ",
            "endLine": 19,
            "startLine": 16
          },
          {
            "content": "3: \n4: If pkg_resources is not installed this test will succeed.\n5: If pkg_resources is installed but is unable to function, this test will fail.\n6: \n7: One known failure case this test can detect is when ansible declares a __requires__ and then tests are run without an egg-info directory.\n8: \"\"\"\n9: ",
            "endLine": 9,
            "startLine": 3

        ],

collection_from_repo }} -x *.json\\\"\\n42:   vars:\\n43:     collection_from_repo: \\\"{{ install_path }}/ansible_test/collection_1\\\"\\n44:     collection_from_artifact: \\\"{{ alt_install_path galaxy_dir }}/ansible_test-collection_1-1.0.0.tar.gz\\\"\\n17:   environment:\\n18:     ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH: \\\"\\\"\\n19: \\n20: - name: check if the files and folders in build_ignore were \",\"endLine\":24,\"startLine\":13}],\"file\":\"test/integration/targets/ansible-galaxy-collection-scm/tasks/multi_collection_repo_all.yml\",\"repo\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/blob/test/integration/targets/ansible-galaxy-collection-scm/tasks/multi_collection_repo_all.yml\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"24: \\n25: # this monkeypatch to py.path.local.LocalPath.pypkgpath fixes PEP420 resolution for collections in pytest < 6.0.0\\n26: def collection_pypkgpath(self):\\n27:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Configure the Python package path so that pytest can find our collections.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n28:     for parent in self.parts(reverse=True):\\n29:         if str(parent) == ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH:\\n30:             return parent\\n31: \",\"endLine\":31,\"startLine\":24},{\"content\":\"13: \\n14: # this monkeypatch to _pytest.pathlib.resolve_package_path fixes PEP420 resolution for collections in pytest >= 6.0.0\\n15: # NB: this code should never run under py2\\n16: def collection_resolve_package_path(path):\\n17:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Configure the Python package path so that pytest can find our collections.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n18:     for parent in path.parents:\\n19:         if str(parent) == ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH:\\n20:             return parent\\n21: \",\"endLine\":21,\"startLine\":13},{\"content\":\"6: \\n7: # set by ansible-test to a single directory, rather than a list of directories as supported by Ansible itself\\n8: ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH = os.path.join(os.environ['ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH'], 'ansible_collections')\\n9: \\n10: \",\"endLine\":10,\"startLine\":6}],\"file\":\"test/lib/ansible_test/_util/target/pytest/plugins/ansible_pytest_collections.py\",\"repo\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/blob/test/lib/ansible_test/_util/target/pytest/plugins/ansible_pytest_collections.py\"}]}",
