            "content": "1288: \n1289: def _build_collection_tar(\n1290:         b_collection_path,  # type: bytes\n1291:         b_tar_path,  # type: bytes\n1292:         collection_manifest,  # type: CollectionManifestType\n1293:         file_manifest,  # type: FilesManifestType\n1294: ",
            "endLine": 1294,
            "startLine": 1288
          },
          {
            "content": "461: \n462: def build_collection(u_collection_path, u_output_path, force):\n463:     # type: (str, str, bool) -> str\n464:     \"\"\"Creates the Ansible collection artifact in a .tar.gz file.\n465: \n466:     :param u_collection_path: The path to the collection to build. This should be the directory that contains the\n467:         galaxy.yml file.\n468:     :param u_output_path: The path to create the collection build artifact. This should be a directory.\n469:     :param force: Whether to overwrite an existing collection build artifact or fail.\n470:     :return: The path to the collection build artifact.\n471:     \"\"\"\n472:     b_collection_path = to_bytes(u_collection_path, errors='surrogate_or_strict')\n473:     try:\n474:         collection_meta = _get_meta_from_src_dir(b_collection_path)\n475:     except LookupError as lookup_err:\n476: ",
            "endLine": 476,
            "startLine": 461
          },
          {
            "content": "1360: \n1361: def _build_collection_dir(b_collection_path, b_collection_output, collection_manifest, file_manifest):\n1362:     \"\"\"Build a collection directory from the manifest data.\n1363: \n1364:     This should follow the same pattern as _build_collection_tar.\n1365:     \"\"\"\n1366:     os.makedirs(b_collection_output, mode=S_IRWXU_RXG_RXO)\n1367: \n1368: ",
            "endLine": 1368,
            "startLine": 1360
          }
        ],

            "content": "104:             namespace_path = os.path.join(root_path, namespace_name)\n105:             collection_names = sorted(name for name in os.listdir(namespace_path) if os.path.isdir(os.path.join(namespace_path, name)))\n106: \n107:             for collection_name in collection_names:\n108:                 collection_path = os.path.join(namespace_path, collection_name)\n109: \n110:                 if collection_path == os.path.join(collection.root, collection.directory):\n111:                     collection_layout = layout\n112:                 else:\n113:                     collection_layout = self.__create_content_layout(self.__layout_providers, self.__source_providers, collection_path, False)[0]\n114: \n115:                 file_count = len(collection_layout.all_files())\n116: \n117: ",

            "endLine": 13,

            "content": "40: - name: test that there are no diff installing from a repo vs artifact\n41:   command: \"diff -ur {{ collection_from_artifact }} {{ collection_from_repo }} -x *.json\"\n42:   vars:\n43:     collection_from_repo: \"{{ install_path }}/ansible_test/collection_1\"\n44:     collection_from_artifact: \"{{ alt_install_path }}/ansible_test/collection_1\"\n45: \n46: - include_tasks: ./empty_installed_collections.yml\n47:   when: cleanup\n48: ",

            "content": "24: \n25: # this monkeypatch to py.path.local.LocalPath.pypkgpath fixes PEP420 resolution for collections in pytest < 6.0.0\n26: def collection_pypkgpath(self):\n27:     \"\"\"Configure the Python package path so that pytest can find our collections.\"\"\"\n28:     for parent in self.parts(reverse=True):\n29:         if str(parent) == ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH:\n30:             return parent\n31: ",
            "endLine": 31,
            "startLine": 24
          },
          {
            "content": "13: \n14: # this monkeypatch to _pytest.pathlib.resolve_package_path fixes PEP420 resolution for collections in pytest >= 6.0.0\n15: # NB: this code should never run under py2\n16: def collection_resolve_package_path(path):\n17:     \"\"\"Configure the Python package path so that pytest can find our collections.\"\"\"\n18:     for parent in path.parents:\n19:         if str(parent) == ANSIBLE_COLLECTIONS_PATH:\n20:             return parent\n21: ",
            "endLine": 21,

12. Task @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Find tar extraction and member caching code in ansible galaxy collection",
  "prompt": "Search the Ansible codebase for the galaxy collection installation code that handles tar file extraction. Specifically, look for:\n\n1. Code that normalizes tar member names (e.g., stripping trailing path separators)\n2. A custom cache or index of tar members\n3. Code that builds a metadata dictionary from tar members\n4. Any workarounds related to CPython tarfile bugs\n\nFocus on:\n- lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py\n- lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/concrete_artifact_manager.py\n\nLook for functions that:\n- Handle directory entries in tar files\n- Create or use a member name cache/index\n- Extract collection contents from tarballs\n- Deal with isdir() checks on tar members\n\nReturn the relevant code sections showing how members are cached and accessed.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "ab9868312dcc65f5b",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now I have all the information. Let me create a comprehensive summary with the code sections:\n\n## Summary\n\nI've found the galaxy collection installation code that handles tar file extraction and member caching. Here are the relevant code sections:\n\n### 1. **Custom Member Cache - Workaround for CPython Tarfile Bug**\n\nFile: `/app/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py` (from commit 56b67cccc5)\n\nThis code was introduced to work around **Python issue #47231** for Python versions before 3.10.6. It creates a normalized cache of tar members:\n\n```python\ndef install_artifact(b_coll_targz_path, b_collection_path, b_temp_path, signatures, keyring, required_signature_count, ignore_signature_errors):\n    \"\"\"Install a collection from tarball under a given path.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        with tarfile.open(b_coll_targz_path, mode='r') as collection_tar:\n            # Remove this once py3.11 is our controller minimum\n            # Workaround for https://bugs.python.org/issue47231\n            # See _extract_tar_dir\n            collection_tar._ansible_normalized_cache = {\n                m.name.removesuffix(os.path.sep): m for m in collection_tar.getmembers()\n            }  # deprecated: description='TarFile member index' core_version='2.18' python_version='3.11'\n            \n            # ... rest of extraction code\n```\n\n**Key aspects:**\n- Builds a dictionary with normalized member names (trailing path separators removed using `removesuffix(os.path.sep)`)\n- Stores this as `_ansible_normalized_cache` attribute on the TarFile object\n- Maps normalized names to TarInfo members for fast lookup\n\n### 2. **Normalized Tar Member Name Handling**\n\nFile: `/app/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py` - `_extract_tar_dir()` function\n\n```python\ndef _extract_tar_dir(tar, dirname, b_dest):\n    \"\"\" Extracts a directory from a collection tar. \"\"\"\n    dirname = to_native(dirname, errors='surrogate_or_strict').removesuffix(os.path.sep)\n\n    try:\n        tar_member = tar._ansible_normalized_cache[dirname]\n    except KeyError:\n        raise AnsibleError(\"Unable to extract '%s' from collection\" % dirname)\n\n    b_dir_path = os.path.join(b_dest, to_bytes(dirname, errors='surrogate_or_strict'))\n\n    b_parent_path = os.path.dirname(b_dir_path)\n    os.makedirs(b_parent_path, mode=S_IRWXU_RXG_RXO, exist_ok=True)\n\n    if tar_member.type == tarfile.SYMTYPE:\n        b_link_path = to_bytes(tar_member.linkname, errors='surrogate_or_strict')\n        if not _is_child_path(b_link_path, b_dest, link_name=b_dir_path):\n            raise AnsibleError(\"Cannot extract symlink '%s' in collection: path points to location outside of \"\n                               \"collection '%s'\" % (to_native(dirname), b_link_path))\n        os.symlink(b_link_path, b_dir_path)\n    else:\n        if not os.path.isdir(b_dir_path):\n            os.mkdir(b_dir_path, S_IRWXU_RXG_RXO)\n```\n\n**Key points:**\n- Removes trailing path separators from directory names using `removesuffix(os.path.sep)`\n- Uses the normalized cache instead of calling `tar.getmember()` directly\n- Handles both symlinks and regular directories using `tarinfo.isdir()` and `tarinfo.type` checks\n\n### 3. **Tar File Extraction Wrapper**\n\nFile: `/app/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/concrete_artifact_manager.py`\n\n```python\n@contextmanager\ndef _tarfile_extract(\n        tar,  # type: tarfile.TarFile\n        member,  # type: tarfile.TarInfo\n):\n    # type: (...) -> t.Iterator[tuple[tarfile.TarInfo, t.Optional[t.IO[bytes]]]]\n    tar_obj = tar.extractfile(member)\n    try:\n        yield member, tar_obj\n    finally:\n        if tar_obj is not None:\n            tar_obj.close()\n```\n\n### 4. **Metadata Dictionary Building from Tar Members**\n\nFile: `/app/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/concrete_artifact_manager.py` - `_get_meta_from_tar()` function\n\n```python\ndef _get_meta_from_tar(\n        b_path,  # type: bytes\n):  # type: (...) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[str], dict[str, str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\n    if not os.path.exists(b_path):\n        raise AnsibleError(\n            f\"Unable to find collection artifact file at '{to_native(b_path)}'.\"\n        )\n\n    if not tarfile.is_tarfile(b_path):\n        raise AnsibleError(\n            \"Collection artifact at '{path!s}' is not a valid tar file.\".\n            format(path=to_native(b_path)),\n        )\n\n    with tarfile.open(b_path, mode='r') as collection_tar:  # type: tarfile.TarFile\n        try:\n            member = collection_tar.getmember(MANIFEST_FILENAME)\n        except KeyError:\n            raise AnsibleError(\n                \"Collection at '{path!s}' does not contain the \"\n                'required file {manifest_file!s}.'.\n                format(\n                    path=to_native(b_path),\n                    manifest_file=MANIFEST_FILENAME,\n                ),\n            )\n\n        with _tarfile_extract(collection_tar, member) as (_member, member_obj):\n            if member_obj is None:\n                raise AnsibleError(\n                    'Collection tar file does not contain '\n                    'member {member!s}'.format(member=MANIFEST_FILENAME),\n                )\n\n            text_content = to_text(\n                member_obj.read(),\n                errors='surrogate_or_strict',\n            )\n\n            try:\n                manifest = json.loads(text_content)\n            except ValueError:\n                raise AnsibleError(\n                    'Collection tar file member {member!s} does not '\n                    'contain a valid json string.'.\n                    format(member=MANIFEST_FILENAME),\n                )\n            return manifest['collection_info']\n```\n\n### 5. **File Extraction with Hash Verification**\n\nFile: `/app/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py` - `_extract_tar_file()` function\n\n```python\ndef _extract_tar_file(tar, filename, b_dest, b_temp_path, expected_hash=None):\n    \"\"\" Extracts a file from a collection tar. \"\"\"\n    with _get_tar_file_member(tar, filename) as (tar_member, tar_obj):\n        if tar_member.type == tarfile.SYMTYPE:\n            actual_hash = _consume_file(tar_obj)\n        else:\n            with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(dir=b_temp_path, delete=False) as tmpfile_obj:\n                actual_hash = _consume_file(tar_obj, tmpfile_obj)\n\n        if expected_hash and actual_hash != expected_hash:\n            raise AnsibleError(\"Checksum mismatch for '%s' inside collection at '%s'\"\n                               % (to_native(filename, errors='surrogate_or_strict'), to_native(tar.name)))\n\n        b_dest_filepath = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(b_dest, to_bytes(filename, errors='surrogate_or_strict')))\n        b_parent_dir = os.path.dirname(b_dest_filepath)\n        if not _is_child_path(b_parent_dir, b_dest):\n            raise AnsibleError(\"Cannot extract tar entry '%s' as it will be placed outside the collection directory\"\n                               % to_native(filename, errors='surrogate_or_strict'))\n\n        if not os.path.exists(b_parent_dir):\n            os.makedirs(b_parent_dir, mode=S_IRWXU_RXG_RXO)\n\n        if tar_member.type == tarfile.SYMTYPE:\n            b_link_path = to_bytes(tar_member.linkname, errors='surrogate_or_strict')\n            if not _is_child_path(b_link_path, b_dest, link_name=b_dest_filepath):\n                raise AnsibleError(\"Cannot extract symlink '%s' in collection: path points to location outside of \"\n                                   \"collection '%s'\" % (to_native(filename), b_link_path))\n            os.symlink(b_link_path, b_dest_filepath)\n        else:\n            shutil.move(to_bytes(tmpfile_obj.name, errors='surrogate_or_strict'), b_dest_filepath)\n\n            # Default to rw-r--r-- and only add execute if the tar file has execute.\n            tar_member = tar.getmember(to_native(filename, errors='surrogate_or_strict'))\n            new_mode = S_IRWU_RG_RO\n            if stat.S_IMODE(tar_member.mode) & stat.S_IXUSR:\n                new_mode |= S_IXANY\n\n            os.chmod(b_dest_filepath, new_mode)\n```\n\n### Key Technical Details:\n\n1. **CPython Bug Workaround**: The `_ansible_normalized_cache` addresses Python issue #47231 which affected tarfile member lookups in Python < 3.10.6\n2. **Path Normalization**: Uses `removesuffix(os.path.sep)` to strip trailing separators from member names\n3. **Member Cache Structure**: Dictionary mapping normalized names to `TarInfo` objects\n4. **Directory Handling**: Checks `tarinfo.type == tarfile.SYMTYPE` for symlinks and `tarinfo.isdir()` for directories\n5. **Security**: Path validation with `_is_child_path()` to prevent directory traversal attacks\n6. **Hash Verification**: SHA256 checksums validated against expected hashes during file extraction",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Search the Ansible codebase for the galaxy collection installation code that handles tar file extraction. Specifically, look for:\n\n1. Code that normalizes tar member names (e.g., stripping trailing path separators)\n2. A custom cache or index of tar members\n3. Code that builds a metadata dictionary from tar members\n4. Any workarounds related to CPython tarfile bugs\n\nFocus on:\n- lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py\n- lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/concrete_artifact_manager.py\n\nLook for functions that:\n- Handle directory entries in tar files\n- Create or use a member name cache/index\n- Extract collection contents from tarballs\n- Deal with isdir() checks on tar members\n\nReturn the relevant code sections showing how members are cached and accessed.",

"commit fd3ce2b2c16caa80df5403567533d156f1698c22\tHEAD\nAuthor: Agent <agent@example.com>\nDate:   Sat Feb 21 20:59:45 2026 +0000\n\n    sg_only truncation\n\ncommit 600c1e67b488ecaf92554f7b73b0335a8ec768f8\trefs/heads/devel\nAuthor: Martin Krizek <martin.krizek@gmail.com>\nDate:   Fri Jun 6 07:59:01 2025 +0200\n\n    Exceptions clean up (#85176)\n    \n    * replace usage of `IOError` as it is an alias to `OSError`\n    * replace usage of `socket.error` as it is an alias to `OSError`\n    * use subclasses of `OSError` rather than inspecting `errno`s\n    * utilize `exist_ok` parameter of `os.makedirs` rather than ignoring\n      `FileExistsError`\n    \n    Make the following changes to the exception handling this patch already\n    updates to be consistent with the new code:\n    * use `ex` as a name for exception being handled\n    * use `from ex` when re-raising exception for additional context\n    * use f-strings and `!r` for quoting\n    * pass exceptions to the `exception` parameter of `fail_json`\n    * use `display.error_as_warning` rather than passing stringified\n      exception into `display.warning`\n    \n    Co-authored-by: Matt Clay <matt@mystile.com>\n\ncommit a8c68be3b949de5a6f35822725802e91972bdf20\trefs/tags/v2.19.0b5\nAuthor: Martin Krizek <martin.krizek@gmail.com>\nDate:   Mon Jun 2 09:51:03 2025 +0200\n\n    Deprecate ansible.compat.importlib_resources (#85226)\n    \n    Co-authored-by: Matt Clay <matt@mystile.com>\n    (cherry picked from commit 3505572470c90d27137aa72f566a3aec2d8f0711)\n\ncommit 3505572470c90d27137aa72f566a3aec2d8f0711\trefs/heads/devel\nAuthor: Martin Krizek <martin.krizek@gmail.com>\nDate:   Mon Jun 2 09:51:03 2025 +0200\n\n    Deprecate ansible.compat.importlib_resources (#85226)\n    \n    Co-authored-by: Matt Clay <matt@mystile.com>\n\ncommit 8c8717a8e404e3dca7e42b3d619029fa492c3ea3\trefs/tags/v2.19.0b3\nAuthor: Matt Davis <6775756+nitzmahone@users.noreply.github.com>\nDate:   Mon May 5 18:00:02 2025 -0700"

"# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-\n# Copyright: (c) 2019-2021, Ansible Project\n# GNU General Public License v3.0+ (see COPYING or https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt)\n\"\"\"Installed collections management package.\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport fnmatch\nimport functools\nimport glob\nimport inspect\nimport json\nimport os\nimport pathlib\nimport queue\nimport re\nimport shutil\nimport stat\nimport sys\nimport tarfile\nimport tempfile\nimport textwrap\nimport threading\nimport time\nimport typing as t\n\nfrom collections import namedtuple\nfrom contextlib import contextmanager\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom hashlib import sha256\nfrom io import BytesIO\nfrom importlib.metadata import distribution\nfrom importlib.resources import files\nfrom itertools import chain\n\ntry:\n    from packaging.requirements import Requirement as PkgReq\nexcept ImportError:\n    class PkgReq:  # type: ignore[no-redef]\n        pass\n\n    HAS_PACKAGING = False\nelse:\n    HAS_PACKAGING = True\n\ntry:\n    from distlib.manifest import Manifest  # type: ignore[import]\n    from distlib import DistlibException  # type: ignore[import]\nexcept ImportError:\n    HAS_DISTLIB = False\nelse:\n    HAS_DISTLIB = True\n\nif t.TYPE_CHECKING:\n    from ansible.galaxy.collection.concrete_artifact_manager import (\n        ConcreteArtifactsManager,\n    )\n\n    ManifestKeysType = t.Literal[\n        'collection_info', 'file_manifest_file', 'format',\n    ]\n    FileMetaKeysType = t.Literal[\n        'name',\n        'ftype',\n        'chksum_type',\n        'chksum_sha256',\n        'format',\n    ]\n    CollectionInfoKeysType = t.Literal[\n        # collection meta:\n        'namespace', 'name', 'version',\n        'authors', 'readme',\n        'tags', 'description',\n        'license', 'license_file',\n        'dependencies',\n        'repository', 'documentation',\n        'homepage', 'issues',\n\n        # files meta:\n        FileMetaKeysType,\n    ]\n    ManifestValueType = t.Dict[CollectionInfoKeysType, t.Union[int, str, t.List[str], t.Dict[str, str], None]]\n    CollectionManifestType = t.Dict[ManifestKeysType, ManifestValueType]\n    FileManifestEntryType = t.Dict[FileMetaKeysType, t.Union[str, int, None]]\n    FilesManifestType = t.Dict[t.Literal['files', 'format'], t.Union[t.List[FileManifestEntryType], int]]\n\nimport ansible.constants as C\nfrom ansible.errors import AnsibleError\nfrom ansible.galaxy.api import GalaxyAPI\nfrom ansible.galaxy.collection.concrete_artifact_manager import (\n    _consume_file,\n    _download_file,\n    _get_json_from_installed_dir,\n    _get_meta_from_src_dir,\n    _tarfile_extract,\n)\nfrom ansible.galaxy.collection.galaxy_api_proxy import MultiGalaxyAPIProxy\nfrom ansible.galaxy.collection.gpg import (\n    run_gpg_verify,\n    parse_gpg_errors,\n    get_signature_from_source,\n    GPG_ERROR_MAP,\n)\ntry:\n    from ansible.galaxy.dependency_resolution import (\n        build_collection_dependency_resolver,\n    )\n    from ansible.galaxy.dependency_resolution.errors import (\n        CollectionDependencyResolutionImpossible,\n        CollectionDependencyInconsistentCandidate,\n    )\n    from ansible.galaxy.dependency_resolution.providers import (\n        RESOLVELIB_VERSION,\n        RESOLVELIB_LOWERBOUND,\n        RESOLVELIB_UPPERBOUND,\n    )\nexcept ImportError:\n    HAS_RESOLVELIB = False\nelse:\n    HAS_RESOLVELIB = True\n\nfrom ansible.galaxy.dependency_resolution.dataclasses import (\n    Candidate, Requirement, _is_installed_collection_dir,\n)\nfrom ansible.galaxy.dependency_resolution.versioning import meets_requirements\nfrom ansible.plugins.loader import get_all_plugin_loaders\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common.file import S_IRWU_RG_RO, S_IRWXU_RXG_RXO, S_IXANY\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common.sentinel import Sentinel\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common.text.converters import to_bytes, to_native, to_text\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common.collections import is_sequence\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common.yaml import yaml_dump\nfrom ansible.utils.collection_loader import AnsibleCollectionRef\nfrom ansible.utils.display import Display\nfrom ansible.utils.hashing import secure_hash, secure_hash_s\n\n\ndisplay = Display()\n\nMANIFEST_FORMAT = 1\nMANIFEST_FILENAME = 'MANIFEST.json'\n\nModifiedContent = namedtuple('ModifiedContent', ['filename', 'expected', 'installed'])\n\nSIGNATURE_COUNT_RE = r\"^(?P<strict>\\+)?(?:(?P<count>\\d+)|(?P<all>all))$\"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass ManifestControl:\n    directives: list[str] = None\n    omit_default_directives: bool = False\n\n    def __post_init__(self):\n        # Allow a dict representing this dataclass to be splatted directly.\n        # Requires attrs to have a default value, so anything with a default\n        # of None is swapped for its, potentially mutable, default\n        for field_name, field_type in inspect.get_annotations(type(self), eval_str=True).items():\n            if getattr(self, field_name) is None:\n                super().__setattr__(field_name, field_type())\n\n\nclass CollectionSignatureError(Exception):\n    def __init__(self, reasons=None, stdout=None, rc=None, ignore=False):\n        self.reasons = reasons\n        self.stdout = stdout\n        self.rc = rc\n        self.ignore = ignore\n\n        self._reason_wrapper = None\n\n    def _report_unexpected(self, collection_name):\n        return (\n            f\"Unexpected error for '{collection_name}': \"\n            f\"GnuPG signature verification failed with the return code {self.rc} and output {self.stdout}\"\n        )\n\n    def _report_expected(self, collection_name):\n        header = f\"Signature verification failed for '{collection_name}' (return code {self.rc}):\"\n        return header + self._format_reasons()\n\n    def _format_reasons(self):\n        if self._reason_wrapper is None:\n            self._reason_wrapper = textwrap.TextWrapper(\n                initial_indent=\"    * \",  # 6 chars\n                subsequent_indent=\"      \",  # 6 chars\n            )\n\n        wrapped_reasons = [\n            '\\n'.join(self._reason_wrapper.wrap(reason))\n            for reason in self.reasons\n        ]\n\n        return '\\n' + '\\n'.join(wrapped_reasons)\n\n    def report(self, collection_name):\n        if self.reasons:\n            return self._report_expected(collection_name)\n\n        return self._report_unexpected(collection_name)"
36. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show devel:lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/concrete_artifact_manager.py 2>/dev/null | head -250",
  "description": "Get concrete_artifact_manager from devel branch"
}
Output
"# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-\n# Copyright: (c) 2020-2021, Ansible Project\n# GNU General Public License v3.0+ (see COPYING or https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt)\n\"\"\"Concrete collection candidate management helper module.\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport os\nimport tarfile\nimport subprocess\nimport typing as t\nimport yaml\n\nfrom contextlib import contextmanager\nfrom hashlib import sha256\nfrom urllib.error import URLError\nfrom urllib.parse import urldefrag\nfrom shutil import rmtree\nfrom tempfile import mkdtemp\n\nif t.TYPE_CHECKING:\n    from ansible.galaxy.dependency_resolution.dataclasses import (\n        Candidate, Collection, Requirement,\n    )\n    from ansible.galaxy.token import GalaxyToken\n\nfrom ansible import context\nfrom ansible.errors import AnsibleError\nfrom ansible.galaxy import get_collections_galaxy_meta_info\nfrom ansible.galaxy.api import should_retry_error\nfrom ansible.galaxy.dependency_resolution.dataclasses import _GALAXY_YAML\nfrom ansible.galaxy.user_agent import user_agent\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common.text.converters import to_bytes, to_native, to_text\nfrom ansible.module_utils.api import retry_with_delays_and_condition\nfrom ansible.module_utils.api import generate_jittered_backoff\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common.process import get_bin_path\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common.sentinel import Sentinel\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common.yaml import yaml_load\nfrom ansible.module_utils.urls import open_url\nfrom ansible.utils.display import Display\n\nimport ansible.constants as C\n\n\ndisplay = Display()\n\nMANIFEST_FILENAME = 'MANIFEST.json'\n\n\nclass ConcreteArtifactsManager:\n    \"\"\"Manager for on-disk collection artifacts.\n\n    It is responsible for:\n        * downloading remote collections from Galaxy-compatible servers and\n          direct links to tarballs or SCM repositories\n        * keeping track of local ones\n        * keeping track of Galaxy API tokens for downloads from Galaxy'ish\n          as well as the artifact hashes\n        * keeping track of Galaxy API signatures for downloads from Galaxy'ish\n        * caching all of above\n        * retrieving the metadata out of the downloaded artifacts\n    \"\"\"\n    def __init__(self, b_working_directory, validate_certs=True, keyring=None, timeout=60, required_signature_count=None, ignore_signature_errors=None):\n        # type: (bytes, bool, str, int, str, list[str]) -> None\n        \"\"\"Initialize ConcreteArtifactsManager caches and constraints.\"\"\"\n        self._validate_certs = validate_certs  # type: bool\n        self._artifact_cache = {}  # type: dict[bytes, bytes]\n        self._galaxy_artifact_cache = {}  # type: dict[Candidate | Requirement, bytes]\n        self._artifact_meta_cache = {}  # type: dict[bytes, dict[str, str | list[str] | dict[str, str] | None | t.Type[Sentinel]]]\n        self._galaxy_collection_cache = {}  # type: dict[Candidate | Requirement, tuple[str, str, GalaxyToken]]\n        self._galaxy_collection_origin_cache = {}  # type: dict[Candidate, tuple[str, list[dict[str, str]]]]\n        self._b_working_directory = b_working_directory  # type: bytes\n        self._supplemental_signature_cache = {}  # type: dict[str, str]\n        self._keyring = keyring  # type: str\n        self.timeout = timeout  # type: int\n        self._required_signature_count = required_signature_count  # type: str\n        self._ignore_signature_errors = ignore_signature_errors  # type: list[str]\n        self._require_build_metadata = True  # type: bool\n\n    @property\n    def keyring(self):\n        return self._keyring\n\n    @property\n    def required_successful_signature_count(self):\n        return self._required_signature_count\n\n    @property\n    def ignore_signature_errors(self):\n        if self._ignore_signature_errors is None:\n            return []\n        return self._ignore_signature_errors\n\n    @property\n    def require_build_metadata(self):\n        # type: () -> bool\n        return self._require_build_metadata\n\n    @require_build_metadata.setter\n    def require_build_metadata(self, value):\n        # type: (bool) -> None\n        self._require_build_metadata = value\n\n    def get_galaxy_artifact_source_info(self, collection):\n        # type: (Candidate) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[dict[str, str]]]]\n        server = collection.src.api_server\n\n        try:\n            download_url = self._galaxy_collection_cache[collection][0]\n            signatures_url, signatures = self._galaxy_collection_origin_cache[collection]\n        except KeyError as key_err:\n            raise RuntimeError(\n                'The is no known source for {coll!s}'.\n                format(coll=collection),\n            ) from key_err\n\n        return {\n            \"format_version\": \"1.0.0\",\n            \"namespace\": collection.namespace,\n            \"name\": collection.name,\n            \"version\": collection.ver,\n            \"server\": server,\n            \"version_url\": signatures_url,\n            \"download_url\": download_url,\n            \"signatures\": signatures,\n        }\n\n    def get_galaxy_artifact_path(self, collection):\n        # type: (t.Union[Candidate, Requirement]) -> bytes\n        \"\"\"Given a Galaxy-stored collection, return a cached path.\n\n        If it's not yet on disk, this method downloads the artifact first.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._galaxy_artifact_cache[collection]\n        except KeyError:\n            pass\n\n        try:\n            url, sha256_hash, token = self._galaxy_collection_cache[collection]\n        except KeyError as key_err:\n            raise RuntimeError(\n                'There is no known source for {coll!s}'.\n                format(coll=collection),\n            ) from key_err\n\n        display.vvvv(\n            \"Fetching a collection tarball for '{collection!s}' from \"\n            'Ansible Galaxy'.format(collection=collection),\n        )\n\n        try:\n            b_artifact_path = _download_file(\n                url,\n                self._b_working_directory,\n                expected_hash=sha256_hash,\n                validate_certs=self._validate_certs,\n                token=token,\n            )  # type: bytes\n        except URLError as err:\n            raise AnsibleError(\n                'Failed to download collection tar '\n                \"from '{coll_src!s}': {download_err!s}\".\n                format(\n                    coll_src=to_native(collection.src),\n                    download_err=to_native(err),\n                ),\n            ) from err\n        except Exception as err:\n            raise AnsibleError(\n                'Failed to download collection tar '\n                \"from '{coll_src!s}' due to the following unforeseen error: \"\n                '{download_err!s}'.\n                format(\n                    coll_src=to_native(collection.src),\n                    download_err=to_native(err),\n                ),\n            ) from err\n        else:\n            display.vvv(\n                \"Collection '{coll!s}' obtained from \"\n                'server {server!s} {url!s}'.format(\n                    coll=collection, server=collection.src or 'Galaxy',\n                    url=collection.src.api_server if collection.src is not None\n                    else '',\n                )\n            )\n\n        self._galaxy_artifact_cache[collection] = b_artifact_path\n        return b_artifact_path\n\n    def get_artifact_path(self, collection):\n        # type: (Collection) -> bytes\n        \"\"\"Given a concrete collection pointer, return a cached path.\n\n        If it's not yet on disk, this method downloads the artifact first.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._artifact_cache[collection.src]\n        except KeyError:\n            pass\n\n        # NOTE: SCM needs to be special-cased as it may contain either\n        # NOTE: one collection in its root, or a number of top-level\n        # NOTE: collection directories instead.\n        # NOTE: The idea is to store the SCM collection as unpacked\n        # NOTE: directory structure under the temporary location and use\n        # NOTE: a \"virtual\" collection that has pinned requirements on\n        # NOTE: the directories under that SCM checkout that correspond\n        # NOTE: to collections.\n        # NOTE: This brings us to the idea that we need two separate\n        # NOTE: virtual Requirement/Candidate types --\n        # NOTE: (single) dir + (multidir) subdirs\n        if collection.is_url:\n            display.vvvv(\n                \"Collection requirement '{collection!s}' is a URL \"\n                'to a tar artifact'.format(collection=collection.fqcn),\n            )\n            try:\n                b_artifact_path = _download_file(\n                    collection.src,\n                    self._b_working_directory,\n                    expected_hash=None,  # NOTE: URLs don't support checksums\n                    validate_certs=self._validate_certs,\n                    timeout=self.timeout\n                )\n            except Exception as err:\n                raise AnsibleError(\n                    'Failed to download collection tar '\n                    \"from '{coll_src!s}': {download_err!s}\".\n                    format(\n                        coll_src=to_native(collection.src),\n                        download_err=to_native(err),\n                    ),\n                ) from err\n        elif collection.is_scm:\n            b_artifact_path = _extract_collection_from_git(\n                collection.src,\n                collection.ver,\n                self._b_working_directory,\n            )\n        elif collection.is_file or collection.is_dir or collection.is_subdirs:\n            b_artifact_path = to_bytes(collection.src)\n        else:\n            # NOTE: This may happen `if collection.is_online_index_pointer`\n            raise RuntimeError(\n                'The artifact is of an unexpected type {art_type!s}'.\n                format(art_type=collection.type)\n            )"

38. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show devel:lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/concrete_artifact_manager.py | sed -n '697,850p'",
  "description": "Get _get_meta_from_tar function"
}
Output
"def _get_meta_from_tar(\n        b_path,  # type: bytes\n):  # type: (...) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[str], dict[str, str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\n    if not os.path.exists(b_path):\n        raise AnsibleError(\n            f\"Unable to find collection artifact file at '{to_native(b_path)}'.\"\n        )\n\n    if not tarfile.is_tarfile(b_path):\n        raise AnsibleError(\n            \"Collection artifact at '{path!s}' is not a valid tar file.\".\n            format(path=to_native(b_path)),\n        )\n\n    with tarfile.open(b_path, mode='r') as collection_tar:  # type: tarfile.TarFile\n        try:\n            member = collection_tar.getmember(MANIFEST_FILENAME)\n        except KeyError:\n            raise AnsibleError(\n                \"Collection at '{path!s}' does not contain the \"\n                'required file {manifest_file!s}.'.\n                format(\n                    path=to_native(b_path),\n                    manifest_file=MANIFEST_FILENAME,\n                ),\n            )\n\n        with _tarfile_extract(collection_tar, member) as (_member, member_obj):\n            if member_obj is None:\n                raise AnsibleError(\n                    'Collection tar file does not contain '\n                    'member {member!s}'.format(member=MANIFEST_FILENAME),\n                )\n\n            text_content = to_text(\n                member_obj.read(),\n                errors='surrogate_or_strict',\n            )\n\n            try:\n                manifest = json.loads(text_content)\n            except ValueError:\n                raise AnsibleError(\n                    'Collection tar file member {member!s} does not '\n                    'contain a valid json string.'.\n                    format(member=MANIFEST_FILENAME),\n                )\n            return manifest['collection_info']\n\n\n@contextmanager\ndef _tarfile_extract(\n        tar,  # type: tarfile.TarFile\n        member,  # type: tarfile.TarInfo\n):\n    # type: (...) -> t.Iterator[tuple[tarfile.TarInfo, t.Optional[t.IO[bytes]]]]\n    tar_obj = tar.extractfile(member)\n    try:\n        yield member, tar_obj\n    finally:\n        if tar_obj is not None:\n            tar_obj.close()"

type: bytes\n1306:    \"\"\"Build a tar.gz collection artifact from the manifest data.\"\"\"\n1312:        b_tar_filepath = os.path.join(b_temp_path, os.path.basename(b_tar_path))\n1314:        with tarfile.open(b_tar_filepath, mode='w:gz') as tar_file:\n1318:                tar_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name)\n1319:                tar_info.size = len(b)\n1320:                tar_info.mtime = int(time.time())\n1321:                tar_info.mode = S_IRWU_RG_RO\n1322:                tar_file.addfile(tarinfo=tar_info, fileobj=b_io)\n1332:                def reset_stat(tarinfo):\n1333:                    if tarinfo.type != tarfile.SYMTYPE:\n1334:                        existing_is_exec = tarinfo.mode & stat.S_IXUSR\n1335:                        tarinfo.mode = S_IRWXU_RXG_RXO if existing_is_exec or tarinfo.isdir() else S_IRWU_RG_RO\n1336:                    tarinfo.uid = tarinfo.gid = 0\n1337:                    tarinfo.uname = tarinfo.gname = ''\n1339:                    return tarinfo\n1342:                    b_link_target = os.path.realpath(b_src_path)\n1343:                    if not os.path.exists(b_link_target):\n1344:                        raise AnsibleError(f\"Failed to find the target path '{to_native(b_link_target)}' for the symlink '{to_native(b_src_path)}'.\")\n1345:                    if _is_child_path(b_link_target, b_collection_path):\n1346:                        b_rel_path = os.path.relpath(b_link_target, start=os.path.dirname(b_src_path))\n1348:                        tar_info = tarfile.TarInfo(filename)\n1349:                        tar_info.type = tarfile.SYMTYPE\n1350:                        tar_info.linkname = to_native(b_rel_path, errors='surrogate_or_strict')\n1351:                        tar_info = reset_stat(tar_info)\n1352:                        tar_file.addfile(tarinfo=tar_info)\n1357:                tar_file.add(\n1364:        shutil.copy(to_native(b_tar_filepath), to_native(b_tar_path))\n1367:        tar_path = to_text(b_tar_path)"

Input
{
  "command": "git show devel:lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py | sed -n '1763,1800p'",
  "description": "Get tar file functions"
}
Output
"def _get_json_from_tar_file(b_path, filename):\n    file_contents = ''\n\n    with tarfile.open(b_path, mode='r') as collection_tar:\n        with _get_tar_file_member(collection_tar, filename) as (dummy, tar_obj):\n            bufsize = 65536\n            data = tar_obj.read(bufsize)\n            while data:\n                file_contents += to_text(data)\n                data = tar_obj.read(bufsize)\n\n    return json.loads(file_contents)\n\n\ndef _get_tar_file_hash(b_path, filename):\n    with tarfile.open(b_path, mode='r') as collection_tar:\n        with _get_tar_file_member(collection_tar, filename) as (dummy, tar_obj):\n            return _consume_file(tar_obj)\n\n\ndef _get_file_hash(b_path, filename):  # type: (bytes, str) -> str\n    filepath = os.path.join(b_path, to_bytes(filename, errors='surrogate_or_strict'))\n    with open(filepath, 'rb') as fp:\n        return _consume_file(fp)\n\n\ndef _is_child_path(path, parent_path, link_name=None):\n    \"\"\" Checks that path is a path within the parent_path specified. \"\"\"\n    b_path = to_bytes(path, errors='surrogate_or_strict')\n\n    if link_name and not os.path.isabs(b_path):\n        # If link_name is specified, path is the source of the link and we need to resolve the absolute path.\n        b_link_dir = os.path.dirname(to_bytes(link_name, errors='surrogate_or_strict'))\n        b_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(b_link_dir, b_path))\n\n    b_parent_path = to_bytes(parent_path, errors='surrogate_or_strict')\n    return b_path == b_parent_path or b_path.startswith(b_parent_path + to_bytes(os.path.sep))"

"def install_artifact(b_coll_targz_path, b_collection_path, b_temp_path, signatures, keyring, required_signature_count, ignore_signature_errors):\n    \"\"\"Install a collection from tarball under a given path.\n\n    :param b_coll_targz_path: Collection tarball to be installed.\n    :param b_collection_path: Collection dirs layout path.\n    :param b_temp_path: Temporary dir path.\n    :param signatures: frozenset of signatures to verify the MANIFEST.json\n    :param keyring: The keyring used during GPG verification\n    :param required_signature_count: The number of signatures that must successfully verify the collection\n    :param ignore_signature_errors: GPG errors to ignore during signature verification\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        with tarfile.open(b_coll_targz_path, mode='r') as collection_tar:\n            # Verify the signature on the MANIFEST.json before extracting anything else\n            _extract_tar_file(collection_tar, MANIFEST_FILENAME, b_collection_path, b_temp_path)\n\n            if keyring is not None:\n                manifest_file = os.path.join(to_text(b_collection_path, errors='surrogate_or_strict'), MANIFEST_FILENAME)\n                verify_artifact_manifest(manifest_file, signatures, keyring, required_signature_count, ignore_signature_errors)\n\n            files_member_obj = collection_tar.getmember('FILES.json')\n            with _tarfile_extract(collection_tar, files_member_obj) as (dummy, files_obj):\n                files = json.loads(to_text(files_obj.read(), errors='surrogate_or_strict'))\n\n            _extract_tar_file(collection_tar, 'FILES.json', b_collection_path, b_temp_path)\n\n            for file_info in files['files']:\n                file_name = file_info['name']\n                if file_name == '.':\n                    continue\n\n                if file_info['ftype'] == 'file':\n                    _extract_tar_file(collection_tar, file_name, b_collection_path, b_temp_path,\n                                      expected_hash=file_info['chksum_sha256'])\n\n                else:\n                    _extract_tar_dir(collection_tar, file_name, b_collection_path)\n\n    except Exception:\n        # Ensure we don't leave the dir behind in case of a failure.\n        shutil.rmtree(b_collection_path)\n\n        b_namespace_path = os.path.dirname(b_collection_path)\n        if not os.listdir(b_namespace_path):\n            os.rmdir(b_namespace_path)\n\n        raise\n\n\ndef install_src(collection, b_collection_path, b_collection_output_path, artifacts_manager):\n    r\"\"\"Install the collection from source control into given dir.\n\n    Generates the Ansible collection artifact data from a galaxy.yml and\n    installs the artifact to a directory.\n    This should follow the same pattern as build_collection, but instead\n    of creating an artifact, install it.\n\n    :param collection: Collection to be installed.\n    :param b_collection_path: Collection dirs layout path.\n    :param b_collection_output_path: The installation directory for the \\\n                                     collection artifact.\n    :param artifacts_manager: Artifacts manager.\n\n    :raises AnsibleError: If no collection metadata found.\n    \"\"\"\n    collection_meta = artifacts_manager.get_direct_collection_meta(collection)\n\n    if 'build_ignore' not in collection_meta:  # installed collection, not src\n        # FIXME: optimize this? use a different process? copy instead of build?\n        collection_meta['build_ignore'] = []\n        collection_meta['manifest'] = Sentinel\n    collection_manifest = _build_manifest(**collection_meta)\n    file_manifest = _build_files_manifest(\n        b_collection_path,\n        collection_meta['namespace'], collection_meta['name'],\n        collection_meta['build_ignore'],\n        collection_meta['manifest'],\n        collection_meta['license_file'],\n    )\n\n    collection_output_path = _build_collection_dir(\n        b_collection_path, b_collection_output_path,\n        collection_manifest, file_manifest,\n    )\n\n    display.display(\n        'Created collection for {coll!s} at {path!s}'.\n        format(coll=collection, path=collection_output_path)\n    )\n\n\ndef _extract_tar_dir(tar, dirname, b_dest):\n    \"\"\" Extracts a directory from a collection tar. \"\"\""
51. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show devel:lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py | sed -n '1679,1750p'",
  "description": "Get tar extraction functions"
}
Output
"def _extract_tar_dir(tar, dirname, b_dest):\n    \"\"\" Extracts a directory from a collection tar. \"\"\"\n    dirname = to_native(dirname, errors='surrogate_or_strict')\n\n    try:\n        tar_member = tar.getmember(dirname)\n    except KeyError:\n        raise AnsibleError(\"Unable to extract '%s' from collection\" % dirname)\n\n    b_dir_path = os.path.join(b_dest, to_bytes(dirname, errors='surrogate_or_strict'))\n\n    b_parent_path = os.path.dirname(b_dir_path)\n    os.makedirs(b_parent_path, mode=S_IRWXU_RXG_RXO, exist_ok=True)\n\n    if tar_member.type == tarfile.SYMTYPE:\n        b_link_path = to_bytes(tar_member.linkname, errors='surrogate_or_strict')\n        if not _is_child_path(b_link_path, b_dest, link_name=b_dir_path):\n            raise AnsibleError(\"Cannot extract symlink '%s' in collection: path points to location outside of \"\n                               \"collection '%s'\" % (to_native(dirname), b_link_path))\n\n        os.symlink(b_link_path, b_dir_path)\n\n    else:\n        if not os.path.isdir(b_dir_path):\n            os.mkdir(b_dir_path, S_IRWXU_RXG_RXO)\n\n\ndef _extract_tar_file(tar, filename, b_dest, b_temp_path, expected_hash=None):\n    \"\"\" Extracts a file from a collection tar. \"\"\"\n    with _get_tar_file_member(tar, filename) as (tar_member, tar_obj):\n        if tar_member.type == tarfile.SYMTYPE:\n            actual_hash = _consume_file(tar_obj)\n\n        else:\n            with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(dir=b_temp_path, delete=False) as tmpfile_obj:\n                actual_hash = _consume_file(tar_obj, tmpfile_obj)\n\n        if expected_hash and actual_hash != expected_hash:\n            raise AnsibleError(\"Checksum mismatch for '%s' inside collection at '%s'\"\n                               % (to_native(filename, errors='surrogate_or_strict'), to_native(tar.name)))\n\n        b_dest_filepath = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(b_dest, to_bytes(filename, errors='surrogate_or_strict')))\n        b_parent_dir = os.path.dirname(b_dest_filepath)\n        if not _is_child_path(b_parent_dir, b_dest):\n            raise AnsibleError(\"Cannot extract tar entry '%s' as it will be placed outside the collection directory\"\n                               % to_native(filename, errors='surrogate_or_strict'))\n\n        if not os.path.exists(b_parent_dir):\n            # Seems like Galaxy does not validate if all file entries have a corresponding dir ftype entry. This check\n            # makes sure we create the parent directory even if it wasn't set in the metadata.\n            os.makedirs(b_parent_dir, mode=S_IRWXU_RXG_RXO)\n\n        if tar_member.type == tarfile.SYMTYPE:\n            b_link_path = to_bytes(tar_member.linkname, errors='surrogate_or_strict')\n            if not _is_child_path(b_link_path, b_dest, link_name=b_dest_filepath):\n                raise AnsibleError(\"Cannot extract symlink '%s' in collection: path points to location outside of \"\n                                   \"collection '%s'\" % (to_native(filename), b_link_path))\n\n            os.symlink(b_link_path, b_dest_filepath)\n\n        else:\n            shutil.move(to_bytes(tmpfile_obj.name, errors='surrogate_or_strict'), b_dest_filepath)\n\n            # Default to rw-r--r-- and only add execute if the tar file has execute.\n            tar_member = tar.getmember(to_native(filename, errors='surrogate_or_strict'))\n            new_mode = S_IRWU_RG_RO\n            if stat.S_IMODE(tar_member.mode) & stat.S_IXUSR:\n                new_mode |= S_IXANY\n\n            os.chmod(b_dest_filepath, new_mode)"

}
Output
"# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-\n# Copyright: (c) 2020-2021, Ansible Project\n# GNU General Public License v3.0+ (see COPYING or https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt)\n\"\"\"Concrete collection candidate management helper module.\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport os\nimport tarfile\nimport subprocess\nimport typing as t\nimport yaml\n\nfrom contextlib import contextmanager\nfrom hashlib import sha256\nfrom urllib.error import URLError\nfrom urllib.parse import urldefrag\nfrom shutil import rmtree\nfrom tempfile import mkdtemp\n\nif t.TYPE_CHECKING:\n    from ansible.galaxy.dependency_resolution.dataclasses import (\n        Candidate, Collection, Requirement,\n    )\n    from ansible.galaxy.token import GalaxyToken\n\nfrom ansible import context\nfrom ansible.errors import AnsibleError\nfrom ansible.galaxy import get_collections_galaxy_meta_info\nfrom ansible.galaxy.api import should_retry_error\nfrom ansible.galaxy.dependency_resolution.dataclasses import _GALAXY_YAML\nfrom ansible.galaxy.user_agent import user_agent\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common.text.converters import to_bytes, to_native, to_text\nfrom ansible.module_utils.api import retry_with_delays_and_condition\nfrom ansible.module_utils.api import generate_jittered_backoff\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common.process import get_bin_path\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common.sentinel import Sentinel\nfrom ansible.module_utils.common.yaml import yaml_load\nfrom ansible.module_utils.urls import open_url\nfrom ansible.utils.display import Display\n\nimport ansible.constants as C\n\n\ndisplay = Display()\n\nMANIFEST_FILENAME = 'MANIFEST.json'\n\n\nclass ConcreteArtifactsManager:\n    \"\"\"Manager for on-disk collection artifacts.\n\n    It is responsible for:\n        * downloading remote collections from Galaxy-compatible servers and\n          direct links to tarballs or SCM repositories\n        * keeping track of local ones\n        * keeping track of Galaxy API tokens for downloads from Galaxy'ish\n          as well as the artifact hashes\n        * keeping track of Galaxy API signatures for downloads from Galaxy'ish\n        * caching all of above\n        * retrieving the metadata out of the downloaded artifacts\n    \"\"\"\n    def __init__(self, b_working_directory, validate_certs=True, keyring=None, timeout=60, required_signature_count=None, ignore_signature_errors=None):\n        # type: (bytes, bool, str, int, str, list[str]) -> None\n        \"\"\"Initialize ConcreteArtifactsManager caches and constraints.\"\"\"\n        self._validate_certs = validate_certs  # type: bool\n        self._artifact_cache = {}  # type: dict[bytes, bytes]\n        self._galaxy_artifact_cache = {}  # type: dict[Candidate | Requirement, bytes]\n        self._artifact_meta_cache = {}  # type: dict[bytes, dict[str, str | list[str] | dict[str, str] | None | t.Type[Sentinel]]]\n        self._galaxy_collection_cache = {}  # type: dict[Candidate | Requirement, tuple[str, str, GalaxyToken]]\n        self._galaxy_collection_origin_cache = {}  # type: dict[Candidate, tuple[str, list[dict[str, str]]]]\n        self._b_working_directory = b_working_directory  # type: bytes\n        self._supplemental_signature_cache = {}  # type: dict[str, str]\n        self._keyring = keyring  # type: str\n        self.timeout = timeout  # type: int\n        self._required_signature_count = required_signature_count  # type: str\n        self._ignore_signature_errors = ignore_signature_errors  # type: list[str]\n        self._require_build_metadata = True  # type: bool\n\n    @property\n    def keyring(self):\n        return self._keyring\n\n    @property\n    def required_successful_signature_count(self):\n        return self._required_signature_count\n\n    @property\n    def ignore_signature_errors(self):\n        if self._ignore_signature_errors is None:\n            return []\n        return self._ignore_signature_errors\n\n    @property\n    def require_build_metadata(self):\n        # type: () -> bool\n        return self._require_build_metadata\n\n    @require_build_metadata.setter"

  "command": "git show devel:lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/concrete_artifact_manager.py | tail -300",

Output
"    return (\n        os.path.join(b_checkout_path, to_bytes(fragment))\n        if fragment else b_checkout_path\n    )\n\n\n# FIXME: use random subdirs while preserving the file names\n@retry_with_delays_and_condition(\n    backoff_iterator=generate_jittered_backoff(retries=6, delay_base=2, delay_threshold=40),\n    should_retry_error=should_retry_error\n)\ndef _download_file(url, b_path, expected_hash, validate_certs, token=None, timeout=60):\n    # type: (str, bytes, t.Optional[str], bool, GalaxyToken, int) -> bytes\n    # ^ NOTE: used in download and verify_collections ^\n    b_tarball_name = to_bytes(\n        url.rsplit('/', 1)[1], errors='surrogate_or_strict',\n    )\n    b_file_name = b_tarball_name[:-len('.tar.gz')]\n\n    b_tarball_dir = mkdtemp(\n        dir=b_path,\n        prefix=b'-'.join((b_file_name, b'')),\n    )  # type: bytes\n\n    b_file_path = os.path.join(b_tarball_dir, b_tarball_name)\n\n    display.display(\"Downloading %s to %s\" % (url, to_text(b_tarball_dir)))\n    # NOTE: Galaxy redirects downloads to S3 which rejects the request\n    # NOTE: if an Authorization header is attached so don't redirect it\n    resp = open_url(\n        to_native(url, errors='surrogate_or_strict'),\n        validate_certs=validate_certs,\n        headers=None if token is None else token.headers(),\n        unredirected_headers=['Authorization'], http_agent=user_agent(),\n        timeout=timeout\n    )\n\n    with open(b_file_path, 'wb') as download_file:  # type: t.BinaryIO\n        actual_hash = _consume_file(resp, write_to=download_file)\n\n    if expected_hash:\n        display.vvvv(\n            'Validating downloaded file hash {actual_hash!s} with '\n            'expected hash {expected_hash!s}'.\n            format(actual_hash=actual_hash, expected_hash=expected_hash)\n        )\n        if expected_hash != actual_hash:\n            raise AnsibleError('Mismatch artifact hash with downloaded file')\n\n    return b_file_path\n\n\ndef _consume_file(read_from, write_to=None):\n    # type: (t.BinaryIO, t.BinaryIO) -> str\n    bufsize = 65536\n    sha256_digest = sha256()\n    data = read_from.read(bufsize)\n    while data:\n        if write_to is not None:\n            write_to.write(data)\n            write_to.flush()\n        sha256_digest.update(data)\n        data = read_from.read(bufsize)\n\n    return sha256_digest.hexdigest()\n\n\ndef _normalize_galaxy_yml_manifest(\n        galaxy_yml,  # type: dict[str, t.Union[str, list[str], dict[str, str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\n        b_galaxy_yml_path,  # type: bytes\n        require_build_metadata=True,  # type: bool\n):\n    # type: (...) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[str], dict[str, str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\n    galaxy_yml_schema = (\n        get_collections_galaxy_meta_info()\n    )  # type: list[dict[str, t.Any]]  # FIXME: <--\n    # FIXME: \ud83d\udc46maybe precise type: list[dict[str, t.Union[bool, str, list[str]]]]\n\n    mandatory_keys = set()\n    string_keys = set()  # type: set[str]\n    list_keys = set()  # type: set[str]\n    dict_keys = set()  # type: set[str]\n    sentinel_keys = set()  # type: set[str]\n\n    for info in galaxy_yml_schema:\n        if info.get('required', False):\n            mandatory_keys.add(info['key'])\n\n        key_list_type = {\n            'str': string_keys,\n            'list': list_keys,\n            'dict': dict_keys,\n            'sentinel': sentinel_keys,\n        }[info.get('type', 'str')]\n        key_list_type.add(info['key'])\n\n    all_keys = frozenset(mandatory_keys | string_keys | list_keys | dict_keys | sentinel_keys)\n\n    set_keys = set(galaxy_yml.keys())\n    missing_keys = mandatory_keys.difference(set_keys)\n    if missing_keys:\n        msg = (\n            \"The collection galaxy.yml at '%s' is missing the following mandatory keys: %s\"\n            % (to_native(b_galaxy_yml_path), \", \".join(sorted(missing_keys)))\n        )\n        if require_build_metadata:\n            raise AnsibleError(msg)\n        display.warning(msg)\n        raise ValueError(msg)\n\n    extra_keys = set_keys.difference(all_keys)\n    if len(extra_keys) > 0:\n        display.warning(\"Found unknown keys in collection galaxy.yml at '%s': %s\"\n                        % (to_text(b_galaxy_yml_path), \", \".join(extra_keys)))\n\n    # Add the defaults if they have not been set\n    for optional_string in string_keys:\n        if optional_string not in galaxy_yml:\n            galaxy_yml[optional_string] = None\n\n    for optional_list in list_keys:\n        list_val = galaxy_yml.get(optional_list, None)\n\n        if list_val is None:\n            galaxy_yml[optional_list] = []\n        elif not isinstance(list_val, list):\n            galaxy_yml[optional_list] = [list_val]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n\n    for optional_dict in dict_keys:\n        if optional_dict not in galaxy_yml:\n            galaxy_yml[optional_dict] = {}\n\n    for optional_sentinel in sentinel_keys:\n        if optional_sentinel not in galaxy_yml:\n            galaxy_yml[optional_sentinel] = Sentinel\n\n    # NOTE: `version: null` is only allowed for `galaxy.yml`\n    # NOTE: and not `MANIFEST.json`. The use-case for it is collections\n    # NOTE: that generate the version from Git before building a\n    # NOTE: distributable tarball artifact.\n    if not galaxy_yml.get('version'):\n        galaxy_yml['version'] = '*'\n\n    return galaxy_yml\n\n\ndef _get_meta_from_dir(\n        b_path,  # type: bytes\n        require_build_metadata=True,  # type: bool\n):  # type: (...) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[str], dict[str, str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\n    try:\n        return _get_meta_from_installed_dir(b_path)\n    except LookupError:\n        return _get_meta_from_src_dir(b_path, require_build_metadata)\n\n\ndef _get_meta_from_src_dir(\n        b_path,  # type: bytes\n        require_build_metadata=True,  # type: bool\n):  # type: (...) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[str], dict[str, str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\n    galaxy_yml = os.path.join(b_path, _GALAXY_YAML)\n    if not os.path.isfile(galaxy_yml):\n        raise LookupError(\n            \"The collection galaxy.yml path '{path!s}' does not exist.\".\n            format(path=to_native(galaxy_yml))\n        )\n\n    with open(galaxy_yml, 'rb') as manifest_file_obj:\n        try:\n            manifest = yaml_load(manifest_file_obj)\n        except yaml.error.YAMLError as yaml_err:\n            raise AnsibleError(\n                \"Failed to parse the galaxy.yml at '{path!s}' with \"\n                'the following error:\\n{err_txt!s}'.\n                format(\n                    path=to_native(galaxy_yml),\n                    err_txt=to_native(yaml_err),\n                ),\n            ) from yaml_err\n\n    if not isinstance(manifest, dict):\n        if require_build_metadata:\n            raise AnsibleError(f\"The collection galaxy.yml at '{to_native(galaxy_yml)}' is incorrectly formatted.\")\n        # Valid build metadata is not required by ansible-galaxy list. Raise ValueError to fall back to implicit metadata.\n        display.warning(f\"The collection galaxy.yml at '{to_native(galaxy_yml)}' is incorrectly formatted.\")\n        raise ValueError(f\"The collection galaxy.yml at '{to_native(galaxy_yml)}' is incorrectly formatted.\")\n\n    return _normalize_galaxy_yml_manifest(manifest, galaxy_yml, require_build_metadata)\n\n\ndef _get_json_from_installed_dir(\n        b_path,  # type: bytes\n        filename,  # type: str\n):  # type: (...) -> dict\n\n    b_json_filepath = os.path.join(b_path, to_bytes(filename, errors='surrogate_or_strict'))\n\n    try:\n        with open(b_json_filepath, 'rb') as manifest_fd:\n            b_json_text = manifest_fd.read()\n    except OSError as ex:\n        raise LookupError(f\"The collection {filename!r} path {to_text(b_json_filepath)!r} does not exist.\") from ex\n\n    manifest_txt = to_text(b_json_text, errors='surrogate_or_strict')\n\n    try:\n        manifest = json.loads(manifest_txt)\n    except ValueError:\n        raise AnsibleError(\n            'Collection tar file member {member!s} does not '\n            'contain a valid json string.'.\n            format(member=filename),\n        )\n\n    return manifest\n\n\ndef _get_meta_from_installed_dir(\n        b_path,  # type: bytes\n):  # type: (...) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[str], dict[str, str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\n    manifest = _get_json_from_installed_dir(b_path, MANIFEST_FILENAME)\n    collection_info = manifest['collection_info']\n\n    version = collection_info.get('version')\n    if not version:\n        raise AnsibleError(\n            u'Collection metadata file `{manifest_filename!s}` at `{meta_file!s}` is expected '\n            u'to have a valid SemVer version value but got {version!s}'.\n            format(\n                manifest_filename=MANIFEST_FILENAME,\n                meta_file=to_text(b_path),\n                version=to_text(repr(version)),\n            ),\n        )\n\n    return collection_info\n\n\ndef _get_meta_from_tar(\n        b_path,  # type: bytes\n):  # type: (...) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[str], dict[str, str], None, t.Type[Sentinel]]]\n    if not os.path.exists(b_path):\n        raise AnsibleError(\n            f\"Unable to find collection artifact file at '{to_native(b_path)}'.\"\n        )\n\n    if not tarfile.is_tarfile(b_path):\n        raise AnsibleError(\n            \"Collection artifact at '{path!s}' is not a valid tar file.\".\n            format(path=to_native(b_path)),\n        )\n\n    with tarfile.open(b_path, mode='r') as collection_tar:  # type: tarfile.TarFile\n        try:\n            member = collection_tar.getmember(MANIFEST_FILENAME)\n        except KeyError:\n            raise AnsibleError(\n                \"Collection at '{path!s}' does not contain the \"\n                'required file {manifest_file!s}.'.\n                format(\n                    path=to_native(b_path),\n                    manifest_file=MANIFEST_FILENAME,\n                ),\n            )\n\n        with _tarfile_extract(collection_tar, member) as (_member, member_obj):\n            if member_obj is None:\n                raise AnsibleError(\n                    'Collection tar file does not contain '\n                    'member {member!s}'.format(member=MANIFEST_FILENAME),\n                )\n\n            text_content = to_text(\n                member_obj.read(),\n                errors='surrogate_or_strict',\n            )\n\n            try:\n                manifest = json.loads(text_content)\n            except ValueError:\n                raise AnsibleError(\n                    'Collection tar file member {member!s} does not '\n                    'contain a valid json string.'.\n                    format(member=MANIFEST_FILENAME),\n                )\n            return manifest['collection_info']\n\n\n@contextmanager\ndef _tarfile_extract(\n        tar,  # type: tarfile.TarFile\n        member,  # type: tarfile.TarInfo\n):\n    # type: (...) -> t.Iterator[tuple[tarfile.TarInfo, t.Optional[t.IO[bytes]]]]\n    tar_obj = tar.extractfile(member)\n    try:\n        yield member, tar_obj\n    finally:\n        if tar_obj is not None:\n            tar_obj.close()"

  "command": "git log --all --oneline --grep=\"tar\\|member\\|normalize\" -- \"lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/*.py\" | head -20",
  "description": "Search git log for tar-related commits"
}

"ccb00b74fe [backport][stable-2.15] Always allow \"no-other-choice\" pre-release dependencies when resolving collection dependency tree (#81747)\ne470954985 Always allow \"no-other-choice\" pre-release dependencies when resolving collection dependency tree (#81746)\n7662a05085 Always allow \"no-other-choice\" pre-release dependencies when resolving collection dependency tree\n6f65397871 galaxy: check if the target for symlink exists (#81586)\n56d142350d Add support for importlib.resources (#78915)\na02e22e902 Add --offline option to 'ansible-galaxy collection install' (#78678)\nb439e41a91 expand ansible-doc coverage (#74963)\n0f95371131 Start of moving away from six (#75863)\nece0be30ab Move ansible.galaxy.collection module into package"

Fixes #80943 (#80949)\n2cd1744be3 Use ansible.module_utils.common.text.converters (#80704)\nd5e2e7a0a8 [ansible-galaxy] Fix installing signed collections (#80661)\n460abe0cef Fetch signatures from galaxy after the dependency resolver runs (#80334)\n2ae013667e ansible-galaxy collection install retry improvements (#80180)\n321848e98d fix installing collections from git repos that contain MANIFEST.json (#79808)\n330af433f3 Use `raise ... from` instead of `six.raise_from`\n56d142350d Add support for importlib.resources (#78915)\nab76916b14 galaxy: Add license_file to manifest directives (#79420)\n942bcf6e7a Add manifest includes for reuse licenses (#79403)\n8cf7a0d3f0 Fix collection install from source respects symlinks (#78983)\na02e22e902 Add --offline option to 'ansible-galaxy collection install' (#78678)\ndeb54e4c5b Relax minimal config to enable manifest functionality (#78574)\nd2f8099118 Use MANIFEST.in style directives to build collections (#78422)\n09d0df1d87 Fix variable use before definition.   "command": "git show 56b67cccc5",

Output
"commit 56b67cccc52312366b9ceed02a6906452864e04d\nAuthor: Matt Martz <matt@sivel.net>\nDate:   Thu Jun 15 16:15:11 2023 -0500\n\n    Resolve issues on python pre-3.10.6 with collection dirs longer than 100 characters (#81061)\n\ndiff --git a/changelogs/fragments/long-collection-paths-fix.yml b/changelogs/fragments/long-collection-paths-fix.yml\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000000..47a8c5c25a\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/changelogs/fragments/long-collection-paths-fix.yml\n@@ -0,0 +1,2 @@\n+bugfixes:\n+- ansible-galaxy - Fix issue installing collections containing directories with more than 100 characters on python versions before 3.10.6\ndiff --git a/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py b/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py\nindex d591f90998..672c65795a 100644\n--- a/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py\n+++ b/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py\n@@ -1561,6 +1561,13 @@ def install_artifact(b_coll_targz_path, b_collection_path, b_temp_path, signatur\n     \"\"\"\n     try:\n         with tarfile.open(b_coll_targz_path, mode='r') as collection_tar:\n+            # Remove this once py3.11 is our controller minimum\n+            # Workaround for https://bugs.python.org/issue47231\n+            # See _extract_tar_dir\n+            collection_tar._ansible_normalized_cache = {\n+                m.name.removesuffix(os.path.sep): m for m in collection_tar.getmembers()\n+            }  # deprecated: description='TarFile member index' core_version='2.18' python_version='3.11'\n+\n             # Verify the signature on the MANIFEST.json before extracting anything else\n             _extract_tar_file(collection_tar, MANIFEST_FILENAME, b_collection_path, b_temp_path)\n \n@@ -1641,22 +1648,12 @@ def install_src(collection, b_collection_path, b_collection_output_path, artifac\n \n def _extract_tar_dir(tar, dirname, b_dest):\n     \"\"\" Extracts a directory from a collection tar. \"\"\"\n-    member_names = [to_native(dirname, errors='surrogate_or_strict')]\n-\n-    # Create list of members with and without trailing separator\n-    if not member_names[-1].endswith(os.path.sep):\n-        member_names.append(member_names[-1] + os.path.sep)\n+    dirname = to_native(dirname, errors='surrogate_or_strict').removesuffix(os.path.sep)\n \n-    # Try all of the member names and stop on the first one that are able to successfully get\n-    for member in member_names:\n-        try:\n-            tar_member = tar.getmember(member)\n-        except KeyError:\n-            continue\n-        break\n-    else:\n-        # If we still can't find the member, raise a nice error.\n-        raise AnsibleError(\"Unable to extract '%s' from collection\" % to_native(member, errors='surrogate_or_strict'))\n+    try:\n+        tar_member = tar._ansible_normalized_cache[dirname]\n+    except KeyError:\n+        raise AnsibleError(\"Unable to extract '%s' from collection\" % dirname)\n \n     b_dir_path = os.path.join(b_dest, to_bytes(dirname, errors='surrogate_or_strict'))\n \ndiff --git a/test/units/cli/galaxy/test_collection_extract_tar.py b/test/units/cli/galaxy/test_collection_extract_tar.py\nindex 526442cc9d..b84f60b619 100644\n--- a/test/units/cli/galaxy/test_collection_extract_tar.py\n+++ b/test/units/cli/galaxy/test_collection_extract_tar.py\n@@ -14,6 +14,7 @@ from ansible.galaxy.collection import _extract_tar_dir\n @pytest.fixture\n def fake_tar_obj(mocker):\n     m_tarfile = mocker.Mock()\n+    m_tarfile._ansible_normalized_cache = {'/some/dir': mocker.Mock()}\n     m_tarfile.type = mocker.Mock(return_value=b'99')\n     m_tarfile.SYMTYPE = mocker.Mock(return_value=b'22')\n \n@@ -22,23 +23,11 @@ def fake_tar_obj(mocker):\n \n def test_extract_tar_member_trailing_sep(mocker):\n     m_tarfile = mocker.Mock()\n-    m_tarfile.getmember = mocker.Mock(side_effect=KeyError)\n+    m_tarfile._ansible_normalized_cache = {}\n \n     with pytest.raises(AnsibleError, match='Unable to extract'):\n         _extract_tar_dir(m_tarfile, '/some/dir/', b'/some/dest')\n \n-    assert m_tarfile.getmember.call_count == 1\n-\n-\n-def test_extract_tar_member_no_trailing_sep(mocker):\n-    m_tarfile = mocker.Mock()\n-    m_tarfile.getmember = mocker.Mock(side_effect=KeyError)\n-\n-    with pytest.raises(AnsibleError, match='Unable to extract'):\n-        _extract_tar_dir(m_tarfile, '/some/dir', b'/some/dest')\n-\n-    assert m_tarfile.getmember.call_count == 2\n-\n \n def test_extract_tar_dir_exists(mocker, fake_tar_obj):\n     mocker.patch('os.makedirs', return_value=None)"

64. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show devel:lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py | sed -n '1588,1640p'",
  "description": "Get install_artifact from devel"
}
Output
"def install_artifact(b_coll_targz_path, b_collection_path, b_temp_path, signatures, keyring, required_signature_count, ignore_signature_errors):\n    \"\"\"Install a collection from tarball under a given path.\n\n    :param b_coll_targz_path: Collection tarball to be installed.\n    :param b_collection_path: Collection dirs layout path.\n    :param b_temp_path: Temporary dir path.\n    :param signatures: frozenset of signatures to verify the MANIFEST.json\n    :param keyring: The keyring used during GPG verification\n    :param required_signature_count: The number of signatures that must successfully verify the collection\n    :param ignore_signature_errors: GPG errors to ignore during signature verification\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        with tarfile.open(b_coll_targz_path, mode='r') as collection_tar:\n            # Verify the signature on the MANIFEST.json before extracting anything else\n            _extract_tar_file(collection_tar, MANIFEST_FILENAME, b_collection_path, b_temp_path)\n\n            if keyring is not None:\n                manifest_file = os.path.join(to_text(b_collection_path, errors='surrogate_or_strict'), MANIFEST_FILENAME)\n                verify_artifact_manifest(manifest_file, signatures, keyring, required_signature_count, ignore_signature_errors)\n\n            files_member_obj = collection_tar.getmember('FILES.json')\n            with _tarfile_extract(collection_tar, files_member_obj) as (dummy, files_obj):\n                files = json.loads(to_text(files_obj.read(), errors='surrogate_or_strict'))\n\n            _extract_tar_file(collection_tar, 'FILES.json', b_collection_path, b_temp_path)\n\n            for file_info in files['files']:\n                file_name = file_info['name']\n                if file_name == '.':\n                    continue\n\n                if file_info['ftype'] == 'file':\n                    _extract_tar_file(collection_tar, file_name, b_collection_path, b_temp_path,\n                                      expected_hash=file_info['chksum_sha256'])\n\n                else:\n                    _extract_tar_dir(collection_tar, file_name, b_collection_path)\n\n    except Exception:\n        # Ensure we don't leave the dir behind in case of a failure.\n        shutil.rmtree(b_collection_path)\n\n        b_namespace_path = os.path.dirname(b_collection_path)\n        if not os.listdir(b_namespace_path):\n            os.rmdir(b_namespace_path)\n\n        raise\n\n\ndef install_src(collection, b_collection_path, b_collection_output_path, artifacts_manager):\n    r\"\"\"Install the collection from source control into given dir.\n\n    Generates the Ansible collection artifact data from a galaxy.yml and"

to_native(dirname, errors='surrogate_or_strict')\\n1675: \\n1676:     try:\\n1677:         tar_member = tar.getmember(dirname)\\n1678:     except KeyError:\\n1679:         raise AnsibleError(\\\"Unable to extract '%s' from collection\\\" % dirname)\\n1680: \\n1681:     b_dir_path = os.path.join(b_dest, to_bytes(dirname, errors='surrogate_or_strict'))\\n1682: \\n1683: b_link_path))\\n1691: \\n1692:         os.symlink(b_link_path, b_dir_path)\\n1693: \\n1694:     else:\\n1695:         if not os.path.isdir(b_dir_path):\\n1696:             os.mkdir(b_dir_path, S_IRWXU_RXG_RXO)\\n1697: \\n1698: \\n1699: def _extract_tar_file(tar, filename, b_dest, b_temp_path, expected_hash=None):\\n1700:     \\\"\\\"\\\" Extracts a file from a collection tar. \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1701:     with _get_tar_file_member(tar, filename) as (tar_member, tar_obj):\\n1702:         if tar_member.type == tarfile.SYMTYPE:\\n1703:             actual_hash = _consume_file(tar_obj)\\n1704: \\n1705:         else:\\n1706:             with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(dir=b_temp_path, delete=False) as tmpfile_obj:\\n1707:                 actual_hash = _consume_file(tar_obj, tmpfile_obj)\\n1708: \\n1709:         if expected_hash and actual_hash != expected_hash:\\n1710:             raise AnsibleError(\\\"Checksum mismatch for '%s' inside collection at '%s'\\\"\\n1711:                                % (to_native(filename, errors='surrogate_or_strict'), to_native(tar.name)))\\n1712: \\n1713:         b_dest_filepath = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(b_dest, to_bytes(filename, errors='surrogate_or_strict')))\\n1714:         b_parent_dir = os.path.dirname(b_dest_filepath)\\n1715:         if not _is_child_path(b_parent_dir, b_dest):\\n1716:             raise AnsibleError(\\\"Cannot extract tar entry '%s' as it will be placed outside the collection directory\\\"\\n1717:                                % to_native(filename, errors='surrogate_or_strict'))\\n1718: \\n1719:         if not os.path.exists(b_parent_dir):\\n1720:             # Seems like Galaxy does not validate if all file entries have a corresponding dir ftype entry. This check\\n1721:             # makes sure we create the parent directory even if it wasn't set in the metadata.\\n1722:             os.makedirs(b_parent_dir, mode=S_IRWXU_RXG_RXO)\\n1723: \\n1724:         if tar_member.type == tarfile.SYMTYPE:\\n1725:             b_link_path = to_bytes(tar_member.linkname, errors='surrogate_or_strict')\\n1726:             if not _is_child_path(b_link_path, b_dest, link_name=b_dest_filepath):\\n1727:                 raise AnsibleError(\\\"Cannot extract symlink '%s' in collection: path points to location outside of \\\"\\n1728:                                    \\\"collection '%s'\\\" % (to_native(filename), b_link_path))\\n1729: \\n1730:             os.symlink(b_link_path, b_dest_filepath)\\n1731: \\n1732:         else:\\n1733:             shutil.move(to_bytes(tmpfile_obj.name, errors='surrogate_or_strict'), b_dest_filepath)\\n1734: \\n1735:             # Default to rw-r--r-- and only add execute if the tar file has execute.\\n1736:             tar_member = tar.getmember(to_native(filename, errors='surrogate_or_strict'))\\n1737:             new_mode = S_IRWU_RG_RO\\n1738:             if stat.S_IMODE(tar_member.mode) & stat.S_IXUSR:\\n1739:                 new_mode |= S_IXANY\\n1740: \\n1741: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible@52b7d4d092359e776da0b79db9752bfc6ca91969/-/blob/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py%3FL1671-1740\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1671: \n1672: def _extract_tar_dir(tar, dirname, b_dest):\n1673:     \"\"\" Extracts a directory from a collection tar. \"\"\"\n1674:     dirname = to_native(dirname, errors='surrogate_or_strict')\n1675: \n1676:     try:\n1677:         tar_member = tar.getmember(dirname)\n1678:     except KeyError:\n1679:         raise AnsibleError(\"Unable to extract '%s' from collection\" % dirname)\n1680: \n1681:     b_dir_path = os.path.join(b_dest, to_bytes(dirname, errors='surrogate_or_strict'))\n1682: \n1683:     b_parent_path = os.path.dirname(b_dir_path)\n1684: errors='surrogate_or_strict')\n1688:         if not _is_child_path(b_link_path, b_dest, link_name=b_dir_path):\n1689:             raise AnsibleError(\"Cannot extract symlink '%s' in collection: path points to location outside of \"\n1690:                                \"collection '%s'\" % (to_native(dirname), b_link_path))\n1691: \n1692:         os.symlink(b_link_path, b_dir_path)\n1693: \n1694:     else:\n1695:         if not os.path.isdir(b_dir_path):\n1696:             os.mkdir(b_dir_path, S_IRWXU_RXG_RXO)\n1697: \n1698: \n1699: def _extract_tar_file(tar, filename, b_dest, b_temp_path, expected_hash=None):\n1700:     \"\"\" Extracts a file from a collection tar. \"\"\"\n1701:     with _get_tar_file_member(tar, filename) as (tar_member, tar_obj):\n1702:         if tar_member.type == tarfile.SYMTYPE:\n1703:             actual_hash = _consume_file(tar_obj)\n1704: \n1705:         else:\n1706:             with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(dir=b_temp_path, delete=False) as tmpfile_obj:\n1707:                 actual_hash = _consume_file(tar_obj, tmpfile_obj)\n1708: \n1709:         if expected_hash and actual_hash != expected_hash:\n1710:             raise AnsibleError(\"Checksum mismatch for '%s' inside collection at '%s'\"\n1711:                                % (to_native(filename, errors='surrogate_or_strict'), to_native(tar.name)))\n1712: \n1713:         b_dest_filepath = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(b_dest, to_bytes(filename, errors='surrogate_or_strict')))\n1714:         b_parent_dir = os.path.dirname(b_dest_filepath)\n1715:
