{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: # -*- coding: utf-8 -*-\\n2: # Copyright (c) 2020 Ansible Project\\n3: # GNU General Public License v3.0+ (see COPYING or https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt)\\n4: \\n5: from __future__ import annotations\\n6: \\n7: import pytest\\n8: \\n9: from ansible.galaxy.collection import _extract_tar_dir\\n10: \\n11: \\n12: @pytest.fixture\\n13: def fake_tar_obj(mocker):\\n14:     m_tarfile = mocker.Mock()\\n15:     m_tarfile.type = mocker.Mock(return_value=b'99')\\n16:     m_tarfile.SYMTYPE = mocker.Mock(return_value=b'22')\\n17: \\n18:     return m_tarfile\\n19: \\n20: \\n21: def test_extract_tar_dir_exists(mocker, fake_tar_obj):\\n22:     mocker.patch('os.makedirs', return_value=None)\\n23:     m_makedir = mocker.patch('os.mkdir', return_value=None)\\n24:     mocker.patch('os.path.isdir', return_value=True)\\n25: \\n26:     _extract_tar_dir(fake_tar_obj, '/some/dir', b'/some/dest')\\n27: \\n28:     assert not m_makedir.called\\n29: \\n30: \\n31: def test_extract_tar_dir_does_not_exist(mocker, fake_tar_obj):\\n32:     mocker.patch('os.makedirs', return_value=None)\\n33:     m_makedir = mocker.patch('os.mkdir', return_value=None)\\n34:     mocker.patch('os.path.isdir', return_value=False)\\n35: \\n36:     _extract_tar_dir(fake_tar_obj, '/some/dir', b'/some/dest')\\n37: \\n38:     assert m_makedir.called\\n39:     assert m_makedir.call_args[0] == (b'/some/dir', 0o0755)\\n40: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible@52b7d4d092359e776da0b79db9752bfc6ca91969/-/blob/test/units/cli/galaxy/test_collection_extract_tar.py\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: # -*- coding: utf-8 -*-\n2: # Copyright (c) 2020 Ansible Project\n3: # GNU General Public License v3.0+ (see COPYING or https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt)\n4: \n5: from __future__ import annotations\n6: \n7: import pytest\n8: \n9: from ansible.galaxy.collection import _extract_tar_dir\n10: \n11: \n12: @pytest.fixture\n13: def fake_tar_obj(mocker):\n14:     m_tarfile = mocker.Mock()\n15:     m_tarfile.type = mocker.Mock(return_value=b'99')\n16:     m_tarfile.SYMTYPE = mocker.Mock(return_value=b'22')\n17: \n18:     return m_tarfile\n19: \n20: \n21: def test_extract_tar_dir_exists(mocker, fake_tar_obj):\n22:     mocker.patch('os.makedirs', return_value=None)\n23:     m_makedir = mocker.patch('os.mkdir', return_value=None)\n24:     mocker.patch('os.path.isdir', return_value=True)\n25: \n26:     _extract_tar_dir(fake_tar_obj, '/some/dir', b'/some/dest')\n27: \n28:     assert not m_makedir.called\n29: \n30: \n31: def test_extract_tar_dir_does_not_exist(mocker, fake_tar_obj):\n32:     mocker.patch('os.makedirs', return_value=None)\n33:     m_makedir = mocker.patch('os.mkdir', return_value=None)\n34:     mocker.patch('os.path.isdir', return_value=False)\n35: \n36:     _extract_tar_dir(fake_tar_obj, '/some/dir', b'/some/dest')\n37: \n38:     assert m_makedir.called\n39:     assert m_makedir.call_args[0] == (b'/some/dir', 0o0755)\n40: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible@52b7d4d092359e776da0b79db9752bfc6ca91969/-/blob/test/units/cli/galaxy/test_collection_extract_tar.py"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"commits\":[{\"author\":\"Robin Roth <robin-roth@online.de>\",\"commit\":\"ee06cd2\",\"date\":\"2016-04-12\",\"message\":\"use custom ismount function in mount module (#2737)\\n\\n* based on cpython os.path.ismount\\n* includes patch from http://bugs.python.org/issue2466\\n* fixes #2186\\n* when the upstream bug is fixed this should be removed/rewritten\\n* use ismount from module_utils\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"use custom ismount function in mount module (#2737)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/ee06cd27b230245b1c4892d2f4eeadf34c596e3d\"},{\"author\":\"Abhijeet Kasurde <akasurde@redhat.com>\",\"commit\":\"1cc5efa\",\"date\":\"2023-08-31\",\"message\":\"tarfile: Handle deprecation warning for extract and extractall (#81545)\\n\\n* Python 3.11.4 introduces a new parameter 'filter' in extract and\\r\\nextractall in tarfile. Handle deprecation warning message emitted\\r\\nin Python 3.12.\\r\\n* added probing mechanism in ansible-galaxy code to detect broken\\r\\ndata filter implementation in tarfile.\\r\\n\\r\\nFixes: #80832\\r\\n\\r\\nSigned-off-by: Abhijeet Kasurde <akasurde@redhat.com>\\r\\nCo-authored-by: Matt Clay <matt@mystile.com>\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"tarfile: Handle deprecation warning for extract and extractall (#81545)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/1cc5efa77b3dd8b0e24cd60175b034a6c4ca4c2c\"},{\"author\":\"Sviatoslav Sydorenko (\u0421\u0432\u044f\u0442\u043e\u0441\u043b\u0430\u0432 \u0421\u0438\u0434\u043e\u0440\u0435\u043d\u043a\u043e) <webknjaz@redhat.com>\",\"commit\":\"59b3e49\",\"date\":\"2024-09-06\",\"message\":\"Stop suggesting `easy_install` in hacking (#83909)\\n\\nIt's been discouraged for the past decade. And CPython actually ships\\nwith pip nowadays, that is bundled within the built-in `ensurepip`\\nstdlib module.\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"Stop suggesting `easy_install` in hacking (#83909)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/59b3e49d70a50867053453a7e4fc0c5d31c03b65\"},{\"author\":\"Felix Fontein <felix@fontein.de>\",\"commit\":\"201aba3\",\"date\":\"2022-05-17\",\"message\":\"Update PSF-license.txt to version from CPython 3.9.5. (#77805)\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"Update PSF-license.txt to version from CPython 3.9.5. (#77805)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/201aba3d0f6801471a3f9ef66eff2b7cc0d77b97\"},{\"author\":\"Toshio Kuratomi <a.badger@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"fa00c69\",\"date\":\"2016-05-02\",\"message\":\"bytearray isn't available in python2.4 (#3583)\\n\\n* Detection of handler depends on the wrong handler failing to list the contents of the tarfile.\\n\\nUse explicit compression types with the python tarfile library to\\nachieve that.\\n\\n* bytearray isn't available in python2.4\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"bytearray isn't available in python2.4 (#3583)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/fa00c69ff2c360b0e193f2f0839609374e66126e\"},{\"author\":\"Sam Doran <sdoran@redhat.com>\",\"commit\":\"987265a\",\"date\":\"2019-09-20\",\"message\":\"Account for empty strings when splitting the host pattern (#62442)\\n\\nImprove tests\\r\\n- add more unit test cases\\r\\n- add specific integration test with more cases\\r\\n\\r\\nTesting shows no major downside to calling .strip() twice in a comprehension vs. using a regular for loop and only calling .strip() once. Going with the comprehension for ease of maintenance and because comprehensions are optimized in CPython.\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"Account for empty strings when splitting the host pattern (#62442)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/987265a6ef920466b44625097ff85f3e845dc8ea\"},{\"author\":\"Adrian Likins <alikins@redhat.com>\",\"commit\":\"7bd57ac\",\"date\":\"2016-08-18\",\"message\":\"Linux mount/fs (lsblk) facts fixes and tests. (#17036)\\n\\nFixes #10779\\r\\n\\r\\nRefactor some of the block device, mount point, and\\r\\nmtab/fstab facts collection for linux for better\\r\\nperformance on systems with lots of block devices.\\r\\n\\r\\nInstead of invoking 'lsblk' for every entry in mtab,\\r\\ninvoke it once, then map the results to mtab entries.\\r\\n\\r\\nChange the args used for invoking 'findmnt' since the\\r\\nprevious combination of args conflicts, so this would\\r\\nalways fail on some systems depending on version.\\r\\n\\r\\nAdd test cases for facts Hardware()/Network()/Virtual() classes\\r\\n__new__ method and verify they c... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"Linux mount/fs (lsblk) facts fixes and tests. (#17036)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/7bd57acda4eee70158eb48a7e0c5947fff82dfb9\"},{\"author\":\"Mohammadreza Abdoli <herbodabdoli@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"7ae5331\",\"date\":\"2020-01-17\",\"message\":\"update ismount() to match upstream from cPython (#64586)\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"update ismount() to match upstream from cPython (#64586)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/7ae53312187ad036971faeea1fd08d77a89aa67f\"},{\"author\":\"Sloane Hertel <19572925+s-hertel@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"a02e22e\",\"date\":\"2022-09-19\",\"message\":\"Add --offline option to 'ansible-galaxy collection install' (#78678)\\n\\n* Add --offline option to 'ansible-galaxy collection install' to prevent querying distribution servers\\r\\n\\r\\nThis allows installing/upgrading individual tarfiles to have dependency resolution.\\r\\nPreviously needed to be done manually with --no-deps or else all collections and dependencies needed to be included in the requirements.\\r\\n\\r\\nCo-authored-by: Sviatoslav Sydorenko <wk.cvs.github@sydorenko.org.ua>\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"Add --offline option to 'ansible-galaxy collection install' (#78678)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/a02e22e902a69aeb465f16bf03f7f5a91b2cb828\"},{\"author\":\"Matt Clay <mclay@redhat.com>\",\"commit\":\"73fadc5\",\"date\":\"2021-01-26\",\"message\":\"Clean up code in ansible-test. (#73379)\\n\\n* Relocate code to fix type dependencies.\\r\\n\\r\\n* Fix missing and unused imports.\\r\\n\\r\\n* Fix type hints.\\r\\n\\r\\n* Suppress PyCharm false positives.\\r\\n\\r\\n* Avoid shadowing `file` built-in.\\r\\n\\r\\n* Use json.JSONEncoder directly instead of super().\\r\\n\\r\\nThis matches the recommended usage and avoids a PyCharm warning.\\r\\n\\r\\n* Remove redundant regex escape.\\r\\n\\r\\n* Remove redundant find_python call.\\r\\n\\r\\n* Use tarfile.open directly.\\r\\n\\r\\n* Add changelog fragment.\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"Clean up code in ansible-test. (#73379)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/73fadc5e97b6ddcc9c8f901bdafe0653aad19523\"},{\"author\":\"Toshio Kuratomi <a.badger@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"c817bef\",\"date\":\"2018-12-04\",\"message\":\"Fix for callback plugins on Python3 when a module returns dictionary keys that aren't strings\\n\\nThis fixes one of the problems reported in https://github.com/ansible/ansible/issues/49343\\n\\nUpstream Python3 bug for the json traceback:\\nhttps://bugs.python.org/issue25457\\n\\nand PR that may fix it:\\nhttps://github.com/python/cpython/pull/8011\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"Fix for callback plugins on Python3 when a module returns dictionary keys that aren't strings\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/c817bef3ae39e8c2f43481c69e387776bc4e2b8c\"},{\"author\":\"Sam Doran <sdoran@redhat.com>\",\"commit\":\"bbef250\",\"date\":\"2020-11-12\",\"message\":\"Fix super annoying Python 2.6 multiprocessing.Queue stack trace in CI (#72604)\\n\\n* Fix super annoying Python 2.6 multiprocessing.Queue stack trace in CI\\r\\n\\r\\nA bug exists in Python 2.6 that sometimes raises an exception during interpreter shutdown. We\\r\\nencounter this frequently in our CI since we run tests on CentOS 6 as the control node, which\\r\\nhas Python 2.6.6 with this bug.\\r\\n\\r\\nThis PR adds a very minor sleep only on Python 2.6 which gets around this issue. I did lot of testing\\r\\nusing a standalon script I found that easily duplicated the issue to find the minimum sleep value\\r\\nneeded to avoid th... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"Fix super annoying Python 2.6 multiprocessing.Queue stack trace in CI (#72604)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/bbef250c2bde5e8c4aeda8629a74ddc3fa55614e\"},{\"author\":\"Jordan Borean <jborean93@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"127d54b\",\"date\":\"2020-03-24\",\"message\":\"galaxy - preserve mode properly on artifact (#68418)\\n\\n* galaxy - preserve mode properly on artifact\\r\\n\\r\\n* Fix py2 encoding issue\\r\\n\\r\\n* Update lib/ansible/galaxy/collection.py\\r\\n\\r\\nCo-Authored-By: Matt Clay <matt@mystile.com>\\r\\n\\r\\n* Use sane defaults instead of sourcing from tarfile\\r\\n\\r\\nCo-authored-by: Matt Clay <matt@mystile.com>\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"galaxy - preserve mode properly on artifact (#68418)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/127d54b3630c65043ec12c4af2024f8ef0bc6d09\"},{\"author\":\"Ben Doherty <ben@thinkoomph.com>\",\"commit\":\"fb03fc8\",\"date\":\"2016-06-01\",\"message\":\"Refactor zip and tarfile loops together, branch where calls are different\\n\\nThis fixed a few bugs and simplified the code\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"Refactor zip and tarfile loops together, branch where calls are different\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/fb03fc8eb104f5c85da39e8daccd8bf84026e032\"},{\"author\":\"\ud83c\uddfa\ud83c\udde6 Sviatoslav Sydorenko (\u0421\u0432\u044f\u0442\u043e\u0441\u043b\u0430\u0432 \u0421\u0438\u0434\u043e\u0440\u0435\u043d\u043a\u043e) <webknjaz@redhat.com>\",\"commit\":\"df08ed3\",\"date\":\"2025-03-11\",\"message\":\"\ud83d\udd25 Remove Python 2 datetime compat fallbacks\\n\\nThis patch marks the `ansible.module_utils.compat.datetime` module as deprecated, including `UTC`, `utcfromtimestamp()`  and `utcnow` shims that it provides, scheduling its removal for v2.21.\\n\\nIt also replaces any uses of the compatibility helpers with non-deprecated calls to CPython stdlib.\\n\\nPR #81874\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Matt Clay <matt@mystile.com>\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"\ud83d\udd25 Remove Python 2 datetime compat fallbacks\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/df08ed3ef38b785c2d53889be58fc580c8a27c6c\"},{\"author\":\"Sam Doran <sdoran@redhat.com>\",\"commit\":\"d45cb01\",\"date\":\"2020-06-15\",\"message\":\"ansible-galaxy - fix collection installation with trailing slashes (#70016)\\n\\nIf we fail to find a member when extracting a directory, try adding a trailing\\r\\nslash to the member name. In certain cases, the member in the tarfile will\\r\\ncontain a trailing slash but the file name in FILES.json will never contain\\r\\nthe trailing slash.\\r\\n\\r\\nIf unable to find the member, handle the KeyError and print a nicer error.\\r\\n\\r\\nAlso check if a directory exists before creating it since it may have been\\r\\nextracted from the archive.\\r\\n\\r\\nFixes #70009\\r\\n\\r\\n* Add unit tests\\r\\n* Use loop for trying to get members\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"ansible-galaxy - fix collection installation with trailing slashes (#70016)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/d45cb01b845d78779877325f03f2d1ab74e1f6b4\"},{\"author\":\"Arnaud RENARD <arenard@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"1a5fd72\",\"date\":\"2019-06-13\",\"message\":\"Ansible galaxy role download should not have to perform aditionnal check against tar archive file extension #56616. (#56617)\\n\\nAnsible galaxy role download checked for `.tar.gz`, but `tarfile.is_tarfile(...)` can identify and open any valid tarfile. This change uses transparent stream compression to make `.tar.gz` and `.tar.bz2` formats valid with python 2.6.x/2.7.x (as well as `.tar.xz` with python 3.x).\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"Ansible galaxy role download should not have to perform aditionnal check against tar archive file extension #56616. (#56617)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/1a5fd720a45d64d916d31c52c54020c4de6dfe68\"},{\"author\":\"Sloane Hertel <19572925+s-hertel@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"e84240d\",\"date\":\"2024-04-24\",\"message\":\"Fix installing roles containing symlinks (#82911)\\n\\n* Fix installing roles containing symlinks\\r\\n\\r\\nFix sanitizing tarfile symlinks relative to the link directory instead of the archive\\r\\n\\r\\nFor example:\\r\\n\\r\\nrole\\r\\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 handlers\\r\\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 utils.yml -> ../tasks/utils/suite.yml\\r\\n\\r\\nThe link ../tasks/utils/suite.yml will resolve to a path outside of the link's directory, but within the role\\r\\n\\r\\nrole/handlers/../tasks/utils/suite.yml\\r\\n\\r\\nthe resolved path relative to the role is tasks/utils/suite.yml, but if the symlink is set to that value, tarfile would extract it from role/handlers/tasks/util... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"Fix installing roles containing symlinks (#82911)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/e84240db84118017f0b61961c6b32cbe7120b3aa\"},{\"author\":\"fallencliff <hzj.zh@foxmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"06d46a1\",\"date\":\"2016-09-19\",\"message\":\"update facts.py for aix (#17539)\\n\\n* update facts.py for aix\\r\\n\\r\\nadd product_serial ,lpar_info,product_name and pv/vg info into facts\\r\\n\\r\\n10.223.219.10 | SUCCESS => {\\r\\n    \\\"ansible_facts\\\": {\\r\\n        \\\"ansible_all_ipv4_addresses\\\": [\\r\\n            \\\"77.77.77.1\\\", \\r\\n            \\\"10.223.219.10\\\"\\r\\n        ], \\r\\n        \\\"ansible_all_ipv6_addresses\\\": [\\r\\n            \\\"::1%1/0\\\"\\r\\n        ], \\r\\n        \\\"ansible_architecture\\\": \\\"chrp\\\", \\r\\n        \\\"ansible_date_time\\\": {\\r\\n            \\\"date\\\": \\\"2016-09-13\\\", \\r\\n            \\\"day\\\": \\\"13\\\", \\r\\n            \\\"epoch\\\": \\\"1473760269\\\", \\r\\n            \\\"hour\\\": \\\"17\\\", \\r\\n            \\\"iso8601\\\"... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"update facts.py for aix (#17539)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/06d46a1ecc7b6726b0e2e7341409a7ab33b549a2\"},{\"author\":\"Jordan Borean <jborean93@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"250610b\",\"date\":\"2025-09-08\",\"message\":\"Remove tarfile data_filter check (#85808)\\n\\nRemoves the now uneeded tarfile check for data_filter now that our\\nminimum Python version on the controller is 3.12 and guarantees it will\\nbe present.\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"Remove tarfile data_filter check (#85808)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/250610b924498cdc3e97994b84cad024eb105817\"},{\"author\":\"Sloane Hertel <19572925+s-hertel@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"41b62f7\",\"date\":\"2022-09-06\",\"message\":\"Limit Galaxy API calls during ansible-galaxy dependency resolution (#77468)\\n\\n* Limit Galaxy API calls during ansible-galaxy collection dependency resolution when possible\\r\\n\\r\\nInstalling a tarfile with a dependency from a Galaxy server (e.g. dependencies: {'ns.coll': '>=1.0.0'}) does not get the available versions of the dependency from the galaxy server if a sufficient version is already installed.\\r\\n\\r\\nCo-authored-by: Sviatoslav Sydorenko <wk.cvs.github@sydorenko.org.ua>\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"Limit Galaxy API calls during ansible-galaxy dependency resolution (#77468)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/41b62f7db459b7c3a1d97302da3b9f2cbf302670\"},{\"author\":\"Michael Scherer <misc@zarb.org>\",\"commit\":\"5aaf1d1\",\"date\":\"2016-11-06\",\"message\":\"On python 3.5, sys.subversion have been removed\\n\\nSo to get the type of the python interpreter, we need to look at\\nsys.implementation.name which do not return 'cpython', instead of 'CPython',\\nbut that's upstream breakage, so not much we can do.\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"On python 3.5, sys.subversion have been removed\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/5aaf1d1a15bea3b968831c00d7a8347448ead06a\"},{\"author\":\"Sloane Hertel <19572925+s-hertel@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"3a42a00\",\"date\":\"2023-11-30\",\"message\":\"Targeted fix for installing roles with symlinks containing '..' (#82165)\\n\\nSet the tarfile attribute to a normalized value from unfrackpath instead\\r\\nof validating path parts and omiting potentially invald parts\\r\\n\\r\\nAllow tarfile paths/links containing '..', '$', '~' as long as the\\r\\nnormalized realpath is in the tarfile's role directory\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"title\":\"Targeted fix for installing roles with symlinks containing '..' (#82165)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/3a42a0036875c8cab6a62ab9ea67a365e1dd4781\"}],\"limitHit\":true,\"query\":\"type:commit (message:tarfile OR message:CPython OR message:bug47231 OR message:\\\"directory extraction\\\") repo:github.com/ansible/ansible count:20\",\"totalCount\":23}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "commits": [
      {
        "author": "Robin Roth <robin-roth@online.de>",
        "commit": "ee06cd2",
        "date": "2016-04-12",
        "message": "use custom ismount function in mount module (#2737)\n\n* based on cpython os.path.ismount\n* includes patch from http://bugs.python.org/issue2466\n* fixes #2186\n* when the upstream bug is fixed this should be removed/rewritten\n* use ismount from module_utils",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "use custom ismount function in mount module (#2737)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/ee06cd27b230245b1c4892d2f4eeadf34c596e3d"
      },
      {
        "author": "Abhijeet Kasurde <akasurde@redhat.com>",
        "commit": "1cc5efa",
        "date": "2023-08-31",
        "message": "tarfile: Handle deprecation warning for extract and extractall (#81545)\n\n* Python 3.11.4 introduces a new parameter 'filter' in extract and\r\nextractall in tarfile. Handle deprecation warning message emitted\r\nin Python 3.12.\r\n* added probing mechanism in ansible-galaxy code to detect broken\r\ndata filter implementation in tarfile.\r\n\r\nFixes: #80832\r\n\r\nSigned-off-by: Abhijeet Kasurde <akasurde@redhat.com>\r\nCo-authored-by: Matt Clay <matt@mystile.com>",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "tarfile: Handle deprecation warning for extract and extractall (#81545)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/1cc5efa77b3dd8b0e24cd60175b034a6c4ca4c2c"
      },
      {
        "author": "Sviatoslav Sydorenko (\u0421\u0432\u044f\u0442\u043e\u0441\u043b\u0430\u0432 \u0421\u0438\u0434\u043e\u0440\u0435\u043d\u043a\u043e) <webknjaz@redhat.com>",
        "commit": "59b3e49",
        "date": "2024-09-06",
        "message": "Stop suggesting `easy_install` in hacking (#83909)\n\nIt's been discouraged for the past decade. And CPython actually ships\nwith pip nowadays, that is bundled within the built-in `ensurepip`\nstdlib module.",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "Stop suggesting `easy_install` in hacking (#83909)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/59b3e49d70a50867053453a7e4fc0c5d31c03b65"
      },
      {
        "author": "Felix Fontein <felix@fontein.de>",
        "commit": "201aba3",
        "date": "2022-05-17",
        "message": "Update PSF-license.txt to version from CPython 3.9.5. (#77805)",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "Update PSF-license.txt to version from CPython 3.9.5. (#77805)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/201aba3d0f6801471a3f9ef66eff2b7cc0d77b97"
      },
      {
        "author": "Toshio Kuratomi <a.badger@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "fa00c69",
        "date": "2016-05-02",
        "message": "bytearray isn't available in python2.4 (#3583)\n\n* Detection of handler depends on the wrong handler failing to list the contents of the tarfile.\n\nUse explicit compression types with the python tarfile library to\nachieve that.\n\n* bytearray isn't available in python2.4",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "bytearray isn't available in python2.4 (#3583)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/fa00c69ff2c360b0e193f2f0839609374e66126e"
      },
      {
        "author": "Sam Doran <sdoran@redhat.com>",
        "commit": "987265a",
        "date": "2019-09-20",
        "message": "Account for empty strings when splitting the host pattern (#62442)\n\nImprove tests\r\n- add more unit test cases\r\n- add specific integration test with more cases\r\n\r\nTesting shows no major downside to calling .strip() twice in a comprehension vs. using a regular for loop and only calling .strip() once. Going with the comprehension for ease of maintenance and because comprehensions are optimized in CPython.",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "Account for empty strings when splitting the host pattern (#62442)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/987265a6ef920466b44625097ff85f3e845dc8ea"
      },
      {
        "author": "Adrian Likins <alikins@redhat.com>",
        "commit": "7bd57ac",
        "date": "2016-08-18",
        "message": "Linux mount/fs (lsblk) facts fixes and tests. (#17036)\n\nFixes #10779\r\n\r\nRefactor some of the block device, mount point, and\r\nmtab/fstab facts collection for linux for better\r\nperformance on systems with lots of block devices.\r\n\r\nInstead of invoking 'lsblk' for every entry in mtab,\r\ninvoke it once, then map the results to mtab entries.\r\n\r\nChange the args used for invoking 'findmnt' since the\r\nprevious combination of args conflicts, so this would\r\nalways fail on some systems depending on version.\r\n\r\nAdd test cases for facts Hardware()/Network()/Virtual() classes\r\n__new__ method and verify they c... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "Linux mount/fs (lsblk) facts fixes and tests. (#17036)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/7bd57acda4eee70158eb48a7e0c5947fff82dfb9"
      },
      {
        "author": "Mohammadreza Abdoli <herbodabdoli@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "7ae5331",
        "date": "2020-01-17",
        "message": "update ismount() to match upstream from cPython (#64586)",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "update ismount() to match upstream from cPython (#64586)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/7ae53312187ad036971faeea1fd08d77a89aa67f"
      },
      {
        "author": "Sloane Hertel <19572925+s-hertel@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "a02e22e",
        "date": "2022-09-19",
        "message": "Add --offline option to 'ansible-galaxy collection install' (#78678)\n\n* Add --offline option to 'ansible-galaxy collection install' to prevent querying distribution servers\r\n\r\nThis allows installing/upgrading individual tarfiles to have dependency resolution.\r\nPreviously needed to be done manually with --no-deps or else all collections and dependencies needed to be included in the requirements.\r\n\r\nCo-authored-by: Sviatoslav Sydorenko <wk.cvs.github@sydorenko.org.ua>",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "Add --offline option to 'ansible-galaxy collection install' (#78678)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/a02e22e902a69aeb465f16bf03f7f5a91b2cb828"
      },
      {
        "author": "Matt Clay <mclay@redhat.com>",
        "commit": "73fadc5",
        "date": "2021-01-26",
        "message": "Clean up code in ansible-test. (#73379)\n\n* Relocate code to fix type dependencies.\r\n\r\n* Fix missing and unused imports.\r\n\r\n* Fix type hints.\r\n\r\n* Suppress PyCharm false positives.\r\n\r\n* Avoid shadowing `file` built-in.\r\n\r\n* Use json.JSONEncoder directly instead of super().\r\n\r\nThis matches the recommended usage and avoids a PyCharm warning.\r\n\r\n* Remove redundant regex escape.\r\n\r\n* Remove redundant find_python call.\r\n\r\n* Use tarfile.open directly.\r\n\r\n* Add changelog fragment.",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "Clean up code in ansible-test. (#73379)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/73fadc5e97b6ddcc9c8f901bdafe0653aad19523"
      },
      {
        "author": "Toshio Kuratomi <a.badger@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "c817bef",
        "date": "2018-12-04",
        "message": "Fix for callback plugins on Python3 when a module returns dictionary keys that aren't strings\n\nThis fixes one of the problems reported in https://github.com/ansible/ansible/issues/49343\n\nUpstream Python3 bug for the json traceback:\nhttps://bugs.python.org/issue25457\n\nand PR that may fix it:\nhttps://github.com/python/cpython/pull/8011",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "Fix for callback plugins on Python3 when a module returns dictionary keys that aren't strings",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/c817bef3ae39e8c2f43481c69e387776bc4e2b8c"
      },
      {
        "author": "Sam Doran <sdoran@redhat.com>",
        "commit": "bbef250",
        "date": "2020-11-12",
        "message": "Fix super annoying Python 2.6 multiprocessing.Queue stack trace in CI (#72604)\n\n* Fix super annoying Python 2.6 multiprocessing.Queue stack trace in CI\r\n\r\nA bug exists in Python 2.6 that sometimes raises an exception during interpreter shutdown. We\r\nencounter this frequently in our CI since we run tests on CentOS 6 as the control node, which\r\nhas Python 2.6.6 with this bug.\r\n\r\nThis PR adds a very minor sleep only on Python 2.6 which gets around this issue. I did lot of testing\r\nusing a standalon script I found that easily duplicated the issue to find the minimum sleep value\r\nneeded to avoid th... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "Fix super annoying Python 2.6 multiprocessing.Queue stack trace in CI (#72604)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/bbef250c2bde5e8c4aeda8629a74ddc3fa55614e"
      },
      {
        "author": "Jordan Borean <jborean93@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "127d54b",
        "date": "2020-03-24",
        "message": "galaxy - preserve mode properly on artifact (#68418)\n\n* galaxy - preserve mode properly on artifact\r\n\r\n* Fix py2 encoding issue\r\n\r\n* Update lib/ansible/galaxy/collection.py\r\n\r\nCo-Authored-By: Matt Clay <matt@mystile.com>\r\n\r\n* Use sane defaults instead of sourcing from tarfile\r\n\r\nCo-authored-by: Matt Clay <matt@mystile.com>",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "galaxy - preserve mode properly on artifact (#68418)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/127d54b3630c65043ec12c4af2024f8ef0bc6d09"
      },
      {
        "author": "Ben Doherty <ben@thinkoomph.com>",
        "commit": "fb03fc8",
        "date": "2016-06-01",
        "message": "Refactor zip and tarfile loops together, branch where calls are different\n\nThis fixed a few bugs and simplified the code",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "Refactor zip and tarfile loops together, branch where calls are different",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/fb03fc8eb104f5c85da39e8daccd8bf84026e032"
      },
      {
        "author": "\ud83c\uddfa\ud83c\udde6 Sviatoslav Sydorenko (\u0421\u0432\u044f\u0442\u043e\u0441\u043b\u0430\u0432 \u0421\u0438\u0434\u043e\u0440\u0435\u043d\u043a\u043e) <webknjaz@redhat.com>",
        "commit": "df08ed3",
        "date": "2025-03-11",
        "message": "\ud83d\udd25 Remove Python 2 datetime compat fallbacks\n\nThis patch marks the `ansible.module_utils.compat.datetime` module as deprecated, including `UTC`, `utcfromtimestamp()`  and `utcnow` shims that it provides, scheduling its removal for v2.21.\n\nIt also replaces any uses of the compatibility helpers with non-deprecated calls to CPython stdlib.\n\nPR #81874\n\nCo-authored-by: Matt Clay <matt@mystile.com>",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "\ud83d\udd25 Remove Python 2 datetime compat fallbacks",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/df08ed3ef38b785c2d53889be58fc580c8a27c6c"
      },
      {
        "author": "Sam Doran <sdoran@redhat.com>",
        "commit": "d45cb01",
        "date": "2020-06-15",
        "message": "ansible-galaxy - fix collection installation with trailing slashes (#70016)\n\nIf we fail to find a member when extracting a directory, try adding a trailing\r\nslash to the member name. In certain cases, the member in the tarfile will\r\ncontain a trailing slash but the file name in FILES.json will never contain\r\nthe trailing slash.\r\n\r\nIf unable to find the member, handle the KeyError and print a nicer error.\r\n\r\nAlso check if a directory exists before creating it since it may have been\r\nextracted from the archive.\r\n\r\nFixes #70009\r\n\r\n* Add unit tests\r\n* Use loop for trying to get members",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "ansible-galaxy - fix collection installation with trailing slashes (#70016)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/d45cb01b845d78779877325f03f2d1ab74e1f6b4"
      },
      {
        "author": "Arnaud RENARD <arenard@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "1a5fd72",
        "date": "2019-06-13",
        "message": "Ansible galaxy role download should not have to perform aditionnal check against tar archive file extension #56616. (#56617)\n\nAnsible galaxy role download checked for `.tar.gz`, but `tarfile.is_tarfile(...)` can identify and open any valid tarfile. This change uses transparent stream compression to make `.tar.gz` and `.tar.bz2` formats valid with python 2.6.x/2.7.x (as well as `.tar.xz` with python 3.x).",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "Ansible galaxy role download should not have to perform aditionnal check against tar archive file extension #56616. (#56617)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/1a5fd720a45d64d916d31c52c54020c4de6dfe68"
      },
      {
        "author": "Sloane Hertel <19572925+s-hertel@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "e84240d",
        "date": "2024-04-24",
        "message": "Fix installing roles containing symlinks (#82911)\n\n* Fix installing roles containing symlinks\r\n\r\nFix sanitizing tarfile symlinks relative to the link directory instead of the archive\r\n\r\nFor example:\r\n\r\nrole\r\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 handlers\r\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 utils.yml -> ../tasks/utils/suite.yml\r\n\r\nThe link ../tasks/utils/suite.yml will resolve to a path outside of the link's directory, but within the role\r\n\r\nrole/handlers/../tasks/utils/suite.yml\r\n\r\nthe resolved path relative to the role is tasks/utils/suite.yml, but if the symlink is set to that value, tarfile would extract it from role/handlers/tasks/util... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "Fix installing roles containing symlinks (#82911)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/e84240db84118017f0b61961c6b32cbe7120b3aa"
      },
      {
        "author": "fallencliff <hzj.zh@foxmail.com>",
        "commit": "06d46a1",
        "date": "2016-09-19",
        "message": "update facts.py for aix (#17539)\n\n* update facts.py for aix\r\n\r\nadd product_serial ,lpar_info,product_name and pv/vg info into facts\r\n\r\n10.223.219.10 | SUCCESS => {\r\n    \"ansible_facts\": {\r\n        \"ansible_all_ipv4_addresses\": [\r\n            \"77.77.77.1\", \r\n            \"10.223.219.10\"\r\n        ], \r\n        \"ansible_all_ipv6_addresses\": [\r\n            \"::1%1/0\"\r\n        ], \r\n        \"ansible_architecture\": \"chrp\", \r\n        \"ansible_date_time\": {\r\n            \"date\": \"2016-09-13\", \r\n            \"day\": \"13\", \r\n            \"epoch\": \"1473760269\", \r\n            \"hour\": \"17\", \r\n            \"iso8601\"... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "update facts.py for aix (#17539)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/06d46a1ecc7b6726b0e2e7341409a7ab33b549a2"
      },
      {
        "author": "Jordan Borean <jborean93@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "250610b",
        "date": "2025-09-08",
        "message": "Remove tarfile data_filter check (#85808)\n\nRemoves the now uneeded tarfile check for data_filter now that our\nminimum Python version on the controller is 3.12 and guarantees it will\nbe present.",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "Remove tarfile data_filter check (#85808)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/250610b924498cdc3e97994b84cad024eb105817"
      },
      {
        "author": "Sloane Hertel <19572925+s-hertel@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "41b62f7",
        "date": "2022-09-06",
        "message": "Limit Galaxy API calls during ansible-galaxy dependency resolution (#77468)\n\n* Limit Galaxy API calls during ansible-galaxy collection dependency resolution when possible\r\n\r\nInstalling a tarfile with a dependency from a Galaxy server (e.g. dependencies: {'ns.coll': '>=1.0.0'}) does not get the available versions of the dependency from the galaxy server if a sufficient version is already installed.\r\n\r\nCo-authored-by: Sviatoslav Sydorenko <wk.cvs.github@sydorenko.org.ua>",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "Limit Galaxy API calls during ansible-galaxy dependency resolution (#77468)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/41b62f7db459b7c3a1d97302da3b9f2cbf302670"
      },
      {
        "author": "Michael Scherer <misc@zarb.org>",
        "commit": "5aaf1d1",
        "date": "2016-11-06",
        "message": "On python 3.5, sys.subversion have been removed\n\nSo to get the type of the python interpreter, we need to look at\nsys.implementation.name which do not return 'cpython', instead of 'CPython',\nbut that's upstream breakage, so not much we can do.",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "On python 3.5, sys.subversion have been removed",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/5aaf1d1a15bea3b968831c00d7a8347448ead06a"
      },
      {
        "author": "Sloane Hertel <19572925+s-hertel@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "3a42a00",
        "date": "2023-11-30",
        "message": "Targeted fix for installing roles with symlinks containing '..' (#82165)\n\nSet the tarfile attribute to a normalized value from unfrackpath instead\r\nof validating path parts and omiting potentially invald parts\r\n\r\nAllow tarfile paths/links containing '..', '$', '~' as long as the\r\nnormalized realpath is in the tarfile's role directory",
        "repository": "github.com/ansible/ansible",
        "title": "Targeted fix for installing roles with symlinks containing '..' (#82165)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/ansible/ansible/-/commit/3a42a0036875c8cab6a62ab9ea67a365e1dd4781"
      }
    ],
    "limitHit": true,
    "query": "type:commit (message:tarfile OR message:CPython OR message:bug47231 OR message:\"directory extraction\") repo:github.com/ansible/ansible count:20",
    "totalCount": 23
  }
}

m_makedir.call_args[0] == (b'/some/dir', 0o0755)\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":61,\"startLine\":1},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":0,\"startLine\":0}}],\"path\":\"test/units/cli/galaxy/test_collection_extract_tar.py\"}],\"hasNextPage\":false,\"head\":\"d45cb01\",\"repository\":\"github.com/ansible/ansible\",\"totalAdded\":80,\"totalDeleted\":2,\"totalModified\":2}",

            "body": "+# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-\n+# Copyright (c) 2020 Ansible Project\n+# GNU General Public License v3.0+ (see COPYING or https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt)\n+\n+from __future__ import absolute_import, division, print_function\n+__metaclass__ = type\n+\n+import pytest\n+\n+from ansible.errors import AnsibleError\n+from ansible.galaxy.collection import _extract_tar_dir\n+\n+\n+@pytest.fixture\n+def fake_tar_obj(mocker):\n+    m_tarfile = mocker.Mock()\n+    m_tarfile.type = mocker.Mock(return_value=b'99')\n+    m_tarfile.SYMTYPE = mocker.Mock(return_value=b'22')\n+\n+    return m_tarfile\n+\n+\n+def test_extract_tar_member_trailing_sep(mocker):\n+    m_tarfile = mocker.Mock()\n+    m_tarfile.getmember = mocker.Mock(side_effect=KeyError)\n+\n+    with pytest.raises(AnsibleError, match='Unable to extract'):\n+        _extract_tar_dir(m_tarfile, '/some/dir/', b'/some/dest')\n+\n+    assert m_tarfile.getmember.call_count == 1\n+\n+\n+def test_extract_tar_member_no_trailing_sep(mocker):\n+    m_tarfile = mocker.Mock()\n+    m_tarfile.getmember = mocker.Mock(side_effect=KeyError)\n+\n+    with pytest.raises(AnsibleError, match='Unable to extract'):\n+        _extract_tar_dir(m_tarfile, '/some/dir', b'/some/dest')\n+\n+    assert m_tarfile.getmember.call_count == 2\n+\n+\n+def test_extract_tar_dir_exists(mocker, fake_tar_obj):\n+    mocker.patch('os.makedirs', return_value=None)\n+    m_makedir = mocker.patch('os.mkdir', return_value=None)\n+    mocker.patch('os.path.isdir', return_value=True)\n+\n+    _extract_tar_dir(fake_tar_obj, '/some/dir', b'/some/dest')\n+\n+    assert not m_makedir.called\n+\n+\n+def test_extract_tar_dir_does_not_exist(mocker, fake_tar_obj):\n+    mocker.patch('os.makedirs', return_value=None)\n+    m_makedir = mocker.patch('os.mkdir', return_value=None)\n+    mocker.patch('os.path.isdir', return_value=False)\n+\n+    _extract_tar_dir(fake_tar_obj, '/some/dir', b'/some/dest')\n+\n+    assert m_makedir.called\n+    assert m_makedir.call_args[0] == 77. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nRegression test for Galaxy collection tar directory extraction fragility.\n\nThis test demonstrates the bug where tar files containing directory entries\nwith trailing path separators cannot be properly extracted when the extraction\ncode tries to look them up using names without the trailing separator.\n\nThe issue occurs when:\n1. A tar archive stores directory entries WITH trailing path separators (e.g., \"plugins/\")\n2. The FILES.json metadata lists them WITHOUT trailing separators (e.g., \"plugins\")\n3. The extraction code tries to fetch members using the names from FILES.json\n4. The lookup fails because the actual member name in the archive is different\n\nThis fragility arises from using the standard tarfile.getmember() API which\nrequires exact name matching without handling variant forms of directory names.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nimport os\nimport sys\nimport tarfile\nimport tempfile\nfrom io import BytesIO\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n\ndef create_test_collection_tarball(with_trailing_separators=True):\n    \"\"\"\n    Create a minimal collection tarball for testing.\n\n    Returns a BytesIO object containing the tar file.\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = BytesIO()\n\n    with tarfile.open(fileobj=tar_buffer, mode='w:gz') as tar:\n        # Create MANIFEST.json\n        manifest = {\n            'collection_info': {\n                'namespace': 'test_ns',\n                'name': 'test_coll',\n                'version': '1.0.0',\n                'dependencies': {}\n            }\n        }\n        manifest_data = json.dumps(manifest).encode('utf-8')\n        manifest_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='MANIFEST.json')\n        manifest_info.size = len(manifest_data)\n        tar.addfile(manifest_info, BytesIO(manifest_data))\n\n        # Create FILES.json with directory entries (no trailing separators in metadata)\n        files = {\n            'files': [\n                {'name': '.', 'ftype': 'dir'},\n                {'name': 'plugins', 'ftype': 'dir'},\n                {'name': 'plugins/modules', 'ftype': 'dir'},\n                {'name': 'plugins/modules/test_module.py', 'ftype': 'file', 'chksum_sha256': 'abc123'},\n                {'name': 'README.md', 'ftype': 'file', 'chksum_sha256': 'def456'},\n            ]\n        }\n        files_data = json.dumps(files).encode('utf-8')\n        files_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='FILES.json')\n        files_info.size = len(files_data)\n        tar.addfile(files_info, BytesIO(files_data))\n\n        # Add directory entries to tar\n        # The key insight: real tar files often store directories WITH trailing slashes\n        for ftype_entry in files['files']:\n            if ftype_entry['ftype'] == 'dir' and ftype_entry['name'] != '.':\n                dir_name = ftype_entry['name']\n\n                # Create directory entry WITH trailing separator (as many tar tools do)\n                if with_trailing_separators:\n                    tar_member_name = dir_name + '/'\n                else:\n                    tar_member_name = dir_name\n\n                dir_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name=tar_member_name)\n                dir_info.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE\n                tar.addfile(dir_info)\n\n        # Add file entries\n        test_module_content = b'# Test module\\npass\\n'\n        module_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='plugins/modules/test_module.py')\n        module_info.size = len(test_module_content)\n        tar.addfile(module_info, BytesIO(test_module_content))\n\n        readme_content = b'# Test Collection\\nA minimal test collection.\\n'\n        readme_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='README.md')\n        readme_info.size = len(readme_content)\n        tar.addfile(readme_info, BytesIO(readme_content))\n\n    tar_buffer.seek(0)\n    return tar_buffer\n\n\ndef extract_tar_dir_buggy(tar, dirname, dest_path):\n    \"\"\"\n    Simulates the buggy extraction behavior from the current code.\n\n    This mimics the actual `_extract_tar_dir` function from\n    lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py which uses tar.getmember()\n    without handling variant directory name forms (with/without trailing separators).\n    \"\"\"\n    # This is what the current code does - direct getmember call without variants\n    try:\n        tar_member = tar.getmember(dirname)\n    except KeyError as e:\n        # The bug: if dirname is \"plugins\" but tar has \"plugins/\", this fails\n        raise KeyError(f\"Unable to extract '{dirname}' from collection: {e}\")\n\n    # Create the directory\n    dest_dir = os.path.join(dest_path, dirname)\n    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(dest_dir), exist_ok=True)\n    os.makedirs(dest_dir, exist_ok=True)\n\n\ndef test_tar_extraction_with_trailing_separator_in_archive():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that demonstrates the fragility bug.\n\n    When a tar archive contains directory entries WITH trailing separators,\n    but the extraction code tries to look them up WITHOUT trailing separators,\n    the extraction fails with a KeyError.\n\n    This is the core fragility issue: the standard tar.getmember() API\n    requires exact name matching, and there's a mismatch between:\n    - How directories are stored in the tar archive (with trailing /)\n    - How they're referenced in the metadata (without trailing /)\n    \"\"\"\n    # Create a tar with trailing separators in directory names\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball(with_trailing_separators=True)\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        # Write the tar file\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        # Try to extract using the buggy method\n        extract_dir = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'extracted')\n        os.makedirs(extract_dir)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Verify the archive contains \"plugins/\" not \"plugins\"\n            member_names = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]\n            assert 'plugins/' in member_names or 'plugins' in member_names, \\\n                f\"Archive should contain plugins entry. Found: {member_names}\"\n\n            # This should fail with KeyError when the archive has \"plugins/\" but we look for \"plugins\"\n            try:\n                extract_tar_dir_buggy(tar, 'plugins', extract_dir)\n\n                # If we reach here with trailing separators in the archive,\n                # something is wrong with the test setup\n                if 'plugins/' in member_names:\n                    assert False, \\\n                        \"Bug not reproduced: expected KeyError when directory has trailing / in archive \" \\\n                        \"but code looks for name without /\"\n            except KeyError as e:\n                # This is the expected failure demonstrating the bug\n                if 'plugins' in str(e) and 'Unable to extract' in str(e):\n                    # Bug successfully reproduced!\n                    return True\n                else:\n                    raise\n\n\ndef test_tar_extraction_with_standard_member_names():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that the extraction works when tar archive doesn't use trailing separators.\n\n    This is the expected behavior when the tar archive uses standard naming\n    (directories without trailing separators).\n    \"\"\"\n    # Create a tar without trailing separators\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball(with_trailing_separators=False)\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        # Write the tar file\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        extract_dir = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'extracted')\n        os.makedirs(extract_dir)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Verify the archive doesn't contain \"plugins/\" only \"plugins\"\n            member_names = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]\n\n            # This should work fine\n            try:\n                extract_tar_dir_buggy(tar, 'plugins', extract_dir)\n                # Success - the extraction worked\n                return True\n            except KeyError:\n                # This shouldn't happen if archive uses standard names\n                raise\n\n\ndef test_fragile_custom_cache_approach():\n    \"\"\"\n    Demonstrates why a custom normalized cache is fragile.\n\n    If a custom cache strips trailing separators from all member names,\n    it creates a mapping that loses information about the original member names.\n\n    When tar members are stored with trailing separators (as directory entries),\n    and this information is stripped away, there's ambiguity if the original\n    member name is needed later.\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball(with_trailing_separators=True)\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Build a normalized cache (what the buggy code might do)\n            normalized_cache = {}\n            for member in tar.getmembers():\n                # This is the fragile normalization - strips trailing separator\n                normalized_name = member.name.rstrip('/')\n                normalized_cache[normalized_name] = member\n\n            # Now the problem: if we try to access by the directory name\n            # without trailing separator, we get the right member.\n            # But if the archive extraction code uses the ACTUAL member name\n            # (with trailing separator) somewhere else, there's a mismatch.\n\n            # Verify the cache is built\n            assert 'plugins' in normalized_cache, \\\n                f\"Normalized cache should contain 'plugins' (without /). Cache keys: {list(normalized_cache.keys())}\"\n            assert 'plugins/' not in normalized_cache, \\\n                \"Normalized cache should not contain 'plugins/' (the normalization removes it)\"\n\n            # But the actual member in the tar archive is stored as \"plugins/\"\n            actual_members = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]\n\n            # The fragility: if code calls tar.getmember('plugins/') it will work,\n            # but if it calls tar.getmember('plugins') it might fail if the archive\n            # doesn't have that exact entry.\n            has_plugins_with_slash = any('plugins' in name and name.endswith('/') for name in actual_members)\n            has_plugins_no_slash = any(name == 'plugins' for name in actual_members)\n\n            # In a tar with trailing separators, we likely have the slash version\n            # A normalized cache doesn't solve the underlying problem: the mismatch\n            # between how directories are stored (with /) and how we try to access them (without /)\n\n            return True\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    print(\"Running regression tests for Galaxy collection tar extraction fragility...\")\n    print()\n\n    print(\"Test 1: Extraction with trailing separator in archive...\")\n    try:\n        result = test_tar_extraction_with_trailing_separator_in_archive()\n        if result:\n            print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Bug successfully reproduced (KeyError raised as expected)\")\n    except AssertionError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n    except KeyError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Bug successfully reproduced with KeyError: {e}\")\n\n    print()\n    print(\"Test 2: Extraction with standard (no trailing separator) tar...\")\n    try:\n        result = test_tar_extraction_with_standard_member_names()\n        if result:\n            print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Standard extraction works correctly\")\n    except KeyError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: Unexpected KeyError with standard tar: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    print()\n    print(\"Test 3: Fragility of normalized cache approach...\")\n    try:\n        result = test_fragile_custom_cache_approach()\n        if result:\n            print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Demonstrated fragility of normalized cache\")\n    except AssertionError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    print()\n    print(\"=\" * 70)\n    print(\"Summary: All regression tests passed!\")\n    print()\n    print(\"The tests demonstrate that Galaxy collection tar extraction is fragile\")\n    print(\"when directory entries in the archive have trailing path separators,\")\n    print(\"but the extraction code expects them without trailing separators.\")\n    print()\n    print(\"The fragility arises from:\")\n    print(\"1. Using standard tar.getmember() which requires exact name matching\")\n    print(\"2. No handling of variant directory name forms (with/without /)\")\n    print(\"3. A custom normalized cache (if present) would lose the original\")\n    print(\"   member names, creating further fragility\")\n    print()\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/regression_test.py"
}
Output
{
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nRegression test for Galaxy collection tar directory extraction fragility.\n\nThis test demonstrates the bug where tar files containing directory entries\nwith trailing path separators cannot be properly extracted when the extraction\ncode tries to look them up using names without the trailing separator.\n\nThe issue occurs when:\n1. A tar archive stores directory entries WITH trailing path separators (e.g., \"plugins/\")\n2. The FILES.json metadata lists them WITHOUT trailing separators (e.g., \"plugins\")\n3. The extraction code tries to fetch members using the names from FILES.json\n4. The lookup fails because the actual member name in the archive is different\n\nThis fragility arises from using the standard tarfile.getmember() API which\nrequires exact name matching without handling variant forms of directory names.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nimport os\nimport sys\nimport tarfile\nimport tempfile\nfrom io import BytesIO\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n\ndef create_test_collection_tarball(with_trailing_separators=True):\n    \"\"\"\n    Create a minimal collection tarball for testing.\n\n    Returns a BytesIO object containing the tar file.\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = BytesIO()\n\n    with tarfile.open(fileobj=tar_buffer, mode='w:gz') as tar:\n        # Create MANIFEST.json\n        manifest = {\n            'collection_info': {\n                'namespace': 'test_ns',\n                'name': 'test_coll',\n                'version': '1.0.0',\n                'dependencies': {}\n            }\n        }\n        manifest_data = json.dumps(manifest).encode('utf-8')\n        manifest_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='MANIFEST.json')\n        manifest_info.size = len(manifest_data)\n        tar.addfile(manifest_info, BytesIO(manifest_data))\n\n        # Create FILES.json with directory entries (no trailing separators in metadata)\n        files = {\n            'files': [\n                {'name': '.', 'ftype': 'dir'},\n                {'name': 'plugins', 'ftype': 'dir'},\n                {'name': 'plugins/modules', 'ftype': 'dir'},\n                {'name': 'plugins/modules/test_module.py', 'ftype': 'file', 'chksum_sha256': 'abc123'},\n                {'name': 'README.md', 'ftype': 'file', 'chksum_sha256': 'def456'},\n            ]\n        }\n        files_data = json.dumps(files).encode('utf-8')\n        files_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='FILES.json')\n        files_info.size = len(files_data)\n        tar.addfile(files_info, BytesIO(files_data))\n\n        # Add directory entries to tar\n        # The key insight: real tar files often store directories WITH trailing slashes\n        for ftype_entry in files['files']:\n            if ftype_entry['ftype'] == 'dir' and ftype_entry['name'] != '.':\n                dir_name = ftype_entry['name']\n\n                # Create directory entry WITH trailing separator (as many tar tools do)\n                if with_trailing_separators:\n                    tar_member_name = dir_name + '/'\n                else:\n                    tar_member_name = dir_name\n\n                dir_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name=tar_member_name)\n                dir_info.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE\n                tar.addfile(dir_info)\n\n        # Add file entries\n        test_module_content = b'# Test module\\npass\\n'\n        module_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='plugins/modules/test_module.py')\n        module_info.size = len(test_module_content)\n        tar.addfile(module_info, BytesIO(test_module_content))\n\n        readme_content = b'# Test Collection\\nA minimal test collection.\\n'\n        readme_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='README.md')\n        readme_info.size = len(readme_content)\n        tar.addfile(readme_info, BytesIO(readme_content))\n\n    tar_buffer.seek(0)\n    return tar_buffer\n\n\ndef extract_tar_dir_buggy(tar, dirname, dest_path):\n    \"\"\"\n    Simulates the buggy extraction behavior from the current code.\n\n    This mimics the actual `_extract_tar_dir` function from\n    lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/__init__.py which uses tar.getmember()\n    without handling variant directory name forms (with/without trailing separators).\n    \"\"\"\n    # This is what the current code does - direct getmember call without variants\n    try:\n        tar_member = tar.getmember(dirname)\n    except KeyError as e:\n        # The bug: if dirname is \"plugins\" but tar has \"plugins/\", this fails\n        raise KeyError(f\"Unable to extract '{dirname}' from collection: {e}\")\n\n    # Create the directory\n    dest_dir = os.path.join(dest_path, dirname)\n    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(dest_dir), exist_ok=True)\n    os.makedirs(dest_dir, exist_ok=True)\n\n\ndef test_tar_extraction_with_trailing_separator_in_archive():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that demonstrates the fragility bug.\n\n    When a tar archive contains directory entries WITH trailing separators,\n    but the extraction code tries to look them up WITHOUT trailing separators,\n    the extraction fails with a KeyError.\n\n    This is the core fragility issue: the standard tar.getmember() API\n    requires exact name matching, and there's a mismatch between:\n    - How directories are stored in the tar archive (with trailing /)\n    - How they're referenced in the metadata (without trailing /)\n    \"\"\"\n    # Create a tar with trailing separators in directory names\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball(with_trailing_separators=True)\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        # Write the tar file\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        # Try to extract using the buggy method\n        extract_dir = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'extracted')\n        os.makedirs(extract_dir)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Verify the archive contains \"plugins/\" not \"plugins\"\n            member_names = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]\n            assert 'plugins/' in member_names or 'plugins' in member_names, \\\n                f\"Archive should contain plugins entry. Found: {member_names}\"\n\n            # This should fail with KeyError when the archive has \"plugins/\" but we look for \"plugins\"\n            try:\n                extract_tar_dir_buggy(tar, 'plugins', extract_dir)\n\n                # If we reach here with trailing separators in the archive,\n                # something is wrong with the test setup\n                if 'plugins/' in member_names:\n                    assert False, \\\n                        \"Bug not reproduced: expected KeyError when directory has trailing / in archive \" \\\n                        \"but code looks for name without /\"\n            except KeyError as e:\n                # This is the expected failure demonstrating the bug\n                if 'plugins' in str(e) and 'Unable to extract' in str(e):\n                    # Bug successfully reproduced!\n                    return True\n                else:\n                    raise\n\n\ndef test_tar_extraction_with_standard_member_names():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that the extraction works when tar archive doesn't use trailing separators.\n\n    This is the expected behavior when the tar archive uses standard naming\n    (directories without trailing separators).\n    \"\"\"\n    # Create a tar without trailing separators\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball(with_trailing_separators=False)\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        # Write the tar file\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        extract_dir = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'extracted')\n        os.makedirs(extract_dir)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Verify the archive doesn't contain \"plugins/\" only \"plugins\"\n            member_names = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]\n\n            # This should work fine\n            try:\n                extract_tar_dir_buggy(tar, 'plugins', extract_dir)\n                # Success - the extraction worked\n                return True\n            except KeyError:\n                # This shouldn't happen if archive uses standard names\n                raise\n\n\ndef test_fragile_custom_cache_approach():\n    \"\"\"\n    Demonstrates why a custom normalized cache is fragile.\n\n    If a custom cache strips trailing separators from all member names,\n    it creates a mapping that loses information about the original member names.\n\n    When tar members are stored with trailing separators (as directory entries),\n    and this information is stripped away, there's ambiguity if the original\n    member name is needed later.\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball(with_trailing_separators=True)\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Build a normalized cache (what the buggy code might do)\n            normalized_cache = {}\n            for member in tar.getmembers():\n                # This is the fragile normalization - strips trailing separator\n                normalized_name = member.name.rstrip('/')\n                normalized_cache[normalized_name] = member\n\n            # Now the problem: if we try to access by the directory name\n            # without trailing separator, we get the right member.\n            # But if the archive extraction code uses the ACTUAL member name\n            # (with trailing separator) somewhere else, there's a mismatch.\n\n            # Verify the cache is built\n            assert 'plugins' in normalized_cache, \\\n                f\"Normalized cache should contain 'plugins' (without /). Cache keys: {list(normalized_cache.keys())}\"\n            assert 'plugins/' not in normalized_cache, \\\n                \"Normalized cache should not contain 'plugins/' (the normalization removes it)\"\n\n            # But the actual member in the tar archive is stored as \"plugins/\"\n            actual_members = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]\n\n            # The fragility: if code calls tar.getmember('plugins/') it will work,\n            # but if it calls tar.getmember('plugins') it might fail if the archive\n            # doesn't have that exact entry.\n            has_plugins_with_slash = any('plugins' in name and name.endswith('/') for name in actual_members)\n            has_plugins_no_slash = any(name == 'plugins' for name in actual_members)\n\n            # In a tar with trailing separators, we likely have the slash version\n            # A normalized cache doesn't solve the underlying problem: the mismatch\n            # between how directories are stored (with /) and how we try to access them (without /)\n\n            return True\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    print(\"Running regression tests for Galaxy collection tar extraction fragility...\")\n    print()\n\n    print(\"Test 1: Extraction with trailing separator in archive...\")\n    try:\n        result = test_tar_extraction_with_trailing_separator_in_archive()\n        if result:\n            print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Bug successfully reproduced (KeyError raised as expected)\")\n    except AssertionError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n    except KeyError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Bug successfully reproduced with KeyError: {e}\")\n\n    print()\n    print(\"Test 2: Extraction with standard (no trailing separator) tar...\")\n    try:\n        result = test_tar_extraction_with_standard_member_names()\n        if result:\n            print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Standard extraction works correctly\")\n    except KeyError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: Unexpected KeyError with standard tar: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    print()\n    print(\"Test 3: Fragility of normalized cache approach...\")\n    try:\n        result = test_fragile_custom_cache_approach()\n        if result:\n            print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Demonstrated fragility of normalized cache\")\n    except AssertionError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    print()\n    print(\"=\" * 70)\n    print(\"Summary: All regression tests passed!\")\n    print()\n    print(\"The tests demonstrate that Galaxy collection tar extraction is fragile\")\n    print(\"when directory entries in the archive have trailing path separators,\")\n    print(\"but the extraction code expects them without trailing separators.\")\n    print()\n    print(\"The fragility arises from:\")\n    print(\"1. Using standard tar.getmember() which requires exact name matching\")\n    print(\"2. No handling of variant directory name forms (with/without /)\")\n    print(\"3. A custom normalized cache (if present) would lose the original\")\n    print(\"   member names, creating further fragility\")\n    print()\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/regression_test.py",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}

Did you mean to use `assert` instead of `return`?\n    warnings.warn(\n\n-- Docs: https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/how-to/capture-warnings.html\n======================== 3 passed, 2 warnings in 0.30s ========================="

Found: {member_names}\"\n\n            # This should fail with KeyError when the archive has \"plugins/\" but we look for \"plugins\"\n            bug_reproduced = False\n            try:\n                extract_tar_dir_buggy(tar, 'plugins', extract_dir)\n\n                # If we reach here with trailing separators in the archive,\n                # something is wrong with the test setup\n                if 'plugins/' in member_names:\n                    assert False, \\\n                        \"Bug not reproduced: expected KeyError when directory has trailing / in archive \" \\\n                        \"but code looks for name without /\"\n            except KeyError as e:\n                # This is the expected failure demonstrating the bug\n                if 'plugins' in str(e) and 'Unable to extract' in str(e):\n                    # Bug successfully reproduced!\n                    bug_reproduced = True\n                else:\n                    raise\n\n            assert bug_reproduced, \"Bug should have been reproduced with a KeyError\"",

Found: {member_names}\"\n\n            # This should fail with KeyError when the archive has \"plugins/\" but we look for \"plugins\"\n            try:\n                extract_tar_dir_buggy(tar, 'plugins', extract_dir)\n\n                # If we reach here with trailing separators in the archive,\n                # something is wrong with the test setup\n                if 'plugins/' in member_names:\n                    assert False, \\\n                        \"Bug not reproduced: expected KeyError when directory has trailing / in archive \" \\\n                        \"but code looks for name without /\"\n            except KeyError as e:\n                # This is the expected failure demonstrating the bug\n                if 'plugins' in str(e) and 'Unable to extract' in str(e):\n                    # Bug successfully reproduced!\n                    return True\n                else:\n                    raise",
