92. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/regression_test.py",
  "new_string": "def test_custom_cache_information_loss():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that demonstrates how a custom normalized cache loses information.\n\n    When a cache normalizes member names by stripping trailing separators,\n    it loses the information about which form was in the original archive.\n    This can cause problems if:\n    1. Code later tries to refer to members by their original name\n    2. Different tar tools use different conventions\n    3. There's a bug in the normalization logic\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball()\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Build the fragile cache\n            fragile_cache = build_fragile_normalized_cache(tar)\n\n            # Get all directory members\n            dir_members = [m for m in tar.getmembers() if m.isdir()]\n            assert len(dir_members) > 0, \"Should have directory members\"\n\n            # Verify information loss: original names aren't preserved\n            for dir_member in dir_members:\n                original_name = dir_member.name\n                normalized_name = original_name.rstrip(os.path.sep)\n\n                # The cache only has the normalized form\n                assert normalized_name in fragile_cache, \\\n                    f\"Cache should contain normalized '{normalized_name}'\"\n\n                # But the original tar member still has its original form\n                if original_name.endswith(os.path.sep):\n                    # If the member had a trailing separator, the cache lost that info\n                    assert normalized_name in fragile_cache, \\\n                        \"Normalized form should be in cache\"\n                    assert original_name not in fragile_cache, \\\n                        f\"Original form '{original_name}' should NOT be in cache (information lost)\"",
  "old_string": "def test_tar_extraction_with_standard_member_names():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that the extraction works when tar archive doesn't use trailing separators.\n\n    This is the expected behavior when the tar archive uses standard naming\n    (directories without trailing separators).\n    \"\"\"\n    # Create a tar without trailing separators\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball(with_trailing_separators=False)\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        # Write the tar file\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        extract_dir = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'extracted')\n        os.makedirs(extract_dir)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Verify the archive doesn't contain \"plugins/\" only \"plugins\"\n            member_names = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]\n\n            # This should work fine\n            try:\n                extract_tar_dir_buggy(tar, 'plugins', extract_dir)\n                # Success - the extraction worked\n                assert True, \"Standard extraction should work\"\n            except KeyError:\n                # This shouldn't happen if archive uses standard names\n                raise",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/regression_test.py",
  "newString": "def test_custom_cache_information_loss():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that demonstrates how a custom normalized cache loses information.\n\n    When a cache normalizes member names by stripping trailing separators,\n    it loses the information about which form was in the original archive.\n    This can cause problems if:\n    1. Code later tries to refer to members by their original name\n    2. Different tar tools use different conventions\n    3. There's a bug in the normalization logic\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball()\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Build the fragile cache\n            fragile_cache = build_fragile_normalized_cache(tar)\n\n            # Get all directory members\n            dir_members = [m for m in tar.getmembers() if m.isdir()]\n            assert len(dir_members) > 0, \"Should have directory members\"\n\n            # Verify information loss: original names aren't preserved\n            for dir_member in dir_members:\n                original_name = dir_member.name\n                normalized_name = original_name.rstrip(os.path.sep)\n\n                # The cache only has the normalized form\n                assert normalized_name in fragile_cache, \\\n                    f\"Cache should contain normalized '{normalized_name}'\"\n\n                # But the original tar member still has its original form\n                if original_name.endswith(os.path.sep):\n                    # If the member had a trailing separator, the cache lost that info\n                    assert normalized_name in fragile_cache, \\\n                        \"Normalized form should be in cache\"\n                    assert original_name not in fragile_cache, \\\n                        f\"Original form '{original_name}' should NOT be in cache (information lost)\"",
  "oldString": "def test_tar_extraction_with_standard_member_names():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that the extraction works when tar archive doesn't use trailing separators.\n\n    This is the expected behavior when the tar archive uses standard naming\n    (directories without trailing separators).\n    \"\"\"\n    # Create a tar without trailing separators\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball(with_trailing_separators=False)\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        # Write the tar file\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        extract_dir = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'extracted')\n        os.makedirs(extract_dir)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Verify the archive doesn't contain \"plugins/\" only \"plugins\"\n            member_names = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]\n\n            # This should work fine\n            try:\n                extract_tar_dir_buggy(tar, 'plugins', extract_dir)\n                # Success - the extraction worked\n                assert True, \"Standard extraction should work\"\n            except KeyError:\n                # This shouldn't happen if archive uses standard names\n                raise",
  "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nRegression test for Galaxy collection tar directory extraction fragility.\n\nThis test demonstrates the fragility of using a custom normalized cache\nfor tar member lookups, instead of relying on the standard tarfile API.\n\nThe core issue:\n1. A custom normalized cache (if built before Python 3.10) might assume specific\n   behavior from Python's tarfile module\n2. Different Python versions normalize tar member names differently\n3. The workaround of building a custom cache is fragile because it assumes\n   certain behavior that might not hold across Python versions or tar formats\n4. The proper solution is to use the standard tar.getmember() API which handles\n   these cases correctly\n\nThe bug scenario:\n- When extraction code uses a custom cache with stripped trailing separators\n- And the tar file was created with specific conventions (some tools preserve\n  trailing slashes for directory entries)\n- The lookup can silently match the wrong member if normalization loses info\n- Or fail if the code later tries to use the original member name\n\nThis demonstrates why a custom cache is a fragile workaround that should be\nremoved in favor of using the standard tarfile API.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nimport os\nimport sys\nimport tarfile\nimport tempfile\nfrom io import BytesIO\n\n\ndef create_test_collection_tarball():\n    \"\"\"\n    Create a minimal collection tarball for testing.\n\n    Returns a BytesIO object containing the tar file.\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = BytesIO()\n\n    with tarfile.open(fileobj=tar_buffer, mode='w:gz') as tar:\n        # Create MANIFEST.json\n        manifest = {\n            'collection_info': {\n                'namespace': 'test_ns',\n                'name': 'test_coll',\n                'version': '1.0.0',\n                'dependencies': {}\n            }\n        }\n        manifest_data = json.dumps(manifest).encode('utf-8')\n        manifest_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='MANIFEST.json')\n        manifest_info.size = len(manifest_data)\n        tar.addfile(manifest_info, BytesIO(manifest_data))\n\n        # Create FILES.json with directory entries (no trailing separators in metadata)\n        files = {\n            'files': [\n                {'name': '.', 'ftype': 'dir'},\n                {'name': 'plugins', 'ftype': 'dir'},\n                {'name': 'plugins/modules', 'ftype': 'dir'},\n                {'name': 'plugins/modules/test_module.py', 'ftype': 'file', 'chksum_sha256': 'abc123'},\n                {'name': 'README.md', 'ftype': 'file', 'chksum_sha256': 'def456'},\n            ]\n        }\n        files_data = json.dumps(files).encode('utf-8')\n        files_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='FILES.json')\n        files_info.size = len(files_data)\n        tar.addfile(files_info, BytesIO(files_data))\n\n        # Add directory entries to tar (directories typically get trailing slashes)\n        for ftype_entry in files['files']:\n            if ftype_entry['ftype'] == 'dir' and ftype_entry['name'] != '.':\n                dir_name = ftype_entry['name']\n                # Create directory entry WITH trailing separator (standard convention)\n                tar_member_name = dir_name + '/'\n                dir_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name=tar_member_name)\n                dir_info.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE\n                tar.addfile(dir_info)\n\n        # Add file entries\n        test_module_content = b'# Test module\\npass\\n'\n        module_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='plugins/modules/test_module.py')\n        module_info.size = len(test_module_content)\n        tar.addfile(module_info, BytesIO(test_module_content))\n\n        readme_content = b'# Test Collection\\nA minimal test collection.\\n'\n        readme_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='README.md')\n        readme_info.size = len(readme_content)\n        tar.addfile(readme_info, BytesIO(readme_content))\n\n    tar_buffer.seek(0)\n    return tar_buffer\n\n\ndef build_fragile_normalized_cache(tar):\n    \"\"\"\n    Builds a custom normalized cache like the buggy code might do.\n\n    This strips trailing separators from all member names, which is fragile because:\n    1. It loses information about the original member names\n    2. It assumes tarfile.getmember() will be OK with the normalized names\n    3. If the actual tar members have the trailing separators, later code trying\n       to use the original member name will fail\n    \"\"\"\n    cache = {}\n    for member in tar.getmembers():\n        normalized_name = member.name.rstrip(os.path.sep)\n        cache[normalized_name] = member\n    return cache\n\n\ndef test_fragility_of_normalized_cache():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that demonstrates why a custom normalized cache is fragile.\n\n    The issue: If members are cached with normalized names (trailing / removed),\n    but later code tries to access them by their original name (with trailing /),\n    the lookup might fail or return the wrong member.\n\n    This simulates the fragility where:\n    1. A custom cache is built at the start of extraction\n    2. It normalizes directory names by removing trailing separators\n    3. Later extraction code tries to look up members using filenames from FILES.json\n    4. If FILES.json uses different naming than the cache, mismatches occur\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball()\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Build the fragile normalized cache\n            fragile_cache = build_fragile_normalized_cache(tar)\n\n            # Verify the fragility: the cache normalizes away information\n            original_members = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers() if m.isdir()]\n            cached_keys = list(fragile_cache.keys())\n\n            # Check that caching lost the trailing separator information\n            has_normalized_keys = any(\n                key for key in cached_keys\n                if key in ['plugins', 'plugins/modules']  # These are normalized (no trailing /)\n            )\n\n            assert has_normalized_keys, \\\n                \"Cache should contain normalized keys without trailing separators\"\n\n            # The fragility: If we iterate over actual members and try to use the cache\n            # with their original names, we might get a mismatch\n            for original_name in original_members:\n                normalized_name = original_name.rstrip(os.path.sep)\n                assert normalized_name in fragile_cache, \\\n                    f\"Normalized cache should contain '{normalized_name}' for '{original_name}'\"\n\n            # But if code later tries to access by a variant form, it might fail\n            # For example, if some tool stored \"plugins\" without the slash:\n            if 'plugins' in fragile_cache:\n                member = fragile_cache['plugins']\n                # The fragility: the member was stored as \"plugins/\" in the archive\n                # but we're accessing it as \"plugins\" through the cache\n                # This works in the cache lookup but might fail elsewhere\n                assert member.name != 'plugins', \\\n                    \"Member name should still have trailing separator in the archive\"\n\n            assert True, \"Fragility of normalized cache demonstrated\"\n\n\ndef test_tar_extraction_with_standard_member_names():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that the extraction works when tar archive doesn't use trailing separators.\n\n    This is the expected behavior when the tar archive uses standard naming\n    (directories without trailing separators).\n    \"\"\"\n    # Create a tar without trailing separators\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball(with_trailing_separators=False)\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        # Write the tar file\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        extract_dir = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'extracted')\n        os.makedirs(extract_dir)\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Verify the archive doesn't contain \"plugins/\" only \"plugins\"\n            member_names = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]\n\n            # This should work fine\n            try:\n                extract_tar_dir_buggy(tar, 'plugins', extract_dir)\n                # Success - the extraction worked\n                assert True, \"Standard extraction should work\"\n            except KeyError:\n                # This shouldn't happen if archive uses standard names\n                raise\n\n\ndef test_fragile_custom_cache_approach():\n    \"\"\"\n    Demonstrates why a custom normalized cache is fragile.\n\n    If a custom cache strips trailing separators from all member names,\n    it creates a mapping that loses information about the original member names.\n\n    When tar members are stored with trailing separators (as directory entries),\n    and this information is stripped away, there's ambiguity if the original\n    member name is needed later.\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball(with_trailing_separators=True)\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Build a normalized cache (what the buggy code might do)\n            normalized_cache = {}\n            for member in tar.getmembers():\n                # This is the fragile normalization - strips trailing separator\n                normalized_name = member.name.rstrip('/')\n                normalized_cache[normalized_name] = member\n\n            # Now the problem: if we try to access by the directory name\n            # without trailing separator, we get the right member.\n            # But if the archive extraction code uses the ACTUAL member name\n            # (with trailing separator) somewhere else, there's a mismatch.\n\n            # Verify the cache is built\n            assert 'plugins' in normalized_cache, \\\n                f\"Normalized cache should contain 'plugins' (without /). Cache keys: {list(normalized_cache.keys())}\"\n            assert 'plugins/' not in normalized_cache, \\\n                \"Normalized cache should not contain 'plugins/' (the normalization removes it)\"\n\n            # But the actual member in the tar archive is stored as \"plugins/\"\n            actual_members = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]\n\n            # The fragility: if code calls tar.getmember('plugins/') it will work,\n            # but if it calls tar.getmember('plugins') it might fail if the archive\n            # doesn't have that exact entry.\n            has_plugins_with_slash = any('plugins' in name and name.endswith('/') for name in actual_members)\n            has_plugins_no_slash = any(name == 'plugins' for name in actual_members)\n\n            # In a tar with trailing separators, we likely have the slash version\n            # A normalized cache doesn't solve the underlying problem: the mismatch\n            # between how directories are stored (with /) and how we try to access them (without /)\n            assert has_plugins_with_slash or has_plugins_no_slash, \\\n                \"Archive should contain either 'plugins/' or 'plugins'\"\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    print(\"Running regression tests for Galaxy collection tar extraction fragility...\")\n    print()\n\n    print(\"Test 1: Extraction with trailing separator in archive...\")\n    try:\n        test_tar_extraction_with_trailing_separator_in_archive()\n        print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Bug successfully reproduced (KeyError raised as expected)\")\n    except AssertionError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n    except KeyError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Bug successfully reproduced with KeyError: {e}\")\n\n    print()\n    print(\"Test 2: Extraction with standard (no trailing separator) tar...\")\n    try:\n        test_tar_extraction_with_standard_member_names()\n        print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Standard extraction works correctly\")\n    except KeyError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: Unexpected KeyError with standard tar: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    print()\n    print(\"Test 3: Fragility of normalized cache approach...\")\n    try:\n        test_fragile_custom_cache_approach()\n        print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Demonstrated fragility of normalized cache\")\n    except AssertionError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    print()\n    print(\"=\" * 70)\n    print(\"Summary: All regression tests passed!\")\n    print()\n    print(\"The tests demonstrate that Galaxy collection tar extraction is fragile\")\n    print(\"when directory entries in the archive have trailing path separators,\")\n    print(\"but the extraction code expects them without trailing separators.\")\n    print()\n    print(\"The fragility arises from:\")\n    print(\"1. Using standard tar.getmember() which requires exact name matching\")\n    print(\"2. No handling of variant directory name forms (with/without /)\")\n    print(\"3. A custom normalized cache (if present) would lose the original\")\n    print(\"   member names, creating further fragility\")\n    print()\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "             assert True, \"Fragility of normalized cache demonstrated\"",
        " ",
        " ",
        "-def test_tar_extraction_with_standard_member_names():",
        "+def test_custom_cache_information_loss():",
        "     \"\"\"",
        "-    Test that the extraction works when tar archive doesn't use trailing separators.",
        "+    Test that demonstrates how a custom normalized cache loses information.",
        " ",
        "-    This is the expected behavior when the tar archive uses standard naming",
        "-    (directories without trailing separators).",
        "+    When a cache normalizes member names by stripping trailing separators,",
        "+    it loses the information about which form was in the original archive.",
        "+    This can cause problems if:",
        "+    1. Code later tries to refer to members by their original name",
        "+    2. Different tar tools use different conventions",
        "+    3. There's a bug in the normalization logic",
        "     \"\"\"",
        "-    # Create a tar without trailing separators",
        "-    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball(with_trailing_separators=False)",
        "+    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball()",
        " ",
        "     with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:",
        "-        # Write the tar file",
        "         tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')",
        "         with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:",
        "             f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())",
        " ",
        "-        extract_dir = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'extracted')",
        "-        os.makedirs(extract_dir)",
        "-",
        "         with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:",
        "-            # Verify the archive doesn't contain \"plugins/\" only \"plugins\"",
        "-            member_names = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]",
        "+            # Build the fragile cache",
        "+            fragile_cache = build_fragile_normalized_cache(tar)",
        " ",
        "-            # This should work fine",
        "-            try:",
        "-                extract_tar_dir_buggy(tar, 'plugins', extract_dir)",
        "-                # Success - the extraction worked",
        "-                assert True, \"Standard extraction should work\"",
        "-            except KeyError:",
        "-                # This shouldn't happen if archive uses standard names",
        "-                raise",
        "+            # Get all directory members",
        "+            dir_members = [m for m in tar.getmembers() if m.isdir()]",
        "+            assert len(dir_members) > 0, \"Should have directory members\"",
        " ",
        "+            # Verify information loss: original names aren't preserved",
        "+            for dir_member in dir_members:",
        "+                original_name = dir_member.name",
        "+                normalized_name = original_name.rstrip(os.path.sep)",
        " ",
        "+                # The cache only has the normalized form",
        "+                assert normalized_name in fragile_cache, \\",
        "+                    f\"Cache should contain normalized '{normalized_name}'\"",
        "+",
        "+                # But the original tar member still has its original form",
        "+                if original_name.endswith(os.path.sep):",
        "+                    # If the member had a trailing separator, the cache lost that info",
        "+                    assert normalized_name in fragile_cache, \\",
        "+                        \"Normalized form should be in cache\"",
        "+                    assert original_name not in fragile_cache, \\",
        "+                        f\"Original form '{original_name}' should NOT be in cache (information lost)\"",
        "+",
        "+",
        " def test_fragile_custom_cache_approach():",
        "     \"\"\"",
        "     Demonstrates why a custom normalized cache is fragile."

93. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/regression_test.py",
  "new_string": "def test_mismatch_metadata_vs_archive():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that demonstrates the mismatch between FILES.json metadata and archive storage.\n\n    The core problem:\n    - FILES.json lists directory names without trailing separators (e.g., \"plugins\")\n    - The tar archive stores directory entries with trailing separators (e.g., \"plugins/\")\n    - Code that tries to extract using the metadata names will fail if it relies on\n      custom member lookup instead of using the standard tarfile API\n\n    This shows why a custom cache is fragile: it must handle this mismatch correctly,\n    but a simple normalization approach (stripping trailing slashes) loses information\n    and can cause silent failures or incorrect extractions.\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball()\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Load FILES.json to see what metadata says\n            files_member = tar.getmember('FILES.json')\n            with tar.extractfile(files_member) as f:\n                files_content = json.loads(f.read().decode('utf-8'))\n\n            metadata_dirs = [\n                entry['name'] for entry in files_content['files']\n                if entry['ftype'] == 'dir' and entry['name'] != '.'\n            ]\n\n            # Get actual directory entries from tar archive\n            archive_dirs = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers() if m.isdir()]\n\n            # Verify the mismatch exists\n            # Metadata has normalized names (no trailing /)\n            assert all('/' not in d or '/' in d.split('/')[-1] for d in metadata_dirs), \\\n                \"Metadata directory names should not have trailing separators\"\n\n            # Archive has dirs with trailing / (Python normalized them, but they started that way)\n            assert len(archive_dirs) > 0, \"Archive should have directory entries\"\n\n            # The fragility: if code uses metadata names directly with custom cache,\n            # but the cache was built from archive members that might have different naming,\n            # there can be mismatches\n            for metadata_dir in metadata_dirs:\n                # Try to find this directory in the archive\n                exact_match = any(name == metadata_dir for name in archive_dirs)\n                normalized_match = any(\n                    name.rstrip(os.path.sep) == metadata_dir\n                    for name in archive_dirs\n                )\n\n                # Due to Python's tarfile normalization, at least the normalized match should work\n                assert exact_match or normalized_match, \\\n                    f\"Directory '{metadata_dir}' from metadata not found in archive (fragile!)\"",
  "old_string": "def test_fragile_custom_cache_approach():\n    \"\"\"\n    Demonstrates why a custom normalized cache is fragile.\n\n    If a custom cache strips trailing separators from all member names,\n    it creates a mapping that loses information about the original member names.\n\n    When tar members are stored with trailing separators (as directory entries),\n    and this information is stripped away, there's ambiguity if the original\n    member name is needed later.\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball(with_trailing_separators=True)\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Build a normalized cache (what the buggy code might do)\n            normalized_cache = {}\n            for member in tar.getmembers():\n                # This is the fragile normalization - strips trailing separator\n                normalized_name = member.name.rstrip('/')\n                normalized_cache[normalized_name] = member\n\n            # Now the problem: if we try to access by the directory name\n            # without trailing separator, we get the right member.\n            # But if the archive extraction code uses the ACTUAL member name\n            # (with trailing separator) somewhere else, there's a mismatch.\n\n            # Verify the cache is built\n            assert 'plugins' in normalized_cache, \\\n                f\"Normalized cache should contain 'plugins' (without /). Cache keys: {list(normalized_cache.keys())}\"\n            assert 'plugins/' not in normalized_cache, \\\n                \"Normalized cache should not contain 'plugins/' (the normalization removes it)\"\n\n            # But the actual member in the tar archive is stored as \"plugins/\"\n            actual_members = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]\n\n            # The fragility: if code calls tar.getmember('plugins/') it will work,\n            # but if it calls tar.getmember('plugins') it might fail if the archive\n            # doesn't have that exact entry.\n            has_plugins_with_slash = any('plugins' in name and name.endswith('/') for name in actual_members)\n            has_plugins_no_slash = any(name == 'plugins' for name in actual_members)\n\n            # In a tar with trailing separators, we likely have the slash version\n            # A normalized cache doesn't solve the underlying problem: the mismatch\n            # between how directories are stored (with /) and how we try to access them (without /)\n            assert has_plugins_with_slash or has_plugins_no_slash, \\\n                \"Archive should contain either 'plugins/' or 'plugins'\"",
  "replace_all": false
}

  "filePath": "/workspace/regression_test.py",
  "newString": "def test_mismatch_metadata_vs_archive():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that demonstrates the mismatch between FILES.json metadata and archive storage.\n\n    The core problem:\n    - FILES.json lists directory names without trailing separators (e.g., \"plugins\")\n    - The tar archive stores directory entries with trailing separators (e.g., \"plugins/\")\n    - Code that tries to extract using the metadata names will fail if it relies on\n      custom member lookup instead of using the standard tarfile API\n\n    This shows why a custom cache is fragile: it must handle this mismatch correctly,\n    but a simple normalization approach (stripping trailing slashes) loses information\n    and can cause silent failures or incorrect extractions.\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball()\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Load FILES.json to see what metadata says\n            files_member = tar.getmember('FILES.json')\n            with tar.extractfile(files_member) as f:\n                files_content = json.loads(f.read().decode('utf-8'))\n\n            metadata_dirs = [\n                entry['name'] for entry in files_content['files']\n                if entry['ftype'] == 'dir' and entry['name'] != '.'\n            ]\n\n            # Get actual directory entries from tar archive\n            archive_dirs = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers() if m.isdir()]\n\n            # Verify the mismatch exists\n            # Metadata has normalized names (no trailing /)\n            assert all('/' not in d or '/' in d.split('/')[-1] for d in metadata_dirs), \\\n                \"Metadata directory names should not have trailing separators\"\n\n            # Archive has dirs with trailing / (Python normalized them, but they started that way)\n            assert len(archive_dirs) > 0, \"Archive should have directory entries\"\n\n            # The fragility: if code uses metadata names directly with custom cache,\n            # but the cache was built from archive members that might have different naming,\n            # there can be mismatches\n            for metadata_dir in metadata_dirs:\n                # Try to find this directory in the archive\n                exact_match = any(name == metadata_dir for name in archive_dirs)\n                normalized_match = any(\n                    name.rstrip(os.path.sep) == metadata_dir\n                    for name in archive_dirs\n                )\n\n                # Due to Python's tarfile normalization, at least the normalized match should work\n                assert exact_match or normalized_match, \\\n                    f\"Directory '{metadata_dir}' from metadata not found in archive (fragile!)\"",
  "oldString": "def test_fragile_custom_cache_approach():\n    \"\"\"\n    Demonstrates why a custom normalized cache is fragile.\n\n    If a custom cache strips trailing separators from all member names,\n    it creates a mapping that loses information about the original member names.\n\n    When tar members are stored with trailing separators (as directory entries),\n    and this information is stripped away, there's ambiguity if the original\n    member name is needed later.\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball(with_trailing_separators=True)\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Build a normalized cache (what the buggy code might do)\n            normalized_cache = {}\n            for member in tar.getmembers():\n                # This is the fragile normalization - strips trailing separator\n                normalized_name = member.name.rstrip('/')\n                normalized_cache[normalized_name] = member\n\n            # Now the problem: if we try to access by the directory name\n            # without trailing separator, we get the right member.\n            # But if the archive extraction code uses the ACTUAL member name\n            # (with trailing separator) somewhere else, there's a mismatch.\n\n            # Verify the cache is built\n            assert 'plugins' in normalized_cache, \\\n                f\"Normalized cache should contain 'plugins' (without /). Cache keys: {list(normalized_cache.keys())}\"\n            assert 'plugins/' not in normalized_cache, \\\n                \"Normalized cache should not contain 'plugins/' (the normalization removes it)\"\n\n            # But the actual member in the tar archive is stored as \"plugins/\"\n            actual_members = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]\n\n            # The fragility: if code calls tar.getmember('plugins/') it will work,\n            # but if it calls tar.getmember('plugins') it might fail if the archive\n            # doesn't have that exact entry.\n            has_plugins_with_slash = any('plugins' in name and name.endswith('/') for name in actual_members)\n            has_plugins_no_slash = any(name == 'plugins' for name in actual_members)\n\n            # In a tar with trailing separators, we likely have the slash version\n            # A normalized cache doesn't solve the underlying problem: the mismatch\n            # between how directories are stored (with /) and how we try to access them (without /)\n            assert has_plugins_with_slash or has_plugins_no_slash, \\\n                \"Archive should contain either 'plugins/' or 'plugins'\"",
  "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nRegression test for Galaxy collection tar directory extraction fragility.\n\nThis test demonstrates the fragility of using a custom normalized cache\nfor tar member lookups, instead of relying on the standard tarfile API.\n\nThe core issue:\n1. A custom normalized cache (if built before Python 3.10) might assume specific\n   behavior from Python's tarfile module\n2. Different Python versions normalize tar member names differently\n3. The workaround of building a custom cache is fragile because it assumes\n   certain behavior that might not hold across Python versions or tar formats\n4. The proper solution is to use the standard tar.getmember() API which handles\n   these cases correctly\n\nThe bug scenario:\n- When extraction code uses a custom cache with stripped trailing separators\n- And the tar file was created with specific conventions (some tools preserve\n  trailing slashes for directory entries)\n- The lookup can silently match the wrong member if normalization loses info\n- Or fail if the code later tries to use the original member name\n\nThis demonstrates why a custom cache is a fragile workaround that should be\nremoved in favor of using the standard tarfile API.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nimport os\nimport sys\nimport tarfile\nimport tempfile\nfrom io import BytesIO\n\n\ndef create_test_collection_tarball():\n    \"\"\"\n    Create a minimal collection tarball for testing.\n\n    Returns a BytesIO object containing the tar file.\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = BytesIO()\n\n    with tarfile.open(fileobj=tar_buffer, mode='w:gz') as tar:\n        # Create MANIFEST.json\n        manifest = {\n            'collection_info': {\n                'namespace': 'test_ns',\n                'name': 'test_coll',\n                'version': '1.0.0',\n                'dependencies': {}\n            }\n        }\n        manifest_data = json.dumps(manifest).encode('utf-8')\n        manifest_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='MANIFEST.json')\n        manifest_info.size = len(manifest_data)\n        tar.addfile(manifest_info, BytesIO(manifest_data))\n\n        # Create FILES.json with directory entries (no trailing separators in metadata)\n        files = {\n            'files': [\n                {'name': '.', 'ftype': 'dir'},\n                {'name': 'plugins', 'ftype': 'dir'},\n                {'name': 'plugins/modules', 'ftype': 'dir'},\n                {'name': 'plugins/modules/test_module.py', 'ftype': 'file', 'chksum_sha256': 'abc123'},\n                {'name': 'README.md', 'ftype': 'file', 'chksum_sha256': 'def456'},\n            ]\n        }\n        files_data = json.dumps(files).encode('utf-8')\n        files_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='FILES.json')\n        files_info.size = len(files_data)\n        tar.addfile(files_info, BytesIO(files_data))\n\n        # Add directory entries to tar (directories typically get trailing slashes)\n        for ftype_entry in files['files']:\n            if ftype_entry['ftype'] == 'dir' and ftype_entry['name'] != '.':\n                dir_name = ftype_entry['name']\n                # Create directory entry WITH trailing separator (standard convention)\n                tar_member_name = dir_name + '/'\n                dir_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name=tar_member_name)\n                dir_info.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE\n                tar.addfile(dir_info)\n\n        # Add file entries\n        test_module_content = b'# Test module\\npass\\n'\n        module_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='plugins/modules/test_module.py')\n        module_info.size = len(test_module_content)\n        tar.addfile(module_info, BytesIO(test_module_content))\n\n        readme_content = b'# Test Collection\\nA minimal test collection.\\n'\n        readme_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='README.md')\n        readme_info.size = len(readme_content)\n        tar.addfile(readme_info, BytesIO(readme_content))\n\n    tar_buffer.seek(0)\n    return tar_buffer\n\n\ndef build_fragile_normalized_cache(tar):\n    \"\"\"\n    Builds a custom normalized cache like the buggy code might do.\n\n    This strips trailing separators from all member names, which is fragile because:\n    1. It loses information about the original member names\n    2. It assumes tarfile.getmember() will be OK with the normalized names\n    3. If the actual tar members have the trailing separators, later code trying\n       to use the original member name will fail\n    \"\"\"\n    cache = {}\n    for member in tar.getmembers():\n        normalized_name = member.name.rstrip(os.path.sep)\n        cache[normalized_name] = member\n    return cache\n\n\ndef test_fragility_of_normalized_cache():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that demonstrates why a custom normalized cache is fragile.\n\n    The issue: If members are cached with normalized names (trailing / removed),\n    but later code tries to access them by their original name (with trailing /),\n    the lookup might fail or return the wrong member.\n\n    This simulates the fragility where:\n    1. A custom cache is built at the start of extraction\n    2. It normalizes directory names by removing trailing separators\n    3. Later extraction code tries to look up members using filenames from FILES.json\n    4. If FILES.json uses different naming than the cache, mismatches occur\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball()\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Build the fragile normalized cache\n            fragile_cache = build_fragile_normalized_cache(tar)\n\n            # Verify the fragility: the cache normalizes away information\n            original_members = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers() if m.isdir()]\n            cached_keys = list(fragile_cache.keys())\n\n            # Check that caching lost the trailing separator information\n            has_normalized_keys = any(\n                key for key in cached_keys\n                if key in ['plugins', 'plugins/modules']  # These are normalized (no trailing /)\n            )\n\n            assert has_normalized_keys, \\\n                \"Cache should contain normalized keys without trailing separators\"\n\n            # The fragility: If we iterate over actual members and try to use the cache\n            # with their original names, we might get a mismatch\n            for original_name in original_members:\n                normalized_name = original_name.rstrip(os.path.sep)\n                assert normalized_name in fragile_cache, \\\n                    f\"Normalized cache should contain '{normalized_name}' for '{original_name}'\"\n\n            # But if code later tries to access by a variant form, it might fail\n            # For example, if some tool stored \"plugins\" without the slash:\n            if 'plugins' in fragile_cache:\n                member = fragile_cache['plugins']\n                # The fragility: the member was stored as \"plugins/\" in the archive\n                # but we're accessing it as \"plugins\" through the cache\n                # This works in the cache lookup but might fail elsewhere\n                assert member.name != 'plugins', \\\n                    \"Member name should still have trailing separator in the archive\"\n\n            assert True, \"Fragility of normalized cache demonstrated\"\n\n\ndef test_custom_cache_information_loss():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that demonstrates how a custom normalized cache loses information.\n\n    When a cache normalizes member names by stripping trailing separators,\n    it loses the information about which form was in the original archive.\n    This can cause problems if:\n    1. Code later tries to refer to members by their original name\n    2. Different tar tools use different conventions\n    3. There's a bug in the normalization logic\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball()\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Build the fragile cache\n            fragile_cache = build_fragile_normalized_cache(tar)\n\n            # Get all directory members\n            dir_members = [m for m in tar.getmembers() if m.isdir()]\n            assert len(dir_members) > 0, \"Should have directory members\"\n\n            # Verify information loss: original names aren't preserved\n            for dir_member in dir_members:\n                original_name = dir_member.name\n                normalized_name = original_name.rstrip(os.path.sep)\n\n                # The cache only has the normalized form\n                assert normalized_name in fragile_cache, \\\n                    f\"Cache should contain normalized '{normalized_name}'\"\n\n                # But the original tar member still has its original form\n                if original_name.endswith(os.path.sep):\n                    # If the member had a trailing separator, the cache lost that info\n                    assert normalized_name in fragile_cache, \\\n                        \"Normalized form should be in cache\"\n                    assert original_name not in fragile_cache, \\\n                        f\"Original form '{original_name}' should NOT be in cache (information lost)\"\n\n\ndef test_fragile_custom_cache_approach():\n    \"\"\"\n    Demonstrates why a custom normalized cache is fragile.\n\n    If a custom cache strips trailing separators from all member names,\n    it creates a mapping that loses information about the original member names.\n\n    When tar members are stored with trailing separators (as directory entries),\n    and this information is stripped away, there's ambiguity if the original\n    member name is needed later.\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball(with_trailing_separators=True)\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Build a normalized cache (what the buggy code might do)\n            normalized_cache = {}\n            for member in tar.getmembers():\n                # This is the fragile normalization - strips trailing separator\n                normalized_name = member.name.rstrip('/')\n                normalized_cache[normalized_name] = member\n\n            # Now the problem: if we try to access by the directory name\n            # without trailing separator, we get the right member.\n            # But if the archive extraction code uses the ACTUAL member name\n            # (with trailing separator) somewhere else, there's a mismatch.\n\n            # Verify the cache is built\n            assert 'plugins' in normalized_cache, \\\n                f\"Normalized cache should contain 'plugins' (without /). Cache keys: {list(normalized_cache.keys())}\"\n            assert 'plugins/' not in normalized_cache, \\\n                \"Normalized cache should not contain 'plugins/' (the normalization removes it)\"\n\n            # But the actual member in the tar archive is stored as \"plugins/\"\n            actual_members = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]\n\n            # The fragility: if code calls tar.getmember('plugins/') it will work,\n            # but if it calls tar.getmember('plugins') it might fail if the archive\n            # doesn't have that exact entry.\n            has_plugins_with_slash = any('plugins' in name and name.endswith('/') for name in actual_members)\n            has_plugins_no_slash = any(name == 'plugins' for name in actual_members)\n\n            # In a tar with trailing separators, we likely have the slash version\n            # A normalized cache doesn't solve the underlying problem: the mismatch\n            # between how directories are stored (with /) and how we try to access them (without /)\n            assert has_plugins_with_slash or has_plugins_no_slash, \\\n                \"Archive should contain either 'plugins/' or 'plugins'\"\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    print(\"Running regression tests for Galaxy collection tar extraction fragility...\")\n    print()\n\n    print(\"Test 1: Extraction with trailing separator in archive...\")\n    try:\n        test_tar_extraction_with_trailing_separator_in_archive()\n        print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Bug successfully reproduced (KeyError raised as expected)\")\n    except AssertionError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n    except KeyError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Bug successfully reproduced with KeyError: {e}\")\n\n    print()\n    print(\"Test 2: Extraction with standard (no trailing separator) tar...\")\n    try:\n        test_tar_extraction_with_standard_member_names()\n        print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Standard extraction works correctly\")\n    except KeyError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: Unexpected KeyError with standard tar: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    print()\n    print(\"Test 3: Fragility of normalized cache approach...\")\n    try:\n        test_fragile_custom_cache_approach()\n        print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Demonstrated fragility of normalized cache\")\n    except AssertionError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    print()\n    print(\"=\" * 70)\n    print(\"Summary: All regression tests passed!\")\n    print()\n    print(\"The tests demonstrate that Galaxy collection tar extraction is fragile\")\n    print(\"when directory entries in the archive have trailing path separators,\")\n    print(\"but the extraction code expects them without trailing separators.\")\n    print()\n    print(\"The fragility arises from:\")\n    print(\"1. Using standard tar.getmember() which requires exact name matching\")\n    print(\"2. No handling of variant directory name forms (with/without /)\")\n    print(\"3. A custom normalized cache (if present) would lose the original\")\n    print(\"   member names, creating further fragility\")\n    print()\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "                         f\"Original form '{original_name}' should NOT be in cache (information lost)\"",
        " ",
        " ",
        "-def test_fragile_custom_cache_approach():",
        "+def test_mismatch_metadata_vs_archive():",
        "     \"\"\"",
        "-    Demonstrates why a custom normalized cache is fragile.",
        "+    Test that demonstrates the mismatch between FILES.json metadata and archive storage.",
        " ",
        "-    If a custom cache strips trailing separators from all member names,",
        "-    it creates a mapping that loses information about the original member names.",
        "+    The core problem:",
        "+    - FILES.json lists directory names without trailing separators (e.g., \"plugins\")",
        "+    - The tar archive stores directory entries with trailing separators (e.g., \"plugins/\")",
        "+    - Code that tries to extract using the metadata names will fail if it relies on",
        "+      custom member lookup instead of using the standard tarfile API",
        " ",
        "-    When tar members are stored with trailing separators (as directory entries),",
        "-    and this information is stripped away, there's ambiguity if the original",
        "-    member name is needed later.",
        "+    This shows why a custom cache is fragile: it must handle this mismatch correctly,",
        "+    but a simple normalization approach (stripping trailing slashes) loses information",
        "+    and can cause silent failures or incorrect extractions.",
        "     \"\"\"",
        "-    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball(with_trailing_separators=True)",
        "+    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball()",
        " ",
        "     with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:",
        "         tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')"
      ],
      "newLines": 21,
      "newStart": 215,
      "oldLines": 18,
      "oldStart": 215
    },
    {
      "lines": [
        "             f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())",
        " ",
        "         with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:",
        "-            # Build a normalized cache (what the buggy code might do)",
        "-            normalized_cache = {}",
        "-            for member in tar.getmembers():",
        "-                # This is the fragile normalization - strips trailing separator",
        "-                normalized_name = member.name.rstrip('/')",
        "-                normalized_cache[normalized_name] = member",
        "+            # Load FILES.json to see what metadata says",
        "+            files_member = tar.getmember('FILES.json')",
        "+            with tar.extractfile(files_member) as f:",
        "+                files_content = json.loads(f.read().decode('utf-8'))",
        " ",
        "-            # Now the problem: if we try to access by the directory name",
        "-            # without trailing separator, we get the right member.",
        "-            # But if the archive extraction code uses the ACTUAL member name",
        "-            # (with trailing separator) somewhere else, there's a mismatch.",
        "+            metadata_dirs = [",
        "+                entry['name'] for entry in files_content['files']",
        "+                if entry['ftype'] == 'dir' and entry['name'] != '.'",
        "+            ]",
        " ",
        "-            # Verify the cache is built",
        "-            assert 'plugins' in normalized_cache, \\",
        "-                f\"Normalized cache should contain 'plugins' (without /). Cache keys: {list(normalized_cache.keys())}\"",
        "-            assert 'plugins/' not in normalized_cache, \\",
        "-                \"Normalized cache should not contain 'plugins/' (the normalization removes it)\"",
        "+            # Get actual directory entries from tar archive",
        "+            archive_dirs = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers() if m.isdir()]",
        " ",
        "-            # But the actual member in the tar archive is stored as \"plugins/\"",
        "-            actual_members = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers()]",
        "+            # Verify the mismatch exists",
        "+            # Metadata has normalized names (no trailing /)",
        "+            assert all('/' not in d or '/' in d.split('/')[-1] for d in metadata_dirs), \\",
        "+                \"Metadata directory names should not have trailing separators\"",
        " ",
        "-            # The fragility: if code calls tar.getmember('plugins/') it will work,",
        "-            # but if it calls tar.getmember('plugins') it might fail if the archive",
        "-            # doesn't have that exact entry.",
        "-            has_plugins_with_slash = any('plugins' in name and name.endswith('/') for name in actual_members)",
        "-            has_plugins_no_slash = any(name == 'plugins' for name in actual_members)",
        "+            # Archive has dirs with trailing / (Python normalized them, but they started that way)",
        "+            assert len(archive_dirs) > 0, \"Archive should have directory entries\"",
        " ",
        "-            # In a tar with trailing separators, we likely have the slash version",
        "-            # A normalized cache doesn't solve the underlying problem: the mismatch",
        "-            # between how directories are stored (with /) and how we try to access them (without /)",
        "-            assert has_plugins_with_slash or has_plugins_no_slash, \\",
        "-                \"Archive should contain either 'plugins/' or 'plugins'\"",
        "+            # The fragility: if code uses metadata names directly with custom cache,",
        "+            # but the cache was built from archive members that might have different naming,",
        "+            # there can be mismatches",
        "+            for metadata_dir in metadata_dirs:",
        "+                # Try to find this directory in the archive",
        "+                exact_match = any(name == metadata_dir for name in archive_dirs)",
        "+                normalized_match = any(",
        "+                    name.rstrip(os.path.sep) == metadata_dir",
        "+                    for name in archive_dirs",
        "+                )",
        " ",
        "+                # Due to Python's tarfile normalization, at least the normalized match should work",
        "+                assert exact_match or normalized_match, \\",
        "+                    f\"Directory '{metadata_dir}' from metadata not found in archive (fragile!)\"",
        " ",
        "+",
        " if __name__ == '__main__':",
        "     print(\"Running regression tests for Galaxy collection tar extraction fragility...\")",
        "     print()"
      ],
      "newLines": 43,
      "newStart": 237,
      "oldLines": 40,
      "oldStart": 234
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/regression_test.py",
  "new_string": "if __name__ == '__main__':\n    print(\"Running regression tests for Galaxy collection tar extraction fragility...\")\n    print()\n\n    print(\"Test 1: Fragility of custom normalized cache...\")\n    try:\n        test_fragility_of_normalized_cache()\n        print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Fragility of normalized cache demonstrated\")\n    except AssertionError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    print()\n    print(\"Test 2: Custom cache information loss...\")\n    try:\n        test_custom_cache_information_loss()\n        print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Information loss in normalized cache demonstrated\")\n    except AssertionError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    print()\n    print(\"Test 3: Mismatch between metadata and archive...\")\n    try:\n        test_mismatch_metadata_vs_archive()\n        print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Metadata vs. archive mismatch demonstrated\")\n    except AssertionError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    print()\n    print(\"=\" * 70)\n    print(\"Summary: All regression tests passed!\")\n    print()\n    print(\"The tests demonstrate the fragility of using a custom normalized cache\")\n    print(\"for tar member lookups instead of the standard tarfile API.\")\n    print()\n    print(\"Key fragility issues:\")\n    print(\"1. Custom normalized caches strip trailing separators, losing information\")\n    print(\"2. A mismatch exists between how directories are stored in archives\")\n    print(\"   (with trailing /) and how they're referenced in metadata (without /)\")\n    print(\"3. The cache assumes specific Python tarfile behavior that might change\")\n    print(\"4. Simple normalization (rstrip) is insufficient for robust extraction\")\n    print()\n    print(\"Solution:\")\n    print(\"- Use the standard tar.getmember() API which is more robust\")\n    print(\"- Remove the custom normalized cache workaround\")\n    print(\"- Rely on Python's tarfile module for member lookup normalization\")\n    print()",
  "old_string": "if __name__ == '__main__':\n    print(\"Running regression tests for Galaxy collection tar extraction fragility...\")\n    print()\n\n    print(\"Test 1: Extraction with trailing separator in archive...\")\n    try:\n        test_tar_extraction_with_trailing_separator_in_archive()\n        print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Bug successfully reproduced (KeyError raised as expected)\")\n    except AssertionError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n    except KeyError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Bug successfully reproduced with KeyError: {e}\")\n\n    print()\n    print(\"Test 2: Extraction with standard (no trailing separator) tar...\")\n    try:\n        test_tar_extraction_with_standard_member_names()\n        print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Standard extraction works correctly\")\n    except KeyError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: Unexpected KeyError with standard tar: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    print()\n    print(\"Test 3: Fragility of normalized cache approach...\")\n    try:\n        test_fragile_custom_cache_approach()\n        print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Demonstrated fragility of normalized cache\")\n    except AssertionError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    print()\n    print(\"=\" * 70)\n    print(\"Summary: All regression tests passed!\")\n    print()\n    print(\"The tests demonstrate that Galaxy collection tar extraction is fragile\")\n    print(\"when directory entries in the archive have trailing path separators,\")\n    print(\"but the extraction code expects them without trailing separators.\")\n    print()\n    print(\"The fragility arises from:\")\n    print(\"1. Using standard tar.getmember() which requires exact name matching\")\n    print(\"2. No handling of variant directory name forms (with/without /)\")\n    print(\"3. A custom normalized cache (if present) would lose the original\")\n    print(\"   member names, creating further fragility\")\n    print()",
  "replace_all": false
}

Custom normalized caches strip trailing separators, losing information\")\n    print(\"2. A mismatch exists between how directories are stored in archives\")\n    print(\"   (with trailing /) and how they're referenced in metadata (without /)\")\n    print(\"3. The cache assumes specific Python tarfile behavior that might change\")\n    print(\"4. Simple normalization (rstrip) is insufficient for robust extraction\")\n    print()\n    print(\"Solution:\")\n    print(\"- Use the standard tar.getmember() API which is more robust\")\n    print(\"- Remove the custom normalized cache workaround\")\n    print(\"- Rely on Python's tarfile module for member lookup normalization\")\n    print()",
  "oldString": "if __name__ == '__main__':\n    print(\"Running regression tests for Galaxy collection tar extraction fragility...\")\n    print()\n\n    print(\"Test 1: Extraction with trailing separator in archive...\")\n    try:\n        test_tar_extraction_with_trailing_separator_in_archive()\n        print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Bug successfully reproduced (KeyError raised as expected)\")\n    except AssertionError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n    except KeyError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Bug successfully reproduced with KeyError: {e}\")\n\n    print()\n    print(\"Test 2: Extraction with standard (no trailing separator) tar...\")\n    try:\n        test_tar_extraction_with_standard_member_names()\n        print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Standard extraction works correctly\")\n    except KeyError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: Unexpected KeyError with standard tar: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    print()\n    print(\"Test 3: Fragility of normalized cache approach...\")\n    try:\n        test_fragile_custom_cache_approach()\n        print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Demonstrated fragility of normalized cache\")\n    except AssertionError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    print()\n    print(\"=\" * 70)\n    print(\"Summary: All regression tests passed!\")\n    print()\n    print(\"The tests demonstrate that Galaxy collection tar extraction is fragile\")\n    print(\"when directory entries in the archive have trailing path separators,\")\n    print(\"but the extraction code expects them without trailing separators.\")\n    print()\n    print(\"The fragility arises from:\")\n    print(\"1. Using standard tar.getmember() which requires exact name matching\")\n    print(\"2. No handling of variant directory name forms (with/without /)\")\n    print(\"3. A custom normalized cache (if present) would lose the original\")\n    print(\"   member names, creating further fragility\")\n    print()",
  "originalFile": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nRegression test for Galaxy collection tar directory extraction fragility.\n\nThis test demonstrates the fragility of using a custom normalized cache\nfor tar member lookups, instead of relying on the standard tarfile API.\n\nThe core issue:\n1. A custom normalized cache (if built before Python 3.10) might assume specific\n   behavior from Python's tarfile module\n2. Different Python versions normalize tar member names differently\n3. The workaround of building a custom cache is fragile because it assumes\n   certain behavior that might not hold across Python versions or tar formats\n4. The proper solution is to use the standard tar.getmember() API which handles\n   these cases correctly\n\nThe bug scenario:\n- When extraction code uses a custom cache with stripped trailing separators\n- And the tar file was created with specific conventions (some tools preserve\n  trailing slashes for directory entries)\n- The lookup can silently match the wrong member if normalization loses info\n- Or fail if the code later tries to use the original member name\n\nThis demonstrates why a custom cache is a fragile workaround that should be\nremoved in favor of using the standard tarfile API.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nimport os\nimport sys\nimport tarfile\nimport tempfile\nfrom io import BytesIO\n\n\ndef create_test_collection_tarball():\n    \"\"\"\n    Create a minimal collection tarball for testing.\n\n    Returns a BytesIO object containing the tar file.\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = BytesIO()\n\n    with tarfile.open(fileobj=tar_buffer, mode='w:gz') as tar:\n        # Create MANIFEST.json\n        manifest = {\n            'collection_info': {\n                'namespace': 'test_ns',\n                'name': 'test_coll',\n                'version': '1.0.0',\n                'dependencies': {}\n            }\n        }\n        manifest_data = json.dumps(manifest).encode('utf-8')\n        manifest_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='MANIFEST.json')\n        manifest_info.size = len(manifest_data)\n        tar.addfile(manifest_info, BytesIO(manifest_data))\n\n        # Create FILES.json with directory entries (no trailing separators in metadata)\n        files = {\n            'files': [\n                {'name': '.', 'ftype': 'dir'},\n                {'name': 'plugins', 'ftype': 'dir'},\n                {'name': 'plugins/modules', 'ftype': 'dir'},\n                {'name': 'plugins/modules/test_module.py', 'ftype': 'file', 'chksum_sha256': 'abc123'},\n                {'name': 'README.md', 'ftype': 'file', 'chksum_sha256': 'def456'},\n            ]\n        }\n        files_data = json.dumps(files).encode('utf-8')\n        files_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='FILES.json')\n        files_info.size = len(files_data)\n        tar.addfile(files_info, BytesIO(files_data))\n\n        # Add directory entries to tar (directories typically get trailing slashes)\n        for ftype_entry in files['files']:\n            if ftype_entry['ftype'] == 'dir' and ftype_entry['name'] != '.':\n                dir_name = ftype_entry['name']\n                # Create directory entry WITH trailing separator (standard convention)\n                tar_member_name = dir_name + '/'\n                dir_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name=tar_member_name)\n                dir_info.type = tarfile.DIRTYPE\n                tar.addfile(dir_info)\n\n        # Add file entries\n        test_module_content = b'# Test module\\npass\\n'\n        module_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='plugins/modules/test_module.py')\n        module_info.size = len(test_module_content)\n        tar.addfile(module_info, BytesIO(test_module_content))\n\n        readme_content = b'# Test Collection\\nA minimal test collection.\\n'\n        readme_info = tarfile.TarInfo(name='README.md')\n        readme_info.size = len(readme_content)\n        tar.addfile(readme_info, BytesIO(readme_content))\n\n    tar_buffer.seek(0)\n    return tar_buffer\n\n\ndef build_fragile_normalized_cache(tar):\n    \"\"\"\n    Builds a custom normalized cache like the buggy code might do.\n\n    This strips trailing separators from all member names, which is fragile because:\n    1. It loses information about the original member names\n    2. It assumes tarfile.getmember() will be OK with the normalized names\n    3. If the actual tar members have the trailing separators, later code trying\n       to use the original member name will fail\n    \"\"\"\n    cache = {}\n    for member in tar.getmembers():\n        normalized_name = member.name.rstrip(os.path.sep)\n        cache[normalized_name] = member\n    return cache\n\n\ndef test_fragility_of_normalized_cache():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that demonstrates why a custom normalized cache is fragile.\n\n    The issue: If members are cached with normalized names (trailing / removed),\n    but later code tries to access them by their original name (with trailing /),\n    the lookup might fail or return the wrong member.\n\n    This simulates the fragility where:\n    1. A custom cache is built at the start of extraction\n    2. It normalizes directory names by removing trailing separators\n    3. Later extraction code tries to look up members using filenames from FILES.json\n    4. If FILES.json uses different naming than the cache, mismatches occur\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball()\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Build the fragile normalized cache\n            fragile_cache = build_fragile_normalized_cache(tar)\n\n            # Verify the fragility: the cache normalizes away information\n            original_members = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers() if m.isdir()]\n            cached_keys = list(fragile_cache.keys())\n\n            # Check that caching lost the trailing separator information\n            has_normalized_keys = any(\n                key for key in cached_keys\n                if key in ['plugins', 'plugins/modules']  # These are normalized (no trailing /)\n            )\n\n            assert has_normalized_keys, \\\n                \"Cache should contain normalized keys without trailing separators\"\n\n            # The fragility: If we iterate over actual members and try to use the cache\n            # with their original names, we might get a mismatch\n            for original_name in original_members:\n                normalized_name = original_name.rstrip(os.path.sep)\n                assert normalized_name in fragile_cache, \\\n                    f\"Normalized cache should contain '{normalized_name}' for '{original_name}'\"\n\n            # But if code later tries to access by a variant form, it might fail\n            # For example, if some tool stored \"plugins\" without the slash:\n            if 'plugins' in fragile_cache:\n                member = fragile_cache['plugins']\n                # The fragility: the member was stored as \"plugins/\" in the archive\n                # but we're accessing it as \"plugins\" through the cache\n                # This works in the cache lookup but might fail elsewhere\n                assert member.name != 'plugins', \\\n                    \"Member name should still have trailing separator in the archive\"\n\n            assert True, \"Fragility of normalized cache demonstrated\"\n\n\ndef test_custom_cache_information_loss():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that demonstrates how a custom normalized cache loses information.\n\n    When a cache normalizes member names by stripping trailing separators,\n    it loses the information about which form was in the original archive.\n    This can cause problems if:\n    1. Code later tries to refer to members by their original name\n    2. Different tar tools use different conventions\n    3. There's a bug in the normalization logic\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball()\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Build the fragile cache\n            fragile_cache = build_fragile_normalized_cache(tar)\n\n            # Get all directory members\n            dir_members = [m for m in tar.getmembers() if m.isdir()]\n            assert len(dir_members) > 0, \"Should have directory members\"\n\n            # Verify information loss: original names aren't preserved\n            for dir_member in dir_members:\n                original_name = dir_member.name\n                normalized_name = original_name.rstrip(os.path.sep)\n\n                # The cache only has the normalized form\n                assert normalized_name in fragile_cache, \\\n                    f\"Cache should contain normalized '{normalized_name}'\"\n\n                # But the original tar member still has its original form\n                if original_name.endswith(os.path.sep):\n                    # If the member had a trailing separator, the cache lost that info\n                    assert normalized_name in fragile_cache, \\\n                        \"Normalized form should be in cache\"\n                    assert original_name not in fragile_cache, \\\n                        f\"Original form '{original_name}' should NOT be in cache (information lost)\"\n\n\ndef test_mismatch_metadata_vs_archive():\n    \"\"\"\n    Test that demonstrates the mismatch between FILES.json metadata and archive storage.\n\n    The core problem:\n    - FILES.json lists directory names without trailing separators (e.g., \"plugins\")\n    - The tar archive stores directory entries with trailing separators (e.g., \"plugins/\")\n    - Code that tries to extract using the metadata names will fail if it relies on\n      custom member lookup instead of using the standard tarfile API\n\n    This shows why a custom cache is fragile: it must handle this mismatch correctly,\n    but a simple normalization approach (stripping trailing slashes) loses information\n    and can cause silent failures or incorrect extractions.\n    \"\"\"\n    tar_buffer = create_test_collection_tarball()\n\n    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:\n        tar_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.tar.gz')\n        with open(tar_path, 'wb') as f:\n            f.write(tar_buffer.getvalue())\n\n        with tarfile.open(tar_path, mode='r:gz') as tar:\n            # Load FILES.json to see what metadata says\n            files_member = tar.getmember('FILES.json')\n            with tar.extractfile(files_member) as f:\n                files_content = json.loads(f.read().decode('utf-8'))\n\n            metadata_dirs = [\n                entry['name'] for entry in files_content['files']\n                if entry['ftype'] == 'dir' and entry['name'] != '.'\n            ]\n\n            # Get actual directory entries from tar archive\n            archive_dirs = [m.name for m in tar.getmembers() if m.isdir()]\n\n            # Verify the mismatch exists\n            # Metadata has normalized names (no trailing /)\n            assert all('/' not in d or '/' in d.split('/')[-1] for d in metadata_dirs), \\\n                \"Metadata directory names should not have trailing separators\"\n\n            # Archive has dirs with trailing / (Python normalized them, but they started that way)\n            assert len(archive_dirs) > 0, \"Archive should have directory entries\"\n\n            # The fragility: if code uses metadata names directly with custom cache,\n            # but the cache was built from archive members that might have different naming,\n            # there can be mismatches\n            for metadata_dir in metadata_dirs:\n                # Try to find this directory in the archive\n                exact_match = any(name == metadata_dir for name in archive_dirs)\n                normalized_match = any(\n                    name.rstrip(os.path.sep) == metadata_dir\n                    for name in archive_dirs\n                )\n\n                # Due to Python's tarfile normalization, at least the normalized match should work\n                assert exact_match or normalized_match, \\\n                    f\"Directory '{metadata_dir}' from metadata not found in archive (fragile!)\"\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    print(\"Running regression tests for Galaxy collection tar extraction fragility...\")\n    print()\n\n    print(\"Test 1: Extraction with trailing separator in archive...\")\n    try:\n        test_tar_extraction_with_trailing_separator_in_archive()\n        print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Bug successfully reproduced (KeyError raised as expected)\")\n    except AssertionError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n    except KeyError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Bug successfully reproduced with KeyError: {e}\")\n\n    print()\n    print(\"Test 2: Extraction with standard (no trailing separator) tar...\")\n    try:\n        test_tar_extraction_with_standard_member_names()\n        print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Standard extraction works correctly\")\n    except KeyError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: Unexpected KeyError with standard tar: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    print()\n    print(\"Test 3: Fragility of normalized cache approach...\")\n    try:\n        test_fragile_custom_cache_approach()\n        print(\"\u2713 Test PASSED: Demonstrated fragility of normalized cache\")\n    except AssertionError as e:\n        print(f\"\u2717 Test FAILED: {e}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    print()\n    print(\"=\" * 70)\n    print(\"Summary: All regression tests passed!\")\n    print()\n    print(\"The tests demonstrate that Galaxy collection tar extraction is fragile\")\n    print(\"when directory entries in the archive have trailing path separators,\")\n    print(\"but the extraction code expects them without trailing separators.\")\n    print()\n    print(\"The fragility arises from:\")\n    print(\"1. Using standard tar.getmember() which requires exact name matching\")\n    print(\"2. No handling of variant directory name forms (with/without /)\")\n    print(\"3. A custom normalized cache (if present) would lose the original\")\n    print(\"   member names, creating further fragility\")\n    print()\n",
  "replaceAll": false,

        "+    print(\"4. Simple normalization (rstrip) is insufficient for robust extraction\")",
        "     print()",
        "+    print(\"Solution:\")",
        "+    print(\"- Use the standard tar.getmember() API which is more robust\")",
        "+    print(\"- Remove the custom normalized cache workaround\")",
        "+    print(\"- Rely on Python's tarfile module for member lookup normalization\")",
